name: Lisp
description: Lisp is a family of high-level programming languages with a long history and a unique syntax. It was originally created as a mathematical notation for computer programs and has since evolved into a powerful tool for artificial intelligence and symbolic computation.
connectionsExplanation: Lisp was one of the first programming languages to introduce the concept of symbolic computation and is considered a pioneer in the field of artificial intelligence. It directly influenced many other programming languages, including Scheme, Common Lisp, and Emacs Lisp.

moons.name: Scheme
moons.description: A dialect of Lisp, known for its simplicity and purity.

moons.name: Common Lisp
moons.description: A more modern and standardized version of Lisp, widely used in AI research.

moons.name: Emacs Lisp
moons.description: The dialect used for scripting the Emacs text editor.

curious_fact: Lisp was named after its inventor, John McCarthy, who wanted to create a language that could express mathematical concepts easily.

name: 1C:Enterprise Programming Language
description: 1C:Enterprise Programming Language is a high-level programming language developed by 1C Company for the 1C:Enterprise platform. It is primarily used for business software development, particularly in Russia and other former Soviet republics.
connectionsExplanation: 1C:Enterprise Programming Language evolved from the earlier 1C:Basic language, which was developed in the 1980s. It is designed to be user-friendly and integrates well with the 1C:Enterprise environment, making it highly suitable for business applications.

curious_fact: 1C:Enterprise Programming Language is known for its ability to handle complex business logic and large datasets efficiently.

name: 2.PAK
description: 2.PAK is an esoteric programming language created in 2000. It is inspired by the Befunge language, known for its two-dimensional code execution. 2.PAK introduces a unique twist by using a 2x2 grid of commands, making it a challenging and unconventional language to program in.
connectionsExplanation: 2.PAK is inspired by Befunge, an esoteric programming language that executes code on a two-dimensional grid. While not a direct descendant, 2.PAK shares the concept of non-linear code execution, which is a hallmark of Befunge. This inspiration led to the development of 2.PAK's unique 2x2 grid command structure, setting it apart from other esoteric languages.
curious_fact: 2.PAK was created as a tribute to the complexity and creativity of esoteric programming languages.

name: 2APL
description: 2APL is an array-oriented programming language developed by IBM. It is an extension of APL, designed to be more efficient and easier to use. 2APL is known for its concise syntax and powerful array manipulation capabilities.
connectionsExplanation: 2APL evolved from APL, which was developed in the 1960s. It was designed to address some of the limitations of APL, such as performance and ease of use, while maintaining its array-oriented paradigm.
curious_fact: 2APL was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'verbs' and 'nouns' in programming, which influenced later array-oriented languages.

name: 3APHP
description: 3APHP is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and structured programming concepts. It is inspired by PHP but introduces unique syntax and paradigms to enhance learning.
connectionsExplanation: 3APHP is a fictional language created to serve as an educational tool, drawing inspiration from PHP's syntax and functionality. It aims to simplify complex programming concepts for beginners while maintaining the core principles of web development.
curious_fact: 3APHP was developed to make web programming more accessible to students by simplifying PHP's syntax and introducing structured programming concepts.

name: 3APL
description: 3APL is a programming language developed by IBM in the 1970s. It is an extension of APL (A Programming Language) designed for business applications. 3APL introduced a number of enhancements aimed at improving the efficiency and usability of APL for business data processing.
connectionsExplanation: 3APL evolved from APL, which was developed in the 1960s. IBM created 3APL as an extension to address specific business needs, particularly in the area of data processing. It was designed to be more efficient and user-friendly for business applications compared to its predecessor.
curious_fact: 3APL was one of the first languages to introduce a graphical user interface for APL, making it more accessible to non-expert users.

name: 3DMLW
description: 3DMLW is a fictional programming language designed for creating 3D models and animations. It is part of a fictional universe where advanced technology and creativity intersect.
connectionsExplanation: 3DMLW was created as part of a fictional world where advanced programming languages are used for artistic and creative purposes. It is inspired by the need for a language that could easily manipulate 3D space and time, drawing parallels to the development of early 3D graphics languages.
curious_fact: 3DMLW was first introduced in a science fiction novel, where it was used to create lifelike animations for a virtual world.

name: 4DOS
description: 4DOS is a command-line interface (CLI) for DOS operating systems. It was developed by Quarterdeck Office Systems and is known for its advanced command set and user-friendly interface. 4DOS was widely used in the 1980s and 1990s, offering features like command history, command line editing, and batch file enhancements.
connectionsExplanation: 4DOS was developed as an enhancement to the standard DOS command line interface. It introduced many features that were not available in the original DOS, such as command history and command line editing, which made it more user-friendly and powerful. 4DOS was particularly popular among users who needed more control over their DOS environment.
curious_fact: 4DOS was one of the first command-line interfaces to support command history, which was a significant improvement over the standard DOS interface.

name: 4th Dimension
description: 4th Dimension is a relational database management system and integrated development environment (IDE) for Mac OS. It was developed by 4D, Inc. and is known for its visual database design and rapid application development capabilities.
connectionsExplanation: 4th Dimension was developed in the 1980s and is not directly related to any earlier programming languages. It is a specialized tool for database management and application development, focusing on ease of use and visual design.
curious_fact: 4th Dimension was one of the first systems to popularize the concept of a '4GL' (Fourth Generation Language) for database applications.

name: 68000 Assembly
description: 68000 Assembly is the assembly language for the Motorola 68000 microprocessor, widely used in personal computers and workstations in the 1980s. It is known for its powerful instruction set and high performance.
connectionsExplanation: The Motorola 68000 processor was a significant advancement in the 1980s, powering many popular computers like the Apple Macintosh and Commodore Amiga. Its assembly language, 68000 Assembly, directly evolved from earlier Motorola processors like the 6800 and 6809, maintaining a similar instruction set architecture.
curious_fact: The 68000 processor was one of the first to use a 32-bit data path with 16-bit addressing, allowing it to handle large memory spaces efficiently.

name: 8080 Assembly
description: 8080 Assembly is a low-level programming language used for programming the Intel 8080 microprocessor, which was widely used in the 1970s and 1980s. It is known for its simplicity and efficiency, making it a popular choice for embedded systems and early personal computers.
connectionsExplanation: 8080 Assembly is a direct descendant of the Intel 8008 microprocessor's assembly language. It was developed to improve upon the 8008 by adding more registers and instructions, making it more powerful and versatile. The 8080 processor was used in many early personal computers, including the Altair 8800, which helped popularize the microcomputer revolution.
curious_fact: The 8080 processor could execute up to 2 million instructions per second, which was quite fast for its time.

name: AManufacturingLanguage
description: AManufacturingLanguage is a specialized programming language designed for automating and optimizing manufacturing processes. It focuses on providing a high-level abstraction for controlling industrial machinery and streamlining production workflows.
connectionsExplanation: AManufacturingLanguage was developed as a proprietary tool by a major manufacturing company to enhance their internal automation capabilities. It draws inspiration from existing industrial control languages but introduces unique features tailored for modern manufacturing environments.
curious_fact: AManufacturingLanguage includes a built-in compiler that optimizes code for real-time execution in factory settings.

name: A#
description: A# is a programming language developed by Andrew W. Appel and others at Princeton University. It is a statically typed, functional programming language designed for teaching and research purposes. A# is based on Standard ML and is part of the ML family of languages.
connectionsExplanation: A# is a direct descendant of Standard ML, which itself is a descendant of ML. It was developed to provide a more accessible and educational version of ML for students and researchers. The language is closely related to other ML-based languages like OCaml and SML.
curious_fact: A# was designed to be a pedagogical tool, focusing on simplicity and clarity in teaching functional programming concepts.
example: let rec factorial n = if n = 0 then 1 else n * factorial (n - 1);;

name: A+
description: A+ is an array-oriented programming language developed by IBM in the 1970s. It was designed for scientific and engineering applications, particularly for matrix operations and numerical computations.
connectionsExplanation: A+ evolved from the A programming language, which was developed at IBM in the 1960s. It shares many features with A, including its array-oriented paradigm and focus on numerical computations.
curious_fact: A+ was one of the first languages to support complex numbers natively.

name: A++
description: A++ is a modern, high-level programming language designed for simplicity and ease of use. It aims to provide a straightforward syntax that allows developers to write efficient and readable code quickly.
connectionsExplanation: A++ was inspired by the C++ language, inheriting its object-oriented features while simplifying the syntax and adding modern conveniences. It is intended to be a more accessible alternative for beginners and experienced developers alike.
curious_fact: A++ introduced a unique feature called 'auto-declaration', which automatically infers variable types based on their initial values.

name: ABAP
description: ABAP (Advanced Business Application Programming) is a high-level programming language developed by SAP SE. It is primarily used for building applications within the SAP environment, such as customizing SAP systems, developing reports, and creating user interfaces.
connectionsExplanation: ABAP is a direct descendant of the original SAP programming language, which was developed in the 1970s. It evolved to meet the growing needs of business applications and became the standard language for SAP development.

name: ABC
description: ABC is an interpreted programming language designed at Bell Labs in the late 1980s. It was intended to be a simple, easy-to-learn language for teaching programming concepts. ABC is known for its minimal syntax and dynamic typing.
connectionsExplanation: ABC was developed as a teaching language at Bell Labs and was influenced by Smalltalk and Lisp. It aimed to simplify programming concepts for beginners and was part of the broader effort to make programming more accessible.
curious_fact: ABC was one of the first languages to use indentation to denote blocks of code, a feature later adopted by Python.

name: ABCALGOL
description: ABCALGOL is a variant of ALGOL 60 developed by the Burroughs Corporation. It was designed to be more efficient and easier to compile than its predecessor.

curious_fact: ABCALGOL introduced several innovations, including a more efficient syntax and better support for structured programming constructs.

name: ABLE
description: ABLE (A Binary List Encoder) was an early programming language developed at IBM. It was designed to simplify the programming of IBM 1401 computers by using a binary list encoding system. ABLE was one of the first high-level programming languages and played a role in the evolution of COBOL.
connectionsExplanation: ABLE was developed at IBM and influenced the creation of COBOL. It was one of the early high-level programming languages that aimed to make programming more accessible by abstracting machine-level details. The development of ABLE contributed to the broader trend of creating more user-friendly programming languages in the 1950s.
curious_fact: ABLE was one of the first languages to use a binary list encoding system, which was a precursor to more modern high-level language features.

name: ABSET
description: ABSET is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It emphasizes simplicity and clarity, making it an ideal language for beginners to understand basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: ABSET was created as a teaching tool in the early 1970s, inspired by the need for a language that could be easily understood by students. It is not related to any existing programming language but draws from the principles of structured programming.
curious_fact: ABSET was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'no-nonsense' programming, focusing on minimal syntax and straightforward logic.

name: ABSYS
description: ABSYS is a programming language designed for the development of artificial intelligence systems. It was developed by the Artificial Intelligence Laboratory at MIT in the early 1970s. ABSYS is known for its unique approach to AI programming, focusing on symbolic computation and knowledge representation.
connectionsExplanation: ABSYS was developed as part of the broader AI research efforts at MIT in the 1970s. It was influenced by earlier symbolic programming languages and was used in various AI projects, contributing to the field's development during that era.
curious_fact: ABSYS was one of the early languages to incorporate symbolic computation, which is now a cornerstone of modern AI systems.

name: ACL2
description: ACL2 (A Computational Logic for Applicative Common Lisp) is a programming language and a theorem prover based on Common Lisp. It is used for formal verification of software and hardware systems.
connectionsExplanation: ACL2 evolved from NQTHM (A Mechanized Logic for Hardware Description Languages) and is a direct descendant of Common Lisp, inheriting its syntax and semantics.
curious_fact: ACL2 is one of the few programming languages designed specifically for formal verification, making it invaluable in critical systems development.

name: ACUCOBOL
description: ACUCOBOL is a commercial version of COBOL developed by Micro Focus. It is known for its ease of use and compatibility with standard COBOL programs. ACUCOBOL is widely used in business, finance, and administrative systems.
connectionsExplanation: ACUCOBOL is a commercial variant of COBOL, which was one of the first high-level programming languages. COBOL was developed in the late 1950s and early 1960s, making it a direct descendant of the pioneering languages like Fortran and COBOL.
curious_fact: ACUCOBOL is one of the few COBOL implementations that runs on modern operating systems, including Windows and Linux.
example: IDENTIFICATION DIVISION. PROGRAM-ID. HelloWorld. PROCEDURE DIVISION. DISPLAY 'Hello, World!'. STOP RUN.

name: ADMB
description: ADMB (Automatic Differentiation Model Builder) is a high-level language and software framework for non-linear statistical modeling. It is particularly suited for complex ecological and biological models.
connectionsExplanation: ADMB is derived from the ADM software, which was developed in the 1980s. ADMB builds upon the principles of automatic differentiation, a technique for computing derivatives of functions specified by computer programs. This makes ADMB highly efficient for optimization problems in statistical modeling.
curious_fact: ADMB is known for its ability to handle large datasets and complex models, making it a popular choice in fields like fisheries science and ecology.

name: AIMACO
description: AIMACO is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by the principles of artificial intelligence and machine learning, making it an ideal language for beginners interested in these fields.

name: AIMMS
description: AIMMS (Advanced Interactive Modeler and Solver System) is a modeling system for mathematical programming problems. It is widely used in industries for optimization and decision-making processes.
connectionsExplanation: AIMMS was developed by Paragon Decision Technologies, which was later acquired by GAMS Software. It is built on top of the GAMS system and extends its capabilities with a more user-friendly interface and advanced modeling features.
curious_fact: AIMMS supports a wide range of solvers, including both commercial and open-source options.
example: Model MyModel; Parameter p; p := 10; EndModel;

name: AJAX
description: AJAX (Asynchronous JavaScript and XML) is a web development technique that allows web pages to be updated asynchronously by exchanging small amounts of data with the server behind the scenes. This enables a more interactive and responsive user experience without requiring a full page reload.
connectionsExplanation: AJAX was developed by Microsoft in the early 2000s and popularized by Google. It revolutionized web development by enabling dynamic content updates, which was a significant advancement over traditional page reloads.
curious_fact: AJAX stands for Asynchronous JavaScript and XML, but XML is not always used in modern AJAX implementations.
example: var xhr = new XMLHttpRequest(); xhr.open('GET', 'https://api.example.com/data', true); xhr.onreadystatechange = function() { if (xhr.readyState === 4 && xhr.status === 200) { console.log(xhr.responseText); } }; xhr.send();

name: AL
description: AL, short for Algorithmic Language, was an early programming language developed in the 1960s. It was designed to be a high-level language that could be used for both scientific and business applications. AL aimed to simplify programming by providing a more intuitive syntax compared to machine code.
connectionsExplanation: AL was one of the early high-level programming languages developed in the 1960s. It was influenced by the need to make programming more accessible to non-specialists. AL was part of the broader trend towards creating languages that could be used for a variety of applications, from scientific computing to business data processing.
curious_fact: AL was one of the first languages to introduce structured programming concepts, which helped in organizing code more effectively.

name: ALCOR

It was designed to be a high-level language that could be easily translated into machine code. Despite its early development, ALCOR did not gain widespread adoption and is now considered a historical curiosity.
connectionsExplanation: ALCOR was developed during a time when many new programming languages were emerging. It was influenced by the need for more efficient and user-friendly languages for scientific computations. While it did not become as popular as other languages of its era, ALCOR played a role in the evolution of programming languages by exploring new paradigms and syntax structures.
curious_fact: ALCOR was one of the first languages to introduce structured programming concepts, which later became a standard in many modern languages.

name: ALGAE
description: ALGAE is a fictional programming language designed to simulate the growth and behavior of algae in a computational environment. It is not intended for practical use but serves as an interesting exploration of biological algorithms and computational models.
connectionsExplanation: ALGAE was created as a thought experiment to explore the intersection of biology and computation. While it is not based on any existing language, it draws inspiration from the structured and procedural paradigms found in languages like ALGOL and COBOL, which were pioneers in the 1950s.
curious_fact: ALGAE uses a unique syntax that mimics the growth patterns of algae, with commands like 'photosynthesize' and 'reproduce'.

name: ALGO
description: ALGO is a hypothetical programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on algorithmic thinking and simplicity. It was never officially released but is often used in computer science curricula to teach basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: ALGO is inspired by early programming languages like ALGOL and BASIC, aiming to provide a straightforward syntax for beginners. Its design reflects the simplicity and clarity of these pioneers, making it a useful tool for teaching fundamental programming principles.
curious_fact: ALGO was conceptualized to be the perfect language for beginners, with a syntax that mirrors natural language.

name: ALGOL
description: ALGOL, short for ALGOrithmic Language, is a family of high-level programming languages originally developed in the mid-1950s. It was one of the first programming languages to introduce structured programming concepts such as blocks, nested functions, and recursion. ALGOL aimed to be a portable and efficient language for algorithmic programming.
connectionsExplanation: ALGOL was developed in the 1950s at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) and the Numerical Control Company (NCC). It was influenced by earlier languages like FORTRAN and aimed to improve upon them by introducing a more structured and readable syntax. ALGOL 60, in particular, had a significant impact on the development of subsequent programming languages, including Pascal and C.
curious_fact: ALGOL 60 was the first language to use the keyword 'else'.

name: ALGOL58
description: ALGOL58, short for Algorithmic Language 1958, was one of the first high-level programming languages. It was designed to be more readable and less error-prone than assembly languages. ALGOL58 introduced several concepts that influenced later languages, such as block structure and nested functions.
connectionsExplanation: ALGOL58 was a pioneering language developed in the late 1950s. It was one of the first to introduce structured programming concepts, which significantly influenced the design of subsequent languages like Pascal and C. Its influence can be seen in the syntax and structure of modern programming languages.
curious_fact: ALGOL58 was one of the first languages to use the keyword 'begin' to denote the start of a block of code.

name: ALGOL60
description: ALGOL60, short for Algorithmic Language 1960, is a high-level programming language developed in the late 1950s and early 1960s. It was one of the first programming languages to introduce structured programming concepts such as blocks, loops, and conditional statements. ALGOL60 aimed to improve the clarity and portability of code, influencing many subsequent programming languages.
connectionsExplanation: ALGOL60 was developed as part of the NATO programming language project, which aimed to create a common language for military and scientific applications. It was one of the pioneering languages that influenced the development of modern programming languages, particularly in the areas of structured programming and syntax design.

moons.name: ALGOL-W

curious_fact: ALGOL60 was one of the first languages to use the keyword 'begin' to start a block of code and 'end' to terminate it, a convention that has been widely adopted in many modern programming languages.

name: ALGOL68
description: ALGOL68 is a high-level programming language developed in the late 1960s as part of the ALGOL X project. It was designed to be a successor to ALGOL60, aiming to improve its syntax and capabilities. Despite its ambitious goals, ALGOL68 never gained widespread adoption due to its complex syntax and the emergence of more practical languages like C and Pascal.
connectionsExplanation: ALGOL68 was developed as part of the ALGOL X project, which aimed to create a more powerful and flexible version of ALGOL60. Its design was influenced by the need to address the limitations of its predecessor while incorporating advanced features like strong typing and structured programming. However, the language's complexity and the rise of more user-friendly alternatives led to its limited adoption in the programming community.

moons.name: ALGOL68R

curious_fact: ALGOL68 was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of strong typing, which is now a standard feature in many modern programming languages.

name: ALGOLW
description: ALGOLW is a variant of the ALGOL programming language developed in the 1960s. It was designed to improve upon the original ALGOL by incorporating more advanced features and addressing some of its limitations. ALGOLW is considered a specialized dialect and is not widely used today.

curious_fact: ALGOLW introduced the concept of 'structured programming' which emphasized the use of blocks and control structures to improve code readability and maintainability.

name: ALTRAN
description: ALTRAN (Advanced Language Translator) was one of the first high-level programming languages designed for scientific and engineering applications. It was developed at MIT and was used for tasks such as matrix manipulation and numerical analysis.
connectionsExplanation: ALTRAN was developed at MIT in the late 1950s and was one of the early high-level programming languages designed for scientific and engineering applications. It was influenced by the need for more efficient and readable code compared to assembly languages.
curious_fact: ALTRAN was one of the first languages to support complex arithmetic operations directly in the language syntax.

name: AMBIT

While it did not gain widespread adoption, it influenced the development of other educational programming languages.

name: AMOS
description: AMOS is a programming language and integrated development environment (IDE) designed for the Commodore 64 computer. It was developed by Martin Klang and was first released in 1987. AMOS is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among beginners and hobbyists.
connectionsExplanation: AMOS was developed as a successor to the AMOS Basic language, which was itself inspired by the BASIC programming language. It was designed to be more accessible and user-friendly, catering to the Commodore 64's graphical capabilities.
curious_fact: AMOS includes a built-in sprite editor, allowing users to create and animate graphics directly within the IDE.

name: AMPL
description: AMPL is a high-level modeling language for mathematical programming problems. It is widely used in the fields of operations research, engineering, and economics for optimization modeling.
connectionsExplanation: AMPL was developed by Bell Laboratories and is based on earlier modeling languages like SETL and GAMS. It provides a concise and expressive way to formulate optimization problems, making it a popular choice for researchers and practitioners.
curious_fact: AMPL supports a wide range of solvers, allowing users to choose the most appropriate one for their specific problem.

name: AMPLE

It was influenced by the need to handle complex optimization problems efficiently, reflecting the growing importance of operations research and mathematical programming in industry and academia.

example: VAR X, Y, Z; BEGIN X := 10; Y := 20; Z := X + Y; PRINT Z; END.

name: ANSYS Parametric Design Language

APDL is closely tied to ANSYS's capabilities, enabling users to leverage the software's powerful simulation tools through scripting.
curious_fact: APDL is one of the oldest scripting languages specifically designed for engineering simulation, dating back to the early 1980s.

name: APL
description: APL (A Programming Language) is a high-level, array-oriented programming language developed in the 1960s by Kenneth E. Iverson. It is known for its concise syntax and mathematical notation, making it particularly suited for scientific and engineering applications. Despite its age, APL remains influential in the field of computer science, especially in the development of other array-oriented languages.
connectionsExplanation: APL was developed as part of a research project at Harvard University and was influenced by Iverson's work on mathematical notation. It is considered a pioneer in the field of array-oriented programming, which has influenced the development of languages like MATLAB and J.

name: APMonitor
description: APMonitor is an optimization software for differential and algebraic equations. It is used in chemical engineering, biotechnology, and other process industries.
connectionsExplanation: APMonitor is a specialized tool for optimization problems and is not directly derived from any mainstream programming language. It is built on top of Python and uses its syntax for scripting.
curious_fact: APMonitor can solve large-scale optimization problems in real-time.
example:
from apmonitor import apm
s = apm('http://byu.apmonitor.com', 'p1')
apm(s, 'clear all')
apm(s, 'pysp', 'model.apm')
apm(s, 'pysp', 'data.csv')
apm(s, 'solve')
results = apm(s, 'results')
print(results)

name: AQASM
description: AQASM is a quantum assembly language designed for programming quantum computers. It provides a low-level interface for specifying quantum operations and is used in the development of quantum algorithms and applications.
connectionsExplanation: AQASM is inspired by classical assembly languages but is tailored for quantum computing. It is designed to be a foundational language for quantum programming, allowing developers to directly interact with quantum hardware.
curious_fact: AQASM is part of the PennyLane quantum machine learning library, which aims to simplify the development of quantum algorithms.

name: ARLA
description: ARLA is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid application development. It emphasizes simplicity and ease of use, making it accessible to beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: ARLA was developed as a successor to the popular scripting language 'ScriptX'. It incorporates several innovative features from 'ScriptX' while also drawing inspiration from modern paradigms such as functional programming and reactive programming.

moons.name: ARLA Framework
moons.description: A comprehensive framework for building web applications with ARLA.

curious_fact: ARLA was created by a team of developers who previously worked on the 'ScriptX' project.

name: ARM Assembly
description: ARM Assembly is a low-level programming language used for writing code that runs directly on ARM-based processors. It is widely used in embedded systems, mobile devices, and other resource-constrained environments.
connectionsExplanation: ARM Assembly evolved from the ARM architecture, which was developed by Acorn Computers in the 1980s. It is a direct descendant of the ARM instruction set architecture, allowing developers to write highly optimized code for ARM processors.
curious_fact: ARM Assembly is used in many popular devices, including smartphones and tablets, due to its efficiency and power-saving capabilities.

name: ARM NEON
description: ARM NEON is a single instruction, multiple data (SIMD) extension to the ARM architecture, designed to accelerate multimedia and signal processing applications. It provides a set of 128-bit wide registers and a rich instruction set for parallel processing.
connectionsExplanation: ARM NEON was introduced as part of the ARM Cortex-A8 processor in 2005. It extends the ARM architecture to support SIMD operations, which are crucial for multimedia and high-performance computing tasks. NEON instructions are designed to work seamlessly with the existing ARM instruction set, allowing developers to leverage parallel processing capabilities without significant changes to their codebase.
curious_fact: ARM NEON instructions can perform up to 16 operations in a single cycle, significantly speeding up multimedia processing tasks.
example: VADD.F32 q0, q0, q1 // Vector addition of single-precision floating-point numbers

name: ART
description: ART (A Real-Time Compiler) was an early programming language developed in the 1960s for real-time computing applications. It was designed to compile directly to machine code, aiming for high performance in systems where speed was critical.
connectionsExplanation: ART was developed at MIT in the early 1960s as part of the efforts to create efficient compilers for real-time systems. It was influenced by the need for high-performance computing in areas such as aerospace and defense, where quick execution was paramount.
curious_fact: ART was one of the first languages to focus on real-time compilation, setting a precedent for future high-performance languages.

name: ARexx
description: ARexx is an extension of the REXX programming language designed for use on the Commodore Amiga operating system. It was developed by Commodore Business Machines to provide a powerful scripting and automation tool for Amiga users.
connectionsExplanation: ARexx evolved from REXX, which was developed by IBM. It was specifically tailored to integrate with the AmigaOS environment, offering enhanced capabilities for system scripting and automation on Amiga computers.
curious_fact: ARexx was one of the first scripting languages to be integrated into a commercial operating system.

name: ASCEND
description: ASCEND is a high-level modeling language for solving systems of non-linear equations, differential equations, and optimization problems. It is particularly suited for chemical engineering and process modeling.
connectionsExplanation: ASCEND was developed at the University of Melbourne in Australia. It is based on the paradigm of declarative programming, allowing users to define models in a way that closely mirrors the mathematical formulation of the problem. ASCEND's design is rooted in the need for a flexible and powerful tool for engineering simulations, making it a specialized language within the scientific computing domain.

moons.name: ASCEND Solver
moons.description: A solver component for ASCEND, used to find solutions to the defined models.

moons.name: ASCEND Compiler
moons.description: A compiler that translates ASCEND models into executable code.

curious_fact: ASCEND was one of the first languages to support automatic differentiation, which simplifies the process of solving complex mathematical models.

name: ASIC
description: ASIC is a domain-specific programming language designed for the specification and synthesis of Application-Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs). It allows engineers to describe the functionality of a circuit in a high-level language, which is then translated into a hardware design.
connectionsExplanation: ASIC evolved from the need to streamline the design process for custom integrated circuits. It builds upon the principles of hardware description languages (HDLs) like VHDL and Verilog, which were developed in the 1980s to facilitate the design of complex digital systems.
curious_fact: ASIC was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of synthesis from high-level descriptions, significantly reducing the time and effort required for hardware design.
example: module adder (input [3:0] a, input [3:0] b, output [4:0] sum);\n  assign sum = a + b;\nendmodule

name: ASSIRIS
description: ASSIRIS is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: ASSIRIS was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical lineage. It is inspired by modern programming languages but is designed to be more accessible for beginners.
curious_fact: ASSIRIS uses a unique syntax that resembles natural language, making it easier for beginners to understand.

name: ASSK
description: ASSK is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and minimalism. It is not intended for practical use but serves as a tool to teach fundamental programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: ASSK was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical lineage. It is inspired by the simplicity of early programming languages like BASIC and the minimalistic approach of esoteric languages.
curious_fact: ASSK was developed in a weekend hackathon by a group of computer science students.

name: ATLASTransformationLanguage
description: ATLASTransformationLanguage is a specialized programming language designed for transforming data structures. It is particularly useful in environments where complex data manipulation is required.
connectionsExplanation: ATLASTransformationLanguage was developed as an extension of the ALGOL family, specifically targeting data transformation tasks. It inherits the structured programming paradigm from ALGOL, which emphasizes clear and concise syntax.
curious_fact: ATLASTransformationLanguage was one of the first languages to introduce a declarative approach to data transformation, setting a precedent for modern data processing tools.
example:
BEGIN
  TRANSFORM data
  OUTPUT result
END

name: ATOLL
description: ATOLL is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by the concept of an atoll, a ring-shaped coral reef, reflecting its modular and interconnected nature.
connectionsExplanation: ATOLL is a fictional language created for educational purposes, designed to be simple and easy to learn. It draws inspiration from the concept of an atoll, a ring-shaped coral reef, which symbolizes its modular and interconnected structure. While it is not based on any existing language, it shares some design principles with modern educational languages like Scratch, which aim to introduce programming concepts in a straightforward manner.
curious_fact: ATOLL was created to demonstrate the beauty of simple, modular programming concepts.

name: ATS
description: ATS (Applied Type System) is a programming language that combines the power of functional programming with the efficiency of systems programming. It is designed to provide strong typing and performance guarantees, making it suitable for developing high-performance applications.
connectionsExplanation: ATS is a descendant of SML (Standard ML) and is influenced by Haskell. It aims to bridge the gap between high-level functional programming and low-level systems programming, offering a statically typed environment with advanced type systems.

moons.name: Pats
moons.description: Pats is a preprocessor for ATS, designed to simplify the syntax and make ATS more accessible to programmers.

curious_fact: ATS is known for its advanced type system, which allows for the specification of complex invariants and properties of programs.

name: AbbreviatedLanguageForAuthorization
description: A fictional programming language designed for authorization tasks, focusing on concise syntax and rapid processing. It is primarily used in internal systems where security and speed are critical.
connectionsExplanation: AbbreviatedLanguageForAuthorization was created as a specialized language for handling authorization tasks in high-security environments. It draws inspiration from modern programming paradigms but is tailored for specific use cases, making it distinct from general-purpose languages.
curious_fact: The language was developed in a single day by a team of security experts to address a critical need for fast and secure authorization processing.

name: AbbreviatedTestLanguageforAllSystems
description: A fictional programming language designed for testing and educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and brevity. It is not intended for production use.
connectionsExplanation: AbbreviatedTestLanguageforAllSystems is a hypothetical language created for the purpose of this exercise. It does not have a historical connection to any existing language.
curious_fact: The language was created to demonstrate the minimal requirements for a functional programming language.

name: Abstract State Machine Language
description: Abstract State Machine Language (ASML) is a formal language used for specifying and verifying the behavior of systems. It is particularly useful in the field of formal methods for software and hardware verification.
connectionsExplanation: ASML is inspired by the concept of abstract state machines, which were introduced by Yuri Gurevich in the 1990s. It provides a rigorous framework for defining system behavior, making it a valuable tool in formal verification processes.
curious_fact: ASML is used in the verification of critical systems, ensuring they behave as intended under all possible conditions.
example: state S1 { action { print("Hello World"); } transition to S2; } state S2 { action { print("Goodbye World"); } }

name: Accent
description: Accent is a programming language developed by IBM in the 1970s. It was designed for the development of business applications and was part of IBM's System R project, which aimed to create a relational database management system. Accent was a procedural language with a syntax influenced by COBOL and PL/I.
connectionsExplanation: Accent was developed as part of IBM's System R project, which aimed to create a relational database management system. It was influenced by COBOL and PL/I, reflecting the business-oriented nature of its intended use. The language was designed to be used in conjunction with the System R database, which was a precursor to modern relational databases.
curious_fact: Accent was one of the first languages to incorporate concepts from relational databases directly into its syntax.

name: AccentR
description: AccentR is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid application development, with a focus on simplicity and ease of use. It is particularly suited for web and mobile applications, offering a clean syntax and powerful libraries.
connectionsExplanation: AccentR was developed as a successor to JavaScript, aiming to improve upon its syntax and performance. It draws inspiration from modern JavaScript frameworks and syntax improvements, making it a direct descendant of JavaScript.

moons.name: AccentR-React
moons.description: A library for building user interfaces in AccentR, inspired by React.

moons.name: AccentR-Express
moons.description: A web application framework for AccentR, inspired by Express.js.

curious_fact: AccentR was created by a team of developers who wanted to streamline the process of building modern web applications.

name: Acorn System BASIC
description: Acorn System BASIC was a dialect of the BASIC programming language developed by Acorn Computers for their early microcomputers. It was designed to be easy to use and was widely used in the UK during the 1980s.
connectionsExplanation: Acorn System BASIC evolved from the original Dartmouth BASIC, which was one of the first high-level programming languages. It was specifically tailored for Acorn's hardware and operating systems, making it a crucial language for the development of early Acorn computers like the BBC Micro.
curious_fact: Acorn System BASIC was one of the first languages used to teach programming in UK schools during the 1980s.

name: Action!
description: Action! is a high-level programming language designed for the Atari 8-bit computers. It was created by Tom Pittman and published by Synapse Software. Action! is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among beginners and hobbyists.
connectionsExplanation: Action! was developed specifically for the Atari 8-bit computers, which were popular in the early 1980s. It was designed to be accessible and efficient for these systems, allowing users to write programs quickly and effectively.
curious_fact: Action! was one of the first high-level languages to be specifically designed for the Atari 8-bit computers.

name: ActionScript
description: ActionScript is a scripting language primarily used for developing interactive content on the Adobe Flash platform. It is a dialect of ECMAScript and is closely related to JavaScript.
connectionsExplanation: ActionScript evolved from JavaScript and was specifically designed to work with Adobe Flash. It shares many similarities with JavaScript but includes additional features tailored for Flash development, such as support for Adobe Flash Player APIs.

moons.name: ActionScript 3
moons.description: ActionScript 3 is the third major version of ActionScript, introduced in 2006. It is a significant upgrade over previous versions, offering better performance, more robust features, and improved security.

curious_fact: ActionScript was originally developed by Macromedia before Adobe acquired the company in 2005.

name: Active Oberon
description: Active Oberon is an extension of the Oberon programming language designed to support object-oriented programming and active objects. It was developed by Niklaus Wirth and his team at ETH Zurich.
connectionsExplanation: Active Oberon evolved from Oberon, which was developed by Niklaus Wirth in the 1980s. Oberon is a direct descendant of Modula-2, which itself evolved from Pascal. Therefore, the lineage can be traced back to Pascal through Modula-2 and Oberon.
curious_fact: Active Oberon introduced the concept of active objects, which are objects that can initiate actions independently of external stimuli.
example: MODULE HelloWorld; IMPORT Out; PROCEDURE Main; BEGIN Out.String("Hello, World!"); Out.Ln END Main; END HelloWorld.

name: Active Server Pages
description: Active Server Pages (ASP) is a server-side scripting technology developed by Microsoft for building dynamic web pages. It uses VBScript or JScript to create interactive web content.
connectionsExplanation: ASP was developed by Microsoft as a successor to ASP Classic, which was introduced in 1996. It is built on top of the Internet Information Services (IIS) and is tightly integrated with Microsoft's .NET framework, allowing developers to create dynamic web applications using a variety of programming languages.

moons.name: ASP.NET
moons.description: ASP.NET is the successor to ASP, providing a more robust framework for building web applications.

curious_fact: ASP was one of the first technologies to popularize server-side scripting on the web.

name: ActiveBasic
description: ActiveBasic is a rapid application development (RAD) tool for Windows programming. It is based on the BASIC programming language and is designed to be easy to learn and use, making it popular among beginners and hobbyists.
connectionsExplanation: ActiveBasic evolved from earlier BASIC dialects and was influenced by the need for a more modern and user-friendly environment for Windows development. It builds upon the simplicity and accessibility of BASIC while incorporating features specific to Windows programming.
curious_fact: ActiveBasic was one of the first tools to offer a visual interface for Windows programming, making it accessible to those without deep programming knowledge.

name: ActiveVFP
description: ActiveVFP is a database management system and development environment for creating database-driven applications. It is based on Visual FoxPro and provides a set of tools for building, deploying, and managing applications.
connectionsExplanation: ActiveVFP is a direct descendant of Visual FoxPro, which was developed by Microsoft. It extends the capabilities of Visual FoxPro by adding support for web development and integration with other Microsoft technologies.
curious_fact: ActiveVFP was one of the first systems to integrate Visual FoxPro with web technologies, allowing developers to create web-based applications using familiar tools.

name: Actor
description: Actor is a domain-specific programming language designed for creating and managing actors in distributed systems. It is particularly suited for building scalable and fault-tolerant applications.
connectionsExplanation: Actor is inspired by the Actor Model, a theoretical model of concurrent computation where actors are the fundamental units of computation. It draws parallels with languages like Erlang and Akka, which also implement the Actor Model to manage concurrency and distribution in applications.
curious_fact: Actor was developed as part of the Actor Framework project, aiming to simplify the development of distributed systems.
example: actor Main { on start { println("Hello, World!"); } }

name: Actor-Based Concurrent Language
description: Actor-Based Concurrent Language (ABCL) is a programming language designed for building concurrent and distributed systems. It emphasizes the actor model, where computation is organized around actors that communicate by exchanging messages. ABCL is particularly suited for applications requiring high concurrency and fault tolerance.
connectionsExplanation: Actor-Based Concurrent Language (ABCL) was developed as an extension of the Erlang programming language, which is widely known for its robust concurrency model. ABCL builds upon Erlang's actor model by introducing additional features and optimizations to enhance performance and scalability in concurrent applications.

moons.name: ActorLib
moons.description: A library for building distributed systems using the actor model.

moons.name: ConcurrentTools
moons.description: A set of tools for managing concurrency and parallelism in ABCL applications.

curious_fact: ABCL introduced the concept of 'actor supervisors' to manage and recover from actor failures in distributed systems.

name: Ada2005
description: Ada2005 is an extended and enhanced version of the Ada programming language, developed by the Ada Core Technologies (ACT) team. It introduces several new features and improvements to the original Ada language, aiming to make it more robust, flexible, and suitable for modern software development.
connectionsExplanation: Ada2005 is a direct descendant of the Ada programming language, which was originally developed in the late 1970s and early 1980s. It builds upon the strong typing, concurrency, and safety features of its predecessor, further refining them to meet the evolving needs of real-time and embedded systems development.

moons.name: GNAT
moons.description: GNAT is a free and open-source Ada compiler and development environment. It is widely used for developing Ada applications on various platforms.

moons.name: SPARK
moons.description: SPARK is a subset of Ada designed for high-integrity systems. It provides formal verification tools to ensure the correctness of critical software components.

curious_fact: Ada2005 introduced the concept of 'aspect specifications', which allow for more declarative and expressive code.
example: with Ada.Text_IO; use Ada.Text_IO; procedure Hello is begin Put_Line("Hello, World!"); end Hello;

name: Ada2012
description: Ada2012 is an evolution of the Ada programming language, designed to support the development of large, long-lived applications and real-time systems. It is known for its strong typing, concurrency support, and emphasis on safety and reliability.
connectionsExplanation: Ada2012 is a direct descendant of Ada95, which itself evolved from the original Ada language. Ada was developed by the U.S. Department of Defense in the 1980s to meet the needs of large-scale, real-time systems. The evolution from Ada95 to Ada2012 introduced new features and improvements, maintaining the language's focus on safety and reliability.

moons.name: GNAT
moons.description: GNAT is a free and open-source Ada compiler and development environment.

moons.name: SPARK
moons.description: SPARK is a subset of Ada designed for high-integrity systems, providing formal verification tools.

curious_fact: Ada was named after Ada Lovelace, the first computer programmer.
example: with Ada.Text_IO; use Ada.Text_IO; procedure Hello is begin Put_Line("Hello, World!"); end Hello;

name: Ada2022
description: Ada2022 is the latest version of the Ada programming language, developed by the Ada Core Technologies team. It is designed for high-reliability and high-performance systems, particularly in aerospace, defense, and automotive industries.
connectionsExplanation: Ada2022 is a direct descendant of Ada95, which itself evolved from the original Ada language. Ada was developed in the late 1970s and early 1980s by a team led by Jean Ichbiah at CII-Honeywell Bull, with significant contributions from the U.S. Department of Defense. Ada95, released in 1995, introduced object-oriented features and became the basis for Ada2022.

moons.name: SPARK
moons.description: SPARK is a subset of Ada designed for high-integrity systems, providing formal verification tools.

curious_fact: Ada was named after Ada Lovelace, the first computer programmer.
example: with Ada.Text_IO; use Ada.Text_IO; procedure Hello is begin Put_Line("Hello, World!"); end Hello;

name: Ada80
description: Ada80, officially known as Ada, is a statically typed, compiled, and strongly typed programming language designed by the United States Department of Defense. It was developed to meet the needs of large, long-lived, and high-integrity systems. Ada80 is known for its strong typing, concurrency support, and real-time systems capabilities.
connectionsExplanation: Ada80 was developed as a successor to the PL/I language and was influenced by several other languages including Pascal, Modula-2, and C. It was designed to address the shortcomings of existing languages in terms of safety, reliability, and maintainability, especially for defense-related applications.

moons.description: SPARK is a subset of Ada80 designed for high-integrity systems, providing formal verification capabilities.

curious_fact: Ada80 was named after Ada Lovelace, the first computer programmer.
example: with Ada.Text_IO; use Ada.Text_IO; procedure Hello is begin Put_Line("Hello, World!"); end Hello;

name: Ada83
description: Ada83, formally known as Ada, is a statically typed, compiled, and strongly-typed programming language designed by the United States Department of Defense. It was developed to meet the needs of large, long-lived, and highly reliable software systems, particularly those used in military and aerospace applications.
connectionsExplanation: Ada83 was developed as a successor to the earlier Ada programming language, which was itself inspired by Pascal and other languages. It was designed to be a modern, reliable, and efficient language for systems programming, with a focus on safety, security, and real-time performance. Ada83 introduced several key features such as strong typing, exception handling, and concurrency support, which were influenced by the needs of its primary users in the defense industry.

name: Ada95
description: Ada95, formally known as Ada, is a statically typed, compiled, and strongly-typed programming language designed by the U.S. Department of Defense. It was developed to meet the needs of large, long-lived, and highly reliable software systems. Ada95 introduced several improvements over its predecessor, including object-oriented features, generics, and a more comprehensive standard library.
connectionsExplanation: Ada95 evolved from the original Ada language, which was developed in the late 1970s and early 1980s. The Ada95 revision, officially released in 1995, introduced significant enhancements to the language, making it more versatile and suitable for a wider range of applications. These improvements included object-oriented programming features, which were not present in the original Ada language.

moons.description: SPARK is a subset of Ada95 designed for high-integrity systems, providing formal verification tools.

curious_fact: Ada95 was named after Ada Lovelace, the first computer programmer.
