name: Address Programming Language
description: Address Programming Language is a fictional esoteric programming language designed for educational purposes. It emphasizes the manipulation of memory addresses directly, making it highly abstract and challenging to use.
connectionsExplanation: Address Programming Language is inspired by the concept of low-level memory manipulation found in assembly languages. It does not have a direct historical predecessor but draws from the paradigm of direct memory access, which is a fundamental aspect of assembly languages.
curious_fact: Address Programming Language was created to challenge programmers' understanding of memory management.

name: AdvPL
description: AdvPL is a programming language developed by TOTVS for use in their ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) software. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, making it popular among Brazilian developers for business applications.
connectionsExplanation: AdvPL evolved from the TOTVS's internal language used in their early ERP systems. It was designed to integrate seamlessly with TOTVS's software, providing a powerful tool for business automation and data management.
curious_fact: AdvPL is known for its integration with TOTVS's ERP, allowing developers to create custom solutions tailored to specific business needs.

name: Advanced Business Application Language
description: Advanced Business Application Language (ABAL) is a programming language developed by IBM for use in business applications. It was designed to be easy to learn and use, with a focus on business data processing.
connectionsExplanation: ABAL was developed as a business-oriented language to simplify programming for IBM's mainframe systems. It was influenced by earlier business languages like COBOL but was designed to be more accessible and efficient for business applications.
curious_fact: ABAL was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of structured programming in business applications.

name: Adventure Definition Language
description: Adventure Definition Language (ADL) is a fictional programming language designed for creating text-based adventure games. It was conceptualized as a simplified language for game developers to define game scenarios, puzzles, and interactions without needing to write extensive code.
connectionsExplanation: ADL was inspired by the early text adventure games of the 1970s, such as 'Colossal Cave Adventure' and 'Zork'. It aimed to provide a more accessible way for game designers to create similar experiences without deep programming knowledge.
curious_fact: ADL was never implemented but is often referenced in discussions about game development history.
example: define room 'Dungeon' with description 'You are in a dark, damp dungeon.'; define object 'sword' with description 'A rusty sword.'; define action 'take sword' with effect 'You pick up the sword.';

name: Advicetaker
description: Advicetaker is a fictional programming language designed to simulate decision-making processes in software. It is inspired by natural language processing and artificial intelligence, aiming to provide a more intuitive and human-like approach to programming.
connectionsExplanation: Advicetaker was created as an experiment in combining AI-driven decision-making with traditional programming paradigms. While it does not have a direct historical predecessor, it draws inspiration from languages like Lisp and Prolog, which have strong roots in symbolic computation and logical reasoning.
curious_fact: Advicetaker uses a unique 'advice' system to guide the execution of code, making it stand out from traditional programming languages.

name: Agda
description: Agda is a dependently typed functional programming language based on Martin-Löf Type Theory. It is designed for writing correct programs by construction, using a system of types that allows for the specification of precise program behavior.
connectionsExplanation: Agda evolved from the earlier programming language Ulf Norell's PhD thesis work on a similar system. It is closely related to other dependently typed languages like Coq and Idris, all of which are rooted in Martin-Löf Type Theory.
curious_fact: Agda's type system is so powerful that it can be used to prove theorems about programs, ensuring correctness by construction.
example:
module HelloWorld where

main : IO ()
main = putStrLn "Hello, world!"

name: AgentCubes
description: AgentCubes is a visual programming environment designed for creating agent-based simulations. It allows users to build simulations by dragging and dropping blocks to define agent behaviors, making it accessible for educational purposes and creative projects.
connectionsExplanation: AgentCubes is inspired by the concept of agent-based modeling, which is a computational modeling technique used to simulate the actions and interactions of autonomous agents. It draws parallels with Logo, another educational programming language, in its approach to visual programming and simplicity.
curious_fact: AgentCubes was developed at the MIT Media Lab and is known for its user-friendly interface that encourages creativity and learning through simulation.

name: AgentSheets
description: AgentSheets is a visual programming environment designed for creating agent-based simulations. It allows users to model complex systems by defining agents with behaviors and interactions. The tool is particularly popular in educational settings for teaching computational thinking and modeling.
connectionsExplanation: AgentSheets was developed by the MIT Media Lab and is inspired by the paradigm of agent-based modeling, which is a method for simulating the actions and interactions of autonomous agents in a system. It is not directly derived from any traditional programming language but is built on the principles of visual programming and computational modeling.
curious_fact: AgentSheets was one of the first tools to popularize agent-based modeling in educational settings.

name: AgentSpeak
description: AgentSpeak is a high-level programming language designed for the development of multi-agent systems. It is particularly suited for scenarios where agents need to communicate and collaborate to achieve common goals.
connectionsExplanation: AgentSpeak was developed as part of the Jason framework, which is an open-source platform for building multi-agent systems. It is inspired by Prolog and Lisp, integrating features from both to provide a declarative and expressive language for agent-based programming.
curious_fact: AgentSpeak was created to simplify the development of complex agent-based systems, making it easier for developers to model and simulate interactions between autonomous agents.

name: Agora
description: Agora is a programming language designed for distributed computing and concurrency. It emphasizes simplicity and ease of use in building distributed systems.
connectionsExplanation: Agora was inspired by the need for a language that could handle distributed computing more intuitively. It draws from paradigms seen in languages like Erlang and Go, but with a focus on simplicity and ease of use.
curious_fact: Agora was one of the first languages to introduce a simple syntax for distributed programming, making it accessible to developers without extensive background in concurrency.

name: AiScript
description: AiScript is a specialized programming language designed for artificial intelligence and machine learning tasks. It provides high-level abstractions and optimizations tailored for AI applications, making it easier for developers to implement complex algorithms and models.
connectionsExplanation: AiScript was developed as an extension of JavaScript, incorporating advanced AI-specific features and optimizations. It aims to bridge the gap between general-purpose programming and AI-centric development, leveraging JavaScript's widespread adoption and ecosystem.

moons.name: TensorFlow
moons.description: A library for numerical computation and machine learning.

moons.name: PyTorch
moons.description: An open-source machine learning library based on the Torch library.

curious_fact: AiScript was created by a team of AI researchers to simplify the development of AI applications, focusing on ease of use and performance.

name: Aldor
description: Aldor is a strongly typed, purely functional programming language designed for writing mathematical software. It is a descendant of the A programming language and is known for its advanced type system and support for symbolic computation.
connectionsExplanation: Aldor evolved from the A programming language, which was developed at the University of Kent at Canterbury. It was designed to provide a more robust and expressive type system for mathematical computations, making it suitable for developing complex mathematical software.
curious_fact: Aldor was one of the first languages to support dependent types, which allow types to depend on values.

name: Alef
description: Alef is a programming language designed by Alan Kay and Adele Goldberg in the 1980s. It was intended to be a pure object-oriented language, predating Smalltalk. Alef was part of the experimental Object-Oriented Software System (OOS) project at Xerox PARC.
connectionsExplanation: Alef is considered a precursor to modern object-oriented programming languages. It was developed during the same period as Smalltalk, which is widely recognized as one of the first successful object-oriented languages. Alef's influence can be seen in the design philosophies of later languages, particularly in the emphasis on objects and messages.
curious_fact: Alef was one of the first languages to use the concept of 'messaging' between objects, a feature that would later become a hallmark of Smalltalk.

name: Aleph
description: Aleph is a fictional programming language designed for advanced theoretical computing and artificial intelligence research. It features a unique syntax and is known for its ability to handle complex algorithms efficiently.
connectionsExplanation: Aleph was inspired by the need for a language that could support the advanced computational models required for AI research. It draws influences from functional programming paradigms and incorporates elements of symbolic computation.
curious_fact: Aleph was created by a team of researchers at MIT in the early 2000s.

name: Algebraic Logic Functional Programming Language
description: ALF is a purely functional programming language developed in the 1970s. It is notable for its strong mathematical foundation and its use of algebraic data types and logic programming principles. Despite its theoretical significance, ALF has not gained widespread adoption.
connectionsExplanation: ALF was developed as part of the research into functional programming and logic programming paradigms. It was influenced by earlier functional languages like Lisp and the logic programming language Prolog. Its development aimed to integrate the strengths of both paradigms into a single language.
curious_fact: ALF was one of the first languages to explore the integration of functional and logic programming concepts.
example: let rec factorial n = if n = 0 then 1 else n * factorial (n - 1); factorial 5

name: Algek
description: Algek is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by the Algol family but with a unique syntax and semantics.
connectionsExplanation: Algek is inspired by Algol, a pioneer in structured programming. While not a direct descendant, it shares Algol's emphasis on clear and concise code structure, making it a modern take on the Algol paradigm.
curious_fact: Algek was created to demonstrate the principles of structured programming in a fun and accessible way.

name: Algoid
description: Algoid is a modern programming language designed for algorithmic problem-solving and data manipulation. It emphasizes simplicity and efficiency, making it suitable for both educational purposes and professional applications.
connectionsExplanation: Algoid was inspired by the structured programming paradigm, aiming to provide a clean and intuitive syntax. It draws from the simplicity of BASIC and the efficiency of C, combining elements to create a language that is easy to learn yet powerful enough for complex tasks.

moons.name: Algolib
moons.description: A standard library for Algoid, providing common algorithms and data structures.

curious_fact: Algoid was developed by a team of educators and software engineers to bridge the gap between beginner-friendly languages and high-performance languages.

name: Alice
description: Alice is a fictional programming language created by Lewis Carroll in his 1865 novel 'Alice's Adventures in Wonderland'. It is a playful and nonsensical language designed to challenge conventional syntax and logic.
connectionsExplanation: Alice is inspired by the whimsical and nonsensical nature of Lewis Carroll's works. It does not have a direct historical predecessor but draws from the spirit of early experimental languages like INTERCAL and Befunge.
curious_fact: Alice was never intended to be a practical programming language but rather a satirical commentary on the complexity of early computer languages.

name: Allegro Common Lisp
description: Allegro Common Lisp (ACL) is a commercial implementation of the Common Lisp programming language developed by Franz Inc. It is known for its performance and extensive libraries, making it popular in industries requiring high-performance computing.
connectionsExplanation: Allegro Common Lisp is a direct descendant of Common Lisp, which itself evolved from earlier Lisp dialects like Maclisp and Interlisp. It inherits the functional programming paradigm and dynamic typing features of Common Lisp, providing a robust environment for both rapid development and high-performance applications.

moons.name: AllegroServe
moons.description: A web server framework for Common Lisp.

moons.name: AllegroCache
moons.description: A persistent object database for Common Lisp.

curious_fact: Allegro Common Lisp was one of the first commercial Lisp implementations to support Unicode.
example:
;; Hello World in Allegro Common Lisp
(princ "Hello, World!")

name: Alma-0
description: Alma-0 is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It emphasizes simplicity and clarity, making it an ideal language for beginners to learn the fundamentals of programming.
connectionsExplanation: Alma-0 was created as an educational tool, drawing inspiration from basic programming concepts and syntax. It is not derived from any existing language but is designed to be intuitive and easy to understand.
curious_fact: Alma-0 was developed by a team of educators to simplify the learning process for new programmers.

name: Alore
description: Alore is a dynamically typed, object-oriented programming language designed for ease of use and rapid development. It was developed in Finland and aimed to provide a modern alternative to languages like Python and Ruby.
connectionsExplanation: Alore was inspired by Python and Ruby, focusing on simplicity and ease of use. It aimed to offer a more dynamic and flexible approach to programming, making it suitable for rapid development and scripting tasks.
curious_fact: Alore was one of the early attempts to create a language that combined the ease of Python with the performance of compiled languages.

name: Alpaca
description: Alpaca is a domain-specific language (DSL) designed for generating HTML and web pages. It is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among web developers who prefer a more declarative approach to web development.
connectionsExplanation: Alpaca is inspired by the simplicity and readability of HTML, aiming to provide a more concise and expressive way to generate web content. It does not have a direct lineage from traditional programming languages but draws from the principles of DSLs and web templating systems.
curious_fact: Alpaca was created by a group of web developers who wanted a language that could generate HTML with less boilerplate code.
example: html { head { title { "My Web Page" } } body { h1 { "Welcome to Alpaca!" } } }

name: Alpha
description: Alpha is a futuristic programming language designed for advanced computational tasks and artificial intelligence. It features a unique syntax and is highly optimized for parallel processing.
connectionsExplanation: Alpha was developed as a successor to Beta, aiming to address the limitations of its predecessor in handling complex algorithms and large-scale data processing. Its design incorporates elements from functional programming and quantum computing paradigms.
curious_fact: Alpha was the first language to implement quantum algorithms natively.

name: Alphard
description: Alphard is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is inspired by the BASIC language but with modern syntax and features.
connectionsExplanation: Alphard was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts. It draws inspiration from BASIC, which was one of the first high-level programming languages, making it a logical descendant in the lineage of early educational languages.
curious_fact: Alphard was developed by a team of educators aiming to make programming accessible to children.

name: AltairBASIC
description: AltairBASIC was one of the first high-level programming languages designed for personal computers. It was developed by Bill Gates and Paul Allen specifically for the Altair 8800, one of the first mass-produced microcomputers. This language played a crucial role in popularizing personal computing and laid the groundwork for Microsoft's future success.
connectionsExplanation: AltairBASIC was developed in response to the Altair 8800's need for a user-friendly programming language. It was inspired by Dartmouth BASIC but was optimized for the limited resources of early microcomputers. This language was instrumental in demonstrating the potential of personal computing to hobbyists and professionals alike.
curious_fact: AltairBASIC was written in assembly language to fit into the 4KB of RAM available on the Altair 8800.

name: Altera Hardware Description Language
description: Altera Hardware Description Language (AHDL) is a hardware description language developed by Altera Corporation for designing digital circuits. It is used to describe the behavior of electronic systems in a high-level, readable format.
connectionsExplanation: AHDL was developed by Altera Corporation in the 1980s as a proprietary hardware description language. It was designed to facilitate the design and synthesis of digital circuits, providing a higher-level abstraction compared to traditional gate-level descriptions. AHDL is closely related to VHDL (VHSIC Hardware Description Language), which was developed around the same time by the European Space Agency and the U.S. Department of Defense. Both languages share similar syntax and concepts, making them part of the same family of hardware description languages.
curious_fact: AHDL was one of the first hardware description languages to be widely adopted in the industry, alongside VHDL.

name: Alusus
description: Alusus is a statically-typed, functional programming language designed for building high-performance systems. It emphasizes safety, correctness, and efficiency, making it suitable for applications where reliability is paramount.
connectionsExplanation: Alusus was developed as a modern alternative to existing functional programming languages, aiming to address some of the perceived limitations in performance and usability. It draws inspiration from functional paradigms but introduces unique features to enhance developer productivity and system performance.
curious_fact: Alusus features a novel type system that allows for compile-time verification of program correctness without sacrificing runtime performance.

name: Amazon Shading Language
description: Amazon Shading Language (ASL) is a specialized shading language developed by Amazon Web Services (AWS) for use in their graphics rendering and real-time 3D applications. It is designed to optimize performance on AWS's GPU infrastructure.
connectionsExplanation: ASL is inspired by and builds upon the principles of GLSL (OpenGL Shading Language), which is widely used for real-time graphics rendering. It is tailored to leverage AWS's cloud-based GPU resources, offering enhanced performance and scalability for graphics-intensive applications.
curious_fact: ASL was developed to support AWS's cloud-based gaming and virtual reality services, enabling developers to create high-performance graphics without the need for on-premises hardware.

name: Amber
description: Amber is an object-oriented programming language designed to be a modern, safe, and efficient alternative to Smalltalk. It was developed by Alan Kay and his team at Viewpoints Research Institute. Amber aims to provide a clean syntax and a powerful runtime environment, making it suitable for both rapid application development and large-scale systems.
connectionsExplanation: Amber is inspired by Smalltalk, one of the first object-oriented programming languages. It shares many of Smalltalk's principles, such as dynamic typing and reflective capabilities, but with modern improvements and optimizations. Amber's design reflects the evolution of object-oriented programming concepts since the 1980s.
curious_fact: Amber was named after the gemstone, symbolizing its clarity and beauty in programming.
example:
Object subclass: HelloWorld [
  | |
  HelloWorld class >> main [
    Transcript show: 'Hello, World!'; cr.
  ]
].

HelloWorld main.

name: AmbientTalk
description: AmbientTalk is a reflective, object-oriented programming language designed for distributed computing. It was developed at the Vrije Universiteit Brussel (VUB) and focuses on simplicity and expressiveness in concurrent programming.
connectionsExplanation: AmbientTalk was influenced by Smalltalk and E, both reflective object-oriented languages. It was designed to address the challenges of distributed systems by providing a simple and intuitive syntax for concurrent programming.
curious_fact: AmbientTalk was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of ambient objects, which can dynamically adapt to their environment.

name: AmiBlitz
description: AmiBlitz is a high-level programming language designed for the Commodore Amiga computer. It was developed to provide a simple and efficient way to create games and applications for the Amiga platform. Despite its age, AmiBlitz remains a niche language with a dedicated community.
connectionsExplanation: AmiBlitz was developed specifically for the Commodore Amiga platform, which was popular in the 1980s and early 1990s. It evolved from earlier BASIC dialects and was influenced by the need for a more modern and powerful language for Amiga development.

moons.name: Blitz3D
moons.description: Blitz3D is a 3D game development library that extends AmiBlitz, allowing developers to create 3D graphics and animations.

curious_fact: AmiBlitz was one of the first languages to introduce a 'fast compile' feature, significantly speeding up the development process on the Amiga.

name: AmigaBasic
description: AmigaBasic is a dialect of the BASIC programming language developed for the Commodore Amiga computer. It was designed to be easy to use and was included with many Amiga computers. Despite its simplicity, it was powerful enough for basic programming tasks and game development.
connectionsExplanation: AmigaBasic is a dialect of BASIC, which has its roots in the early 1960s. It evolved from earlier versions of BASIC and was specifically tailored for the Commodore Amiga, reflecting the platform's capabilities and user base.
curious_fact: AmigaBasic was one of the first languages to support multitasking on the Amiga, allowing users to run multiple programs simultaneously.

name: AmigaE
description: AmigaE is a high-level programming language designed for the Commodore Amiga computer. It was created to provide a more modern and efficient alternative to the Amiga's native languages like AmigaBASIC and C. AmigaE is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among hobbyists and small developers.
connectionsExplanation: AmigaE was developed to enhance the programming capabilities of the Commodore Amiga. It was influenced by the C programming language but aimed to be more accessible to beginners. The language was widely used in the Amiga community for developing games, utilities, and applications during the 1990s.
curious_fact: AmigaE was one of the first languages to support object-oriented programming on the Amiga platform.

name: Ana
description: Ana is a modern, high-level programming language designed for simplicity and ease of use. It emphasizes readability and developer productivity, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced programmers. Ana is dynamically typed and supports multiple programming paradigms, including procedural, object-oriented, and functional programming.
connectionsExplanation: Ana was developed as a response to the growing demand for a more accessible and efficient programming language. It draws inspiration from popular languages like Python and JavaScript, incorporating features that enhance developer experience while maintaining performance.

moons.name: LibAna
moons.description: A comprehensive library for data analysis and manipulation.

moons.name: WebAna
moons.description: A framework for building web applications with Ana.

curious_fact: Ana was created by a team of developers who previously worked on popular web browsers.

name: Analitik
description: Analitik is a lesser-known programming language developed in the 1960s. It was designed for scientific and engineering applications, focusing on ease of use and mathematical operations. Despite its initial popularity, it has since declined in usage due to the rise of more modern languages.
connectionsExplanation: Analitik was developed during a time when there was a need for specialized languages for scientific computing. It evolved from earlier languages like FORTRAN and COBOL, which were also designed for specific domains. However, unlike its predecessors, Analitik aimed to simplify the syntax and improve the user experience, which contributed to its initial success but ultimately led to its decline as more general-purpose languages emerged.
curious_fact: Analitik was one of the first languages to introduce a simple syntax for matrix operations, which was highly valued in scientific communities at the time.

name: Analytica
description: Analytica is a visual programming language developed by Lumina Decision Systems. It is designed for modeling and analyzing complex systems, particularly in fields like economics, engineering, and science. Analytica uses a spreadsheet-like interface but with a focus on dependency-directed computation.
connectionsExplanation: Analytica was developed as a successor to SuperDecision, a software for decision analysis. It was designed to address the limitations of traditional spreadsheet software in handling complex models and dependencies.
curious_fact: Analytica is known for its ability to automatically propagate uncertainty through models, making it a powerful tool for risk analysis.
example: Var X := 10; Var Y := 20; Result := X + Y;

name: Analytical Engine Order Code
description: Analytical Engine Order Code is a theoretical programming language inspired by Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine, a mechanical general-purpose computer designed in the early 19th century. It is a conceptual language that represents the foundational ideas of computer programming before the advent of electronic computers.
connectionsExplanation: Analytical Engine Order Code is considered the root of all modern programming languages. Charles Babbage's design, though never fully built, laid the groundwork for the concept of a programmable computer. This theoretical language represents the initial vision of algorithmic instructions that could be processed by a machine.
curious_fact: The Analytical Engine was designed to perform any calculation given to it, making it a precursor to modern computers.
example: ADD 1 TO 2, STORE RESULT IN A

name: AngelScript
description: AngelScript is an embedded scripting language designed to be easy to integrate into applications. It is often used in game development for adding scripting capabilities without the need for a separate programming language.
connectionsExplanation: AngelScript was developed by Andreas Jönsson as a lightweight scripting language for game development. It is inspired by C++ and is designed to be easy to learn and integrate, making it a popular choice for game developers who need scripting capabilities.
curious_fact: AngelScript was originally developed for use in the game 'Worms 2: Armageddon'.

name: Apache Ant
description: Apache Ant is a Java-based build tool designed to automate the build process for Java applications. It uses XML files to describe the build process and dependencies.
connectionsExplanation: Apache Ant was developed to address the limitations of the traditional make utility, which was not well-suited for Java projects due to its reliance on Unix-like environments. Ant was created by James Duncan Davidson and others at Apache Software Foundation in 2000.
curious_fact: Apache Ant was one of the first tools to popularize the use of XML for build configuration in software development.

name: Apache Groovy
description: Apache Groovy is a powerful, dynamic language for the Java platform. It builds on the strengths of Java but simplifies development with additional features like closures, metaprogramming, and a concise syntax. Groovy is widely used for scripting, automation, and building web applications.
connectionsExplanation: Apache Groovy was created to provide a more dynamic and productive alternative to Java. It was inspired by languages like Ruby and Python, aiming to offer a scripting-like experience on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM). Groovy's design allows it to seamlessly integrate with Java libraries and frameworks, making it a popular choice for Java developers looking to enhance their productivity.

moons.name: Grails
moons.description: A web application framework built on Groovy and Spring.

moons.name: Spock
moons.description: A testing and specification framework for Java and Groovy applications.

curious_fact: Groovy was named after the musical instrument, reflecting its harmonious integration with Java.

name: Apex
description: Apex is a strongly typed, object-oriented programming language developed by Salesforce. It is primarily used for developing custom business logic on the Salesforce platform. Apex is designed to be easy to learn and integrates seamlessly with Salesforce's declarative features.
connectionsExplanation: Apex was developed by Salesforce to provide a way to write custom business logic for their platform. It is built on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) and is designed to be familiar to Java developers. Apex is a direct descendant of Java, inheriting many of its features and syntax.

moons.name: Force.com
moons.description: A cloud-based platform for building custom business applications.

name: Apple Dylan
description: Apple Dylan is a dialect of the Dylan programming language developed by Apple Computer. It was designed to be a modern, dynamic language with a focus on ease of use and integration with Apple's ecosystem. Despite its promising start, it never gained widespread adoption.
connectionsExplanation: Apple Dylan was developed as a dialect of the Dylan programming language, which was created in the late 1980s. While it was intended to be a modern alternative to languages like Lisp and Smalltalk, Apple's focus on integrating it with their ecosystem led to a limited audience. The language's development was largely driven by Apple's internal needs rather than a broader market appeal.
curious_fact: Apple Dylan was one of the first languages to support Unicode natively.
example:
define method hello-world () => ()
  format-out("Hello, World!\n")
end method

name: AppleScript
description: AppleScript is a scripting language developed by Apple Inc. for its Macintosh computers. It allows users to automate tasks and control applications on macOS.
connectionsExplanation: AppleScript was introduced in 1993 as part of System 7.5 and was designed to be a high-level, English-like scripting language that could be used to automate tasks on Macintosh computers. It was inspired by HyperTalk, the scripting language used in HyperCard, and was intended to provide a more powerful and flexible scripting environment for Mac users.

moons.name: Automator

curious_fact: AppleScript was one of the first scripting languages to be integrated into a major operating system.
example:
tell application "Safari"
    activate
    open location "https://www.example.com"

name: Applesoft BASIC
description: Applesoft BASIC is a dialect of the BASIC programming language developed by Apple Computer for its Apple II series of personal computers. It was one of the first high-level programming languages available on a mass-market personal computer.
connectionsExplanation: Applesoft BASIC was developed by Apple Computer specifically for the Apple II series, making it a direct descendant of the original Dartmouth BASIC. It was designed to be user-friendly and accessible, which contributed to the popularity of the Apple II in educational settings.
curious_fact: Applesoft BASIC was one of the first programming languages to support color graphics on a personal computer.

name: APL
description: APL (A Programming Language) is a high-level array-oriented programming language developed by Kenneth E. Iverson. It is known for its concise syntax and mathematical notation, making it particularly suited for scientific and engineering applications. Despite its age, APL continues to be used in niche areas and has influenced many modern programming languages.
connectionsExplanation: APL was developed by Kenneth E. Iverson in the early 1960s at IBM. It was one of the first languages to use a mathematical notation for programming, which influenced the development of other array-oriented languages like APL2 and J. APL's influence can also be seen in the design of modern languages that emphasize functional programming and concise syntax.

moons.name: APL2
moons.description: APL2 is an extension of APL that adds new features and improvements, including a more structured syntax and enhanced array manipulation capabilities.

moons.name: J
moons.description: J is a modern successor to APL, designed to be more accessible and efficient. It maintains the array-oriented paradigm but with a more conventional syntax.

curious_fact: APL was originally designed to be a notation for mathematical expressions, not a full programming language.

name: Arc
description: Arc is a Lisp-like programming language designed by Paul Graham. It emphasizes simplicity and ease of use, aiming to make programming more accessible. Arc is known for its minimalistic syntax and its focus on functional programming.
connectionsExplanation: Arc evolved from Lisp, specifically drawing inspiration from Common Lisp and Scheme. It was designed to address some of the perceived shortcomings of existing Lisp dialects, aiming to provide a more modern and user-friendly environment.
curious_fact: Arc was one of the languages used to build the popular web application Reddit.

name: Arduino
description: Arduino is an open-source electronics platform based on easy-to-use hardware and software. It's widely used for building interactive electronic devices and projects.
connectionsExplanation: Arduino was developed by a group of students and hobbyists at the Interaction Design Institute Ivrea, Italy. It was inspired by Processing, a programming language for visual arts, and aims to make electronics accessible to artists, designers, educators, and hobbyists.

moons.description: The official Integrated Development Environment for Arduino programming.

curious_fact: Arduino was named after the Italian Renaissance polymath Leonardo da Vinci's robot, Automaton.

name: Arduino Programming Language
description: Arduino Programming Language is a C++-based language designed for writing code for Arduino boards. It simplifies the process of programming microcontrollers, making it accessible to hobbyists and artists.
connectionsExplanation: Arduino Programming Language is based on C++ and is designed to be easy to learn and use for beginners. It was developed by Arduino.cc, an open-source hardware and software company, to facilitate the development of interactive electronic projects.

moons.name: Arduino IDE
moons.description: The official Integrated Development Environment for Arduino programming.

moons.name: Arduino CLI
moons.description: Command-line interface for Arduino development.

curious_fact: Arduino was originally developed by a group of students and professors at the Interaction Design Institute Ivrea in Italy.

name: Arendelle
description: Arendelle is a fictional programming language created for the animated film Frozen. It is designed to be simple and elegant, with a focus on visual programming elements. The language is not intended for practical use but serves as a creative tool for the film's story.
connectionsExplanation: Arendelle is a fictional language created for the film Frozen, inspired by the visual and magical elements of the movie. It is not based on any real programming language but shares some conceptual similarities with visual programming languages like Scratch.
curious_fact: Arendelle's syntax is designed to resemble the icy landscapes of the film, with commands like 'snowflake' and 'iceberg'.

name: Argh!
description: Argh! is an esoteric programming language inspired by Brainfuck. It is known for its minimalistic syntax and is often used as a joke or a challenge for programmers.
connectionsExplanation: Argh! was created as a humorous take on Brainfuck, a minimalist esoteric programming language. It shares the same core concept of using a small set of commands to manipulate memory, but with a twist in syntax and command set.
curious_fact: Argh! was created by David Morgan-Mar, who also created Brainfuck.

name: Argos
description: Argos is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid application development. It emphasizes simplicity and ease of use, making it accessible to beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: Argos was developed as a successor to the popular scripting language 'Bash', aiming to improve its syntax and add more robust features. It draws inspiration from the simplicity of Python and the flexibility of JavaScript, making it a versatile tool for various programming tasks.

curious_fact: Argos was named after the ancient Greek god of hunting, symbolizing its versatility in handling a wide range of programming tasks.

name: Argus
description: Argus is a modern, high-performance programming language designed for systems programming and real-time applications. It emphasizes safety, concurrency, and efficiency, making it suitable for developing operating systems, network protocols, and embedded systems.
connectionsExplanation: Argus was developed as a successor to C, aiming to address some of the limitations and safety issues present in C while maintaining its performance. It draws inspiration from languages like Rust and Go, incorporating features such as memory safety without garbage collection and strong concurrency support.

moons.description: A real-time operating system built using the Argus programming language.

moons.name: Argus-NET
moons.description: A networking library for Argus, providing high-performance network communication capabilities.

curious_fact: Argus was named after the mythological Greek god Argus, known for his ability to see all and his many eyes.

name: ArkTS
description: ArkTS is a TypeScript-based programming language developed by Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd. It is designed for building high-performance applications, particularly for mobile and desktop platforms. ArkTS aims to provide a seamless development experience by leveraging the strengths of TypeScript while optimizing for performance and resource efficiency.
connectionsExplanation: ArkTS is a modern language developed by Huawei, which builds upon the robustness and type safety of TypeScript. It is designed to address the specific needs of high-performance application development, particularly in the context of Huawei's ecosystem. While it shares some similarities with TypeScript, ArkTS introduces optimizations and features tailored for Huawei's hardware and software environments.
curious_fact: ArkTS was developed to support Huawei's vision of a fully connected, intelligent world, emphasizing performance and efficiency in application development.

name: ArmScript
description: ArmScript is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by ARM assembly language but abstracts away the complexity, making it accessible to beginners.
connectionsExplanation: ArmScript was created as an educational tool to introduce the concepts of assembly language in a more approachable manner. It draws inspiration from ARM assembly, which is widely used in embedded systems and mobile devices, but simplifies the syntax and structure to facilitate learning.
curious_fact: ArmScript was developed by a team of educators aiming to bridge the gap between high-level programming and low-level assembly language.

name: Armada
description: Armada is a fictional programming language designed for space exploration and interstellar communication. It emphasizes efficiency and compactness, making it ideal for transmitting code across vast distances.
connectionsExplanation: Armada was inspired by the need for a language that could handle the unique challenges of space communication. It draws from the paradigms of C and Assembly, focusing on low-level control and high performance.
curious_fact: Armada's syntax is based on binary-like structures, allowing for rapid code transmission.

name: Artemis
description: Artemis is a modern, high-performance programming language designed for distributed systems and cloud computing. It emphasizes safety, reliability, and efficiency, making it suitable for large-scale applications.
connectionsExplanation: Artemis was developed by a team of researchers at the University of California, Berkeley, inspired by the need for a language that could handle the complexities of modern distributed systems. It draws on concepts from functional programming and concurrent programming paradigms.

moons.description: A framework for building distributed applications in Artemis.

moons.name: Galileo
moons.description: A library for data processing and analytics in Artemis.

curious_fact: Artemis was named after the Greek goddess of the hunt, symbolizing its ability to efficiently track and manage resources in distributed systems.

name: AspectC++
description: AspectC++ is an extension of the C++ programming language that supports aspect-oriented programming (AOP). It allows developers to encapsulate cross-cutting concerns, such as logging and error handling, in separate modules called aspects.
connectionsExplanation: AspectC++ was developed as an extension to C++ to support aspect-oriented programming. It builds upon the C++ language, adding features that allow for modularization of cross-cutting concerns, which are common in large software systems.
curious_fact: AspectC++ was one of the first languages to introduce aspect-oriented programming to C++.
example: aspect LoggingAspect { advice call("*::log()") : before() { cout << "Logging call to " << joinPoint->signature() << endl; } };

name: AspectJ
description: AspectJ is an aspect-oriented programming language that extends the Java programming language. It provides a way to encapsulate cross-cutting concerns, such as logging, transaction management, and security, in separate modules called aspects.
connectionsExplanation: AspectJ evolved from the Aspect-Oriented Programming (AOP) paradigm, which was first introduced in the late 1990s. It builds upon the Java language, adding support for aspects that can be woven into the Java bytecode. This allows developers to modularize cross-cutting concerns, improving code maintainability and separation of concerns.
curious_fact: AspectJ was developed by the Eclipse Foundation and is widely used in enterprise environments for its robust AOP capabilities.
example: public aspect LoggingAspect { pointcut methodExecution(): execution(* *(..)); before(): methodExecution() { System.out.println("Method execution started"); } }

name: AssemblyScript
description: AssemblyScript is a TypeScript-like language that compiles to WebAssembly. It allows developers to write high-level code while maintaining the performance benefits of WebAssembly.
connectionsExplanation: AssemblyScript is a direct descendant of TypeScript, designed to compile to WebAssembly. It leverages TypeScript's syntax and type system to provide a familiar development experience for web developers while enabling the use of WebAssembly for performance-critical applications.
curious_fact: AssemblyScript was created to bring the benefits of TypeScript to the WebAssembly ecosystem, allowing developers to write performant web applications with familiar syntax.
example: function greet(name: string): string { return "Hello, " + name + "!"; }

name: Astek
description: Astek is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not widely used outside of academic settings.
connectionsExplanation: Astek was created as a teaching tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts without the complexity of existing languages. It draws inspiration from basic procedural languages but with a modern syntax.
curious_fact: Astek was developed by a team of educators aiming to make programming accessible to children.

name: Asymptote
description: Asymptote is a powerful descriptive vector graphics language for technical drawing, inspired by MetaPost but with an improved C++-like syntax. It is particularly popular in mathematical and scientific illustrations.
connectionsExplanation: Asymptote was developed by Andy Hammerlindl, John C. Bowman, and Tom Prince. It is inspired by MetaPost but incorporates features from C++ and other modern programming languages, making it a unique tool for creating complex technical graphics.
curious_fact: Asymptote can generate high-quality output for complex mathematical illustrations, often used in academic publications.

name: AtScript
description: AtScript is a programming language developed by Google as an extension to TypeScript, aimed at providing advanced type system features and better tooling support for AngularJS applications. It was designed to improve the development experience for large-scale applications.
connectionsExplanation: AtScript was developed as an extension to TypeScript, which itself is a superset of JavaScript. It was created to enhance the type system and provide better tooling for AngularJS applications, reflecting Google's focus on improving JavaScript development for complex web applications.
curious_fact: AtScript was intended to be a temporary solution until TypeScript could fully support the advanced features it introduced.
example: class Greeter { constructor(public greeting: string) { } greet() { return "Hello, " + this.greeting; } } let greeter = new Greeter("world"); console.log(greeter.greet());

name: AtariBASIC
description: AtariBASIC is a dialect of the BASIC programming language developed by Atari, Inc. for their 8-bit home computers, such as the Atari 400 and 800. It was designed to be easy to learn and use, making it popular among beginners and hobbyists in the late 1970s and early 1980s.
connectionsExplanation: AtariBASIC is a direct descendant of the original Dartmouth BASIC, which was developed in the late 1960s. It evolved to meet the specific needs of the Atari 8-bit computers, incorporating features that were optimized for the hardware capabilities of the time.
curious_fact: AtariBASIC was one of the first programming languages to be included with a home computer system, making it accessible to a wide audience.

name: AtariLOGO
description: AtariLOGO is a dialect of the LOGO programming language developed by Atari for their educational line of computers. It was designed to introduce children to programming concepts through turtle graphics.
connectionsExplanation: AtariLOGO is a direct descendant of the original LOGO language, which was developed by Seymour Papert and his team at MIT in the late 1960s. It was specifically tailored for the Atari 8-bit computers, incorporating features that made it accessible to young users.
curious_fact: AtariLOGO was one of the first programming languages designed specifically for educational purposes in home computers.

name: AtariMicrosoftBASIC
description: AtariMicrosoftBASIC is a variant of the BASIC programming language developed by Microsoft for the Atari 8-bit family of computers. It was widely used in educational settings and for simple game development during the 1980s.
connectionsExplanation: AtariMicrosoftBASIC evolved from the original Dartmouth BASIC, which was developed in the early 1960s. It was specifically tailored for the Atari 8-bit computers, incorporating features that were optimized for the hardware capabilities of the time.
curious_fact: AtariMicrosoftBASIC was one of the first programming languages to be widely available on home computers, making it accessible to a broad audience.

name: AtariPascal
description: AtariPascal is a Pascal dialect developed by Apple Computer for the Apple II series of computers. It was designed to be a simple and easy-to-learn programming language suitable for beginners and hobbyists. AtariPascal was part of the Apple Pascal System, which included a compiler and integrated development environment.
connectionsExplanation: AtariPascal evolved from the standard Pascal language, which was developed by Niklaus Wirth in the late 1960s. It was designed to be more accessible for the Apple II platform, incorporating features that catered to the hardware and software environment of the time.
curious_fact: AtariPascal was one of the first programming languages to be widely used on personal computers, helping to democratize software development in the early 1980s.

name: AtariSTBASIC
description: AtariSTBASIC is a dialect of the BASIC programming language designed for the Atari ST series of computers. It was included as a built-in language in the operating system, making it accessible to users for writing simple programs and games.
connectionsExplanation: AtariSTBASIC is a direct descendant of the BASIC language, which was developed in the late 1960s. It evolved to cater to the specific hardware and software environment of the Atari ST, a popular home computer in the mid-1980s.
curious_fact: AtariSTBASIC was one of the first BASIC dialects to support color graphics and sound capabilities on home computers.

name: AtejiPX
description: AtejiPX is a programming language designed to support parallel programming and concurrency. It extends C++ with additional constructs for specifying parallelism and synchronization. The language is particularly suited for applications requiring high performance and efficient use of multi-core processors.
connectionsExplanation: AtejiPX is an extension of C++ and was developed to address the challenges of parallel programming. It introduces new constructs and syntax to facilitate the specification of parallel tasks and synchronization, making it a specialized tool for high-performance computing environments.
curious_fact: AtejiPX was one of the first languages to introduce parallel programming constructs directly into C++ syntax.

name: AtlasAutocode
description: AtlasAutocode is a modern programming language designed to simplify the process of building and deploying applications. It focuses on developer productivity and automation, making it easier to create complex systems with minimal code.
connectionsExplanation: AtlasAutocode was developed to address the growing need for automation in software development. It draws inspiration from modern paradigms such as functional programming and declarative syntax, aiming to provide a more intuitive and efficient coding experience.

name: Atom
description: Atom is a hackable text editor developed by GitHub. It is built on web technologies and is highly customizable with a rich ecosystem of packages and themes.
connectionsExplanation: Atom was created by GitHub to provide a flexible and extensible text editor for developers. It is not directly derived from any traditional programming language but is inspired by the need for a modern, web-based editor that can integrate seamlessly with GitHub's workflow.

name: Austral
description: Austral is a purely functional programming language designed to be simple and elegant. It emphasizes immutability and type safety, aiming to provide a clean and concise syntax for developers.
connectionsExplanation: Austral is inspired by Haskell and other purely functional languages. It was created to explore new paradigms in functional programming, focusing on simplicity and ease of use.
curious_fact: Austral was developed as a research project to investigate the practicality of a purely functional language in modern software development.

name: AutoHotkey
description: AutoHotkey is a scripting language for Windows that allows users to automate repetitive tasks, create custom keyboard shortcuts, and automate GUI interactions. It is widely used for productivity enhancements and game automation.
connectionsExplanation: AutoHotkey was created by Chris Mallett in 2003 as a tool to automate repetitive tasks on Windows. It evolved from earlier scripting tools and is inspired by BASIC and other scripting languages. AutoHotkey is not directly derived from any specific language but draws from a lineage of scripting and automation tools.

moons.description: An enhanced version of AutoHotkey with improved Unicode support and additional features.

curious_fact: AutoHotkey scripts can be compiled into standalone executables, making them portable.

name: AutoHotkey
description: AutoHotkey is a scripting language for Windows that allows users to automate repetitive tasks, create custom keyboard shortcuts, and automate GUI interactions. It is widely used for productivity enhancements and game automation.
connectionsExplanation: AutoHotkey evolved from AutoIt, a scripting language designed for automating the Windows GUI. It was created by Chris Mallett and has since become a popular tool for Windows automation tasks.
curious_fact: AutoHotkey scripts can be compiled into standalone executables, making them easy to distribute.

name: AutoIt
description: AutoIt is a scripting language designed for automating the Windows GUI. It is particularly popular for automating repetitive tasks, game scripting, and creating simple applications. AutoIt is known for its ease of use and the ability to interact with Windows applications.
connectionsExplanation: AutoIt was created by Jonathan Bennett in 1999. It is inspired by BASIC and Visual Basic, but with a focus on Windows automation. AutoIt is not directly derived from any specific language but draws from the scripting paradigms of BASIC and Visual Basic, making it a unique tool in the automation space.

moons.description: A library that allows AutoIt scripts to interact with other applications and the Windows API.

curious_fact: AutoIt was originally created to automate repetitive tasks in Windows, such as clicking buttons and filling out forms.

name: AutoLISP
description: AutoLISP is a dialect of the LISP programming language developed by Autodesk for use with its AutoCAD software. It is used for automating tasks within AutoCAD and is known for its ability to interact with the AutoCAD application program interface (API).
connectionsExplanation: AutoLISP evolved from Common Lisp and is specifically tailored for use with AutoCAD. It inherits the functional programming paradigm and list processing capabilities of LISP, making it well-suited for tasks involving geometric data manipulation.
curious_fact: AutoLISP was one of the first programming languages to be integrated directly into a commercial CAD application.

name: Automatically Programmed Tool
description: Automatically Programmed Tool (APT) was an early programming language developed in the 1960s for use in the aerospace industry. It was designed to simplify the programming of numerical control systems for machine tools. APT was one of the first languages to use English-like syntax, making it accessible to engineers who were not computer specialists.
connectionsExplanation: APT was developed at MIT in the 1960s as part of the Air Force Cambridge Research Center's efforts to automate the programming of machine tools. It was one of the pioneering languages in the field of numerical control and was used extensively in the aerospace industry for tasks such as milling and drilling.
curious_fact: APT was one of the first languages to use English-like syntax, which made it easier for non-programmers to use.

name: Avenue
description: Avenue is a procedural programming language developed by Apple Computer for the Apple II series of computers. It was designed to be easy to learn and use, making it popular among beginners and educators. Despite its simplicity, Avenue was not widely adopted outside of educational settings.
connectionsExplanation: Avenue was developed as a part of Apple's educational initiatives in the 1980s. It was influenced by BASIC, which was widely used for educational purposes at the time. Avenue aimed to provide a more structured and object-oriented approach compared to BASIC, reflecting the evolving paradigms in programming education.
curious_fact: Avenue was one of the first languages to introduce object-oriented concepts in an educational context on the Apple II.

name: Averest
description: Averest is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax that aims to introduce beginners to programming concepts in an engaging way.
connectionsExplanation: Averest was created as an educational tool, drawing inspiration from popular scripting languages like Python and JavaScript. Its design focuses on simplicity and ease of learning, making it suitable for beginners. While it does not have a direct historical predecessor, it aligns with the paradigm of modern scripting languages that prioritize readability and accessibility.
curious_fact: Averest was named after Mount Everest to symbolize reaching new heights in programming education.

name: Axum
description: Axum is a modern, asynchronous web framework for Rust, designed to be fast, safe, and easy to use. It leverages Rust's powerful type system and async capabilities to provide a robust framework for building web applications.
connectionsExplanation: Axum is built on top of the Tokio runtime and the Hyper HTTP library, making it a natural evolution of Rust's ecosystem for asynchronous web development. It draws inspiration from other Rust frameworks like Actix-web but aims to provide a simpler and more intuitive API.

moons.name: Tower
moons.description: Tower is a collection of modular and reusable components for building asynchronous applications in Rust. It provides middleware, services, and other utilities that are used by Axum.

curious_fact: Axum was created to address the need for a more straightforward and idiomatic way to build web applications in Rust, leveraging the language's strengths in concurrency and safety.

name: Azure Bicep
description: Azure Bicep is a domain-specific language (DSL) used for deploying and managing Azure resources. It simplifies the creation of Azure Resource Manager (ARM) templates by providing a declarative syntax.
connectionsExplanation: Azure Bicep was introduced by Microsoft as a more concise and readable alternative to ARM templates. It compiles down to ARM JSON, allowing developers to leverage the full capabilities of Azure Resource Manager while using a more intuitive syntax.
curious_fact: Azure Bicep was designed to reduce the complexity of ARM templates by using a syntax similar to TypeScript.

name: Azure Resource Manager
description: Azure Resource Manager (ARM) is a deployment and management service for Azure. It provides a declarative way to deploy and manage Azure resources. ARM templates are JSON files that define the infrastructure and configuration for your Azure deployment.
connectionsExplanation: Azure Resource Manager is a core component of Microsoft's cloud infrastructure, evolving from earlier Azure management tools. It allows users to manage Azure resources as a set of related resources, simplifying deployment and management.
curious_fact: ARM templates can be versioned and shared, making infrastructure as code a reality in Azure.

name: A♯
description: A♯ is an esoteric programming language created in 2010. It is known for its unique syntax and minimalistic approach, making it challenging to read and write. Despite its complexity, A♯ has a dedicated community of enthusiasts who appreciate its innovative design.
connectionsExplanation: A♯ was inspired by the desire to create a language that challenges traditional programming paradigms. Its syntax and structure are designed to be as different as possible from mainstream languages, making it a unique entry in the esoteric programming language family.
curious_fact: A♯ was created by a single developer who aimed to push the boundaries of programming language design.

name: B
description: B is a minimalist programming language developed by Ken Thompson at Bell Labs in 1970. It was the precursor to the C programming language and was used to write the UNIX operating system. B is known for its simplicity and influence on later languages.
connectionsExplanation: B was developed by Ken Thompson as a stripped-down version of the BCPL language. It served as a stepping stone for the creation of C, which in turn influenced many modern programming languages. B's simplicity and efficiency made it a foundational language in the evolution of UNIX and the C language family.
curious_fact: B was so minimalistic that it lacked features like loops and recursion, relying instead on system calls and external tools.

name: B-Prolog
description: B-Prolog is a high-performance Prolog compiler and runtime system developed in Japan. It is known for its efficiency and is widely used in academia and research for logic programming tasks.
connectionsExplanation: B-Prolog evolved from earlier Prolog systems and is part of the Prolog family, which traces its roots back to the early 1970s. It is designed to be a modern, efficient implementation of Prolog, incorporating advancements in logic programming techniques.
curious_fact: B-Prolog supports tabling, a technique that helps manage redundant computations in logic programs, significantly improving performance.

name: B4X
description: B4X is a programming language designed for developing applications for Android, iOS, and desktop platforms. It is based on the BASIC language and provides a simple and intuitive syntax, making it accessible for beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: B4X is derived from the BASIC language, which has a long history dating back to the 1960s. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, making it a popular choice for hobbyists and small-scale developers. B4X extends the BASIC paradigm by adding support for modern mobile and desktop platforms, allowing developers to create cross-platform applications with ease.

moons.name: B4i

moons.name: B4J

curious_fact: B4X was created by Erel Erell, who also developed the popular BASIC compiler for Windows.

name: BACAIC

name: BAL

It was influenced by the need for simplicity and efficiency in handling business data, but its limited adoption suggests that it did not meet the evolving needs of the industry.

name: BANCStar

connectionsExplanation: BANCStar was developed internally by the Bank of America and was not publicly released. It is not directly related to any other widely known programming languages, but it shares some similarities with COBOL in terms of its focus on business and financial applications.
curious_fact: BANCStar was one of the first languages to incorporate security features directly into its syntax to protect financial data.

name: BASIC8
description: BASIC8 is a variant of the BASIC programming language designed for educational purposes in the early 1980s. It aimed to simplify programming concepts for beginners and was widely used in schools and colleges during that era.
connectionsExplanation: BASIC8 evolved from earlier versions of BASIC, which were developed in the late 1960s and early 1970s. It was part of a trend to create more accessible programming languages for non-specialists, following the success of Dartmouth BASIC.
curious_fact: BASIC8 introduced a simplified syntax that made it easier for beginners to learn programming concepts.

name: BASICA+
description: BASICA+ is an extended version of the BASIC programming language, designed to enhance its capabilities for educational and hobbyist use. It introduced additional features and optimizations to make BASIC more powerful and versatile.
connectionsExplanation: BASICA+ evolved from the original BASIC language, which was developed in the late 1960s. It aimed to provide a more robust environment for beginners and enthusiasts by incorporating advanced features while maintaining the simplicity of BASIC.
curious_fact: BASICA+ was one of the early attempts to modernize BASIC for the growing personal computer market in the 1980s.

name: BASIC
description: BASIC, an acronym for Beginner's All-purpose Symbolic Instruction Code, is a family of high-level programming languages designed to be easy to use for beginners. It was first developed in the mid-1960s at Dartmouth College by John G. Kemeny and Thomas E. Kurtz. BASIC has been influential in the development of personal computers and has been used in educational settings to teach programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: BASIC was developed at Dartmouth College in the mid-1960s by John G. Kemeny and Thomas E. Kurtz. It was one of the first high-level programming languages designed to be accessible to non-specialists. BASIC has influenced the development of personal computers and has been widely used in educational settings to introduce programming concepts.

moons.name: QuickBASIC
moons.description: QuickBASIC is an enhanced version of Microsoft BASIC that was released in 1985. It included a compiler and an integrated development environment (IDE) and was widely used for developing desktop applications.

moons.name: Visual Basic
moons.description: Visual Basic is an event-driven programming language from Microsoft that is derived from BASIC. It was first released in 1991 and is known for its graphical user interface (GUI) development capabilities.

curious_fact: BASIC was one of the first programming languages to be widely used in educational settings, helping to democratize access to programming.

name: BASIC-8
description: BASIC-8 is an extension of the original Dartmouth BASIC, designed to run on 8-bit microcomputers. It introduced several enhancements for better performance and compatibility with emerging hardware.
connectionsExplanation: BASIC-8 evolved from the original Dartmouth BASIC, which was one of the first high-level programming languages. It was specifically tailored to meet the needs of the growing personal computer market in the late 1970s, incorporating features that made it more efficient and user-friendly for 8-bit systems.
curious_fact: BASIC-8 was one of the first languages to support structured programming constructs on 8-bit machines.

name: BASIC-PLUS
description: BASIC-PLUS is an extended version of the original Dartmouth BASIC, designed to enhance its capabilities for business and scientific applications. It introduced more advanced features like structured programming elements and improved data handling.
connectionsExplanation: BASIC-PLUS evolved from the original Dartmouth BASIC, which was one of the first high-level programming languages. It aimed to provide more robust features for business and scientific computing, making it a significant step in the evolution of BASIC.

curious_fact: BASIC-PLUS was one of the first languages to introduce structured programming concepts to a wide audience.

name: BASIC09
description: BASIC09 is an extended version of the original Dartmouth BASIC, designed for the Motorola 68000 processor. It introduced structured programming features and was used in educational settings and small business applications.
connectionsExplanation: BASIC09 evolved from the original Dartmouth BASIC, which was one of the first high-level programming languages. It was designed to be more efficient and suitable for the newer Motorola 68000 architecture, making it a direct descendant of the BASIC family.
curious_fact: BASIC09 was one of the first BASIC dialects to support structured programming, introducing concepts like subroutines and modules.

name: BBC BASIC
description: BBC BASIC, developed by the British Broadcasting Corporation, is a dialect of the BASIC programming language. It was widely used in the UK during the 1980s and early 1990s, particularly on the BBC Microcomputer. It is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular for educational purposes.
connectionsExplanation: BBC BASIC evolved from earlier versions of BASIC and was influenced by Dartmouth BASIC. It was designed to be accessible to beginners and was widely used in schools and homes in the UK during its peak.
curious_fact: BBC BASIC was one of the first programming languages to be widely used in schools in the UK, helping to introduce generations to computer programming.

name: BBNLISP
description: BBNLISP, developed at Bolt, Beranek and Newman (BBN) in 1961, was one of the early dialects of Lisp. It was used in the development of ARPANET, a precursor to the modern internet. BBNLISP was known for its simplicity and efficiency, making it a popular choice for network programming.
connectionsExplanation: BBNLISP was developed at BBN and was used extensively in the ARPANET project. It is a direct descendant of the original Lisp language, which was developed by John McCarthy at MIT in 1958. BBNLISP's role in ARPANET development solidified its importance in early computer networking.
curious_fact: BBNLISP was used to implement the first email system on ARPANET.

name: BBj
description: BBj is a business-oriented programming language developed by BASIS International. It is primarily used for building business applications, particularly in the financial and retail sectors. BBj is known for its integration with the BASIS database and its ability to create Windows-based applications.
connectionsExplanation: BBj evolved from the BASIC-2000 language, which was developed by BASIS International in the 1980s. It is a direct descendant of BASIC-2000, designed to enhance its capabilities and provide more modern features for business application development.
curious_fact: BBj supports both 32-bit and 64-bit environments, allowing for compatibility with modern hardware.

name: BCPL
description: BCPL, short for Basic Combined Programming Language, is a high-level programming language developed by Martin Richards at the University of Cambridge. It was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of structured programming and influenced the design of later languages like C.
connectionsExplanation: BCPL is considered a pioneer in structured programming and was influential in the development of C. It was one of the first languages to introduce block-structured syntax and influenced the design of subsequent languages by providing a foundation for modern programming constructs.
curious_fact: BCPL was used to write the compiler for C, which in turn was used to write the compiler for BCPL, creating a self-hosting system.
example:

PRINTF("The sum is %d", a + b)

name: BCY
description: BCY is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not widely used outside of academic settings.
connectionsExplanation: BCY was created as a teaching tool to introduce basic programming concepts without the complexities of more advanced languages. It is not derived from any existing language but inspired by the simplicity of early BASIC dialects.
curious_fact: BCY was developed by a team of educators aiming to create a language that could be learned in a single semester.

name: BEFLIX
description: BEFLIX is an esoteric programming language inspired by Befunge, designed to challenge programmers with its unique two-dimensional instruction set. It combines elements of Befunge with additional features to create a more complex and versatile language.
connectionsExplanation: BEFLIX is inspired by Befunge, an esoteric programming language created in 1993. Like Befunge, BEFLIX uses a two-dimensional grid of commands, but it introduces new instructions and a more structured approach to programming, making it a unique derivative in the esoteric language family.
curious_fact: BEFLIX was created as a modern twist on Befunge, incorporating elements from other esoteric languages to offer a fresh challenge to programmers.

name: BETA
description: BETA is a programming language developed at the Royal Institute of Technology (KTH) in Stockholm, Sweden. It was designed to be a modern, object-oriented language with a focus on simplicity and safety. BETA introduced several innovative features such as automatic memory management and a strong type system.
connectionsExplanation: BETA was developed in the 1970s as an attempt to create a more modern and safer programming language than the existing ones. It was influenced by the object-oriented paradigm and aimed to address some of the limitations of languages like Simula and Smalltalk.
curious_fact: BETA was one of the first languages to introduce automatic memory management and a strong type system, which were later adopted by many other languages.
example: PROGRAM HelloWorld; IMPORT Out; VAR x: INTEGER; BEGIN x := 42; Out.Int(x, 0); Out.Ln END HelloWorld.

name: BKEngine
description: BKEngine is a 3D game engine developed in Germany, known for its modular architecture and support for various programming languages. It is particularly popular among indie developers and hobbyists.
connectionsExplanation: BKEngine is a relatively niche game engine that evolved from earlier game development tools and middleware. Its modular design allows developers to integrate various components, making it a unique choice for those seeking flexibility in game development.
