connectionsExplanation: Caché ObjectScript evolved from earlier versions of ObjectScript used in InterSystems' products. It is closely tied to the Caché database system, which provides a robust environment for developing and deploying applications.
curious_fact: Caché ObjectScript is known for its integration with the Caché database, allowing for seamless data manipulation and retrieval.

name: Cadence SKILL
description: Cadence SKILL is a scripting language developed by Cadence Design Systems for customizing and automating tasks within their electronic design automation (EDA) tools. It is primarily used in the semiconductor industry for chip design and verification.
connectionsExplanation: Cadence SKILL evolved from the need to automate repetitive tasks in the complex workflow of semiconductor design. It is built on top of the Lisp programming language, inheriting its functional programming paradigms and dynamic typing. This makes SKILL well-suited for the intricate and data-intensive tasks involved in EDA.
curious_fact: SKILL is named after the acronym for 'Scripted Knowledge Integration Language'.

name: CafeOBJ
description: CafeOBJ is a reflective logic programming language designed for algebraic specification and verification. It is particularly suited for formal methods and software engineering.
connectionsExplanation: CafeOBJ evolved from OBJ3, which was itself a descendant of the OBJ language. OBJ3 was developed at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, and CafeOBJ was later developed at the National Institute of Informatics in Japan. CafeOBJ extends the capabilities of OBJ3 by adding reflective features and a more powerful specification language.
curious_fact: CafeOBJ supports reflective programming, allowing programs to inspect and modify their own structure and behavior.
example:
module HelloWorld is
  op hello : -> .
  eq hello() = println("Hello, World!").
endm

name: Caml
description: Caml is a family of programming languages developed in France, primarily known for its strong static typing and functional programming features. It has been influential in the development of other languages like OCaml and F#.
connectionsExplanation: Caml was developed at the INRIA (Institut National de Recherche en Informatique et en Automatique) in France. It was designed to be a practical and efficient language for both research and industrial applications, drawing inspiration from functional programming paradigms.

moons.name: OCaml
moons.description: OCaml is a dialect of Caml that emphasizes both functional and imperative programming. It is widely used in industry and academia for its robustness and performance.

moons.name: F#
moons.description: F# is a dialect of Caml developed by Microsoft. It integrates well with the .NET framework and supports both functional and imperative programming styles.

curious_fact: Caml was one of the first languages to popularize the use of polymorphic types in a practical setting.
example: let rec factorial n = if n = 0 then 1 else n * factorial (n - 1);;

name: CamlLight
description: CamlLight is a lightweight subset of OCaml, designed to be more accessible for beginners and to integrate well with other languages. It maintains the functional programming paradigm of OCaml but with a simpler syntax and fewer advanced features.
connectionsExplanation: CamlLight evolved from OCaml, which itself is a descendant of the ML family of programming languages. OCaml was developed in the early 1990s as a more practical and efficient version of ML, and CamlLight was created later to make OCaml more approachable for new users and to facilitate interoperability with other languages.
curious_fact: CamlLight was developed to make OCaml more accessible, particularly for students and those new to functional programming.
example:
let rec factorial n = if n = 0 then 1 else n * factorial (n - 1);;
factorial 5;;

name: Caméléon
description: Caméléon is a fictional programming language designed to mimic the adaptability of a chameleon, allowing it to change its syntax and semantics based on the context in which it is used. It was created as an academic exercise to explore the concept of context-sensitive programming languages.
connectionsExplanation: Caméléon was inspired by the idea of creating a language that could adapt to different programming paradigms, similar to how a chameleon changes its color to blend into its environment. It is not based on any existing language but draws inspiration from the principles of context-sensitive languages and dynamic typing.
curious_fact: Caméléon is one of the few languages that can change its syntax and semantics on the fly, making it highly adaptable but also challenging to learn and debug.

name: Candle
description: Candle is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid application development. It emphasizes simplicity and ease of use, making it accessible to beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers. Candle is particularly well-suited for web development and automation tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Candle was inspired by the simplicity and readability of Python, aiming to provide a more intuitive syntax and a more robust set of features. Its development began as an internal project at a tech startup, focusing on creating a language that could streamline the development process and improve productivity.

moons.name: Flame
moons.description: A web framework built on top of Candle, designed to simplify the creation of dynamic web applications.

moons.name: Wick
moons.description: A library for automating repetitive tasks and integrating with various APIs.

curious_fact: Candle was named after the wax candle, symbolizing the language's ability to illuminate and guide developers through complex projects.

name: Cangjie
description: Cangjie is a Chinese input method that allows users to type Chinese characters by selecting components. It was developed in Taiwan and is widely used in Chinese-speaking regions. Unlike phonetic input methods, Cangjie focuses on the visual structure of characters.
connectionsExplanation: Cangjie was developed in the 1980s as a response to the need for a more efficient way to input Chinese characters on computers. It is not directly derived from any programming language but is a significant tool in the realm of Chinese computing and digital communication.
curious_fact: Cangjie is named after the ancient Chinese divination method, which inspired its component-based approach.
example: Cangjie input method allows users to type Chinese characters by selecting components.

name: Carbon
description: Carbon is a modern systems programming language designed to be fast, safe, and easy to use. It aims to provide the performance of C with the safety and expressiveness of Swift.
connectionsExplanation: Carbon was developed by Apple as a successor to Swift for systems programming tasks. It inherits Swift's safety features and performance goals while aiming to be more compatible with C and C++ ecosystems.
curious_fact: Carbon was initially developed as a research project and is not yet widely adopted outside of Apple.

name: Cascading Style Sheets
description: Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) is a stylesheet language used to describe the presentation of a document written in HTML or XML. It is a cornerstone technology of the World Wide Web, alongside HTML and JavaScript.
connectionsExplanation: CSS was developed by Håkon Wium Lie and Bert Bos at the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) and Netscape Communications. It was first proposed in 1994 and became a W3C recommendation in 1996. CSS is designed to enable the separation of content and presentation, making it easier to manage and maintain web designs.
curious_fact: CSS is responsible for the visual styling of web pages, allowing developers to control layout, colors, fonts, and other presentation aspects.
example: body { background-color: lightblue; } h1 { color: navy; margin-left: 20px; }

name: CasioBASIC
description: CasioBASIC is a dialect of BASIC designed for use on Casio calculators, particularly the fx-7000G and fx-8000G series. It is a simple, easy-to-learn language that allows users to write programs directly on the calculator's built-in keyboard.
connectionsExplanation: CasioBASIC is a specialized dialect of BASIC, which itself evolved from FORTRAN and COBOL in the 1950s. It was developed by Casio to provide a user-friendly programming environment for their graphing calculators, allowing students and educators to perform complex calculations and create custom programs.
curious_fact: CasioBASIC was one of the first programming languages designed specifically for calculators, making it accessible to a wide audience.

name: Cat
description: Cat is an esoteric programming language created by Chris Pressey in 2016. It is designed to be difficult to understand and write, with syntax inspired by the feline language. Cat is not intended for practical use but rather as a challenge for programmers.
connectionsExplanation: Cat is an esoteric language created by Chris Pressey in 2016. It is inspired by the feline language and is designed to be difficult to understand and write. Cat does not have a direct lineage from other programming languages but is part of the esoteric programming language family.
curious_fact: Cat is one of the few programming languages that uses the feline language as inspiration.

name: Categorical Programming Language
description: Categorical Programming Language is a modern programming language designed to support categorical logic and type theory. It aims to provide a robust framework for developing applications that require advanced mathematical reasoning and formal verification.
connectionsExplanation: Categorical Programming Language draws inspiration from the principles of category theory, which is a branch of mathematics that deals with the study of structures and relationships between mathematical objects. This language is part of a growing trend in programming languages that leverage mathematical concepts to enhance expressiveness and correctness.
curious_fact: Categorical Programming Language was developed by a team of mathematicians and computer scientists who sought to bridge the gap between theoretical mathematics and practical programming.

name: Catrobat
description: Catrobat is a visual programming language designed for children and beginners. It allows users to create programs and games using a graphical interface, making it accessible for those new to programming.
connectionsExplanation: Catrobat was developed as part of the EU-funded project 'Catrobat - A Platform for Creative Learning'. It is inspired by Scratch but focuses on mobile devices, offering a more intuitive interface and additional features tailored for mobile environments.

moons.name: Catroid
moons.description: Catroid is an Android app that allows users to create and share animated stories and games using Catrobat.

curious_fact: Catrobat was created by the University of Regensburg and the Fraunhofer Society.
example:
create sprite "MySprite"
set x to 0
set y to 0
repeat 10
  change x by 10
  wait 0.5 seconds
end

name: Cayenne
description: Cayenne is a domain-specific language (DSL) designed for embedded systems programming, particularly for microcontrollers. It is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among hobbyists and small-scale developers.
connectionsExplanation: Cayenne was developed to address the need for a more accessible and efficient way to program microcontrollers. It draws inspiration from C and assembly languages but abstracts away many of the complexities, making it easier for beginners to get started with embedded systems programming.
curious_fact: Cayenne was named after the Cayenne pepper to symbolize its spiciness in terms of complexity compared to other languages.

name: Cecil
description: Cecil is a procedural programming language developed in the early 1970s. It was designed to be a high-level language with a syntax similar to assembly language, aiming to bridge the gap between high-level and low-level programming. Cecil is known for its use in educational settings and for its influence on later languages.
connectionsExplanation: Cecil was developed at the University of California, Berkeley, in the early 1970s. It was influenced by the need for a language that could be used to teach computer science concepts while still allowing for low-level operations. Cecil's design was inspired by the structure of assembly language, making it a unique language in its time.
curious_fact: Cecil was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of structured programming in an educational context.

name: Cel
description: Cel is a modern, high-level programming language designed for simplicity and ease of use. It emphasizes readability and concise syntax, making it accessible to beginners while still being powerful enough for complex applications.
connectionsExplanation: Cel was inspired by the simplicity and elegance of Python, aiming to provide a more intuitive syntax and a cleaner design. It draws from the paradigms of functional programming while maintaining a straightforward imperative style.
curious_fact: Cel introduced a novel feature called 'auto-inference', which automatically deduces variable types based on their initial values, reducing the need for explicit type declarations.

name: Cerner CCL
description: Cerner CCL (Cerner Command Language) is a procedural programming language developed by Cerner Corporation for use in their healthcare information systems. It is primarily used for scripting and automation within Cerner's electronic health record (EHR) systems.
connectionsExplanation: Cerner CCL is a specialized language developed by Cerner Corporation for internal use in their healthcare software. It is not derived from any mainstream programming language but is designed to integrate seamlessly with Cerner's EHR systems, allowing for customization and automation of clinical workflows.
curious_fact: Cerner CCL is one of the few programming languages specifically designed for healthcare IT, reflecting the unique needs of the medical industry.

name: Ceylon
description: Ceylon is a modern, statically typed programming language designed for the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) and the Common Language Runtime (CLR). It emphasizes expressiveness, safety, and modularity, aiming to provide a more elegant and concise syntax compared to Java.
connectionsExplanation: Ceylon was developed by Red Hat and is influenced by Java, Scala, and other modern languages. It was designed to address some of the limitations of Java, such as verbosity and complex type systems, while maintaining compatibility with existing Java libraries and tools.

moons.name: Ceylon IDE
moons.description: An integrated development environment for Ceylon.

moons.name: Ceylon SDK
moons.description: A software development kit providing libraries and tools for Ceylon development.

curious_fact: Ceylon's syntax is designed to be readable and concise, with a focus on reducing boilerplate code.

name: Cg
description: Cg is a high-level shading language developed by NVIDIA for programming graphics hardware. It allows developers to write shader programs in a C-like syntax, making it easier to create complex visual effects in real-time graphics applications.
connectionsExplanation: Cg was developed by NVIDIA as a high-level shading language to simplify the process of writing shader programs for graphics hardware. It was designed to be more accessible to programmers familiar with C and C++ by providing a C-like syntax for shader programming. Cg evolved from earlier shading languages and was intended to be a more user-friendly alternative to low-level assembly languages used for graphics programming.
curious_fact: Cg was deprecated by NVIDIA in 2010 in favor of GLSL and HLSL, but it remains a significant part of graphics programming history.
example: void main(float4 position : POSITION, out float4 oPosition : POSITION, uniform float4x4 modelViewProj) { oPosition = mul(modelViewProj, position); }

name: Ch
description: Ch is a C/C++ interpreter and development environment designed to simplify the process of writing, debugging, and running C/C++ programs. It provides a user-friendly interface and additional features like syntax highlighting and a built-in debugger.
connectionsExplanation: Ch is an extension of C/C++ and is designed to be compatible with existing C/C++ code. It was developed to make C/C++ more accessible to beginners and to provide a more interactive development experience.
curious_fact: Ch introduced the concept of a C/C++ interpreter, which was relatively uncommon at the time.

name: Chaos
description: Chaos is an esoteric programming language created by Jon Blow in 1982. It is known for its minimalistic design and unconventional syntax, making it challenging to understand and use. Despite its complexity, Chaos has a dedicated following among programming enthusiasts.
connectionsExplanation: Chaos is an esoteric language that emerged in the early 1980s, a period when many such languages were created. It does not have a direct lineage from mainstream languages but shares a common interest in pushing the boundaries of programming language design.
curious_fact: Chaos was one of the first esoteric programming languages to gain recognition, inspiring many others in the genre.

name: Chapel
description: Chapel is a parallel programming language designed for high-performance computing. It aims to provide a simple and intuitive syntax for expressing parallelism and concurrency, making it easier to write efficient code for modern multi-core and distributed systems.
connectionsExplanation: Chapel was developed at Cray Inc. as part of their effort to create a language that could effectively utilize the parallel processing capabilities of modern supercomputers. It draws inspiration from various paradigms, including functional programming and data-parallelism, to provide a flexible and powerful tool for scientific computing and high-performance applications.

moons.name: Chapel Compiler
moons.description: The official compiler for Chapel, enabling the translation of Chapel code into executable binaries.

moons.name: Chapel Standard Library
moons.description: A comprehensive library providing a wide range of functionalities, from basic data structures to advanced parallel algorithms.

curious_fact: Chapel was named after the chapel at Cray Inc., where it was initially developed.

name: Charity
description: Charity is a programming language designed for writing concurrent and distributed programs. It is based on the Charity Calculus, which is a typed lambda calculus with a unique approach to concurrency. Charity is known for its strong typing and its ability to handle complex concurrent systems.
connectionsExplanation: Charity is based on the Charity Calculus, which is a typed lambda calculus. It is not directly derived from any mainstream programming language but shares conceptual roots with functional programming paradigms. The language was developed to address the challenges of concurrent programming, particularly in distributed systems.
curious_fact: Charity was developed by Jeremy Gibbons and his team at the University of Oxford.
example: let rec map f [] = [] | map f (x::xs) = (f x) :: (map f xs)

name: Charm
description: Charm is a domain-specific programming language designed for parallel programming in scientific computing. It is particularly suited for applications in physics and chemistry, offering a high-level abstraction for parallel algorithms.
connectionsExplanation: Charm is inspired by the Message Passing Interface (MPI) and is designed to simplify the development of parallel applications. It abstracts away many of the complexities of parallel programming, making it accessible to scientists and researchers who may not have extensive experience in parallel computing.
curious_fact: Charm was developed at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and is named after the charm in the Harry Potter series, symbolizing its ability to simplify complex programming tasks.

name: Charm++
description: Charm++ is a parallel object-oriented programming language designed for high-performance computing. It is particularly suited for developing scalable parallel applications on distributed memory systems.
connectionsExplanation: Charm++ evolved from Charm, an earlier parallel programming system. It extends the capabilities of Charm by incorporating object-oriented principles, making it more accessible and flexible for developers.
curious_fact: Charm++ was developed at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and is widely used in scientific computing and simulations.

name: ChatScript
description: ChatScript is a scripting language designed for natural language processing and chatbot development. It is known for its ability to handle complex conversational patterns and is widely used in creating intelligent chatbots.
connectionsExplanation: ChatScript was developed by Bruce Wilcox in 2001 and is not directly derived from any other language. It is designed to handle natural language processing tasks, making it unique in its domain.
curious_fact: ChatScript is one of the earliest and most influential languages for building chatbots, predating many modern AI chatbot frameworks.

name: ChavaScript
description: ChavaScript is a fictional programming language inspired by Java and JavaScript, designed to explore the intersection of object-oriented and functional programming paradigms. It aims to provide a modern, concise syntax while maintaining the robustness and scalability of Java.
connectionsExplanation: ChavaScript was created as a thought experiment to bridge the gap between Java's strong typing and JavaScript's dynamic nature. It draws inspiration from Java's class-based structure and JavaScript's functional programming capabilities, aiming to offer developers a more versatile and efficient language.
curious_fact: ChavaScript introduced the concept of 'dynamic classes' which allow for runtime class modifications, a unique feature not found in traditional Java or JavaScript.
example: class HelloWorld { static main() { print("Hello, World!"); } }

name: CheckedC
description: CheckedC is an extension of the C programming language that adds optional type safety features to help prevent common programming errors such as buffer overflows and null pointer dereferences.
connectionsExplanation: CheckedC was developed by Microsoft as an extension to C to enhance its safety features. It builds upon the existing C language, adding annotations to variables and function parameters to enforce type safety checks at compile time.
curious_fact: CheckedC was designed to be a drop-in extension, allowing existing C codebases to be gradually enhanced with safety features.
example: int main() { int arr[5]; arr[5] = 0; // This would cause a compile-time error in CheckedC }

name: ChezScheme
description: ChezScheme is a highly efficient implementation of the Scheme programming language, known for its performance and robustness. It is widely used in academic settings and for research purposes.
connectionsExplanation: ChezScheme is a direct descendant of the Scheme language, which was developed in the late 1970s. It is part of the Lisp/Scheme family, which traces its roots back to the analytical-engine-order-code through the pioneering work of John McCarthy and others in the 1950s.
curious_fact: ChezScheme is one of the fastest Scheme implementations, often used in benchmarking and performance comparisons.

name: ChibiScheme
description: ChibiScheme is a minimalist Scheme implementation written in C. It is designed to be small, fast, and easy to understand, making it suitable for educational purposes and embedded systems.
connectionsExplanation: ChibiScheme is a direct descendant of the Scheme programming language, which was developed in the late 1970s. It inherits the functional programming paradigm and Lisp-like syntax from its ancestors, such as Lisp and ALGOL, which were pioneering languages in the 1950s and 1960s.
curious_fact: ChibiScheme is named after the Chibi character from the anime series 'Chibi-Robo'.

name: ChineseBASIC
description: ChineseBASIC is a variant of the BASIC programming language developed in China. It was designed to introduce programming concepts to Chinese-speaking students and professionals. The language uses simplified Chinese characters for keywords, making it accessible to those with a knowledge of the language.
connectionsExplanation: ChineseBASIC was developed as a localized version of BASIC to cater to the Chinese market. It aimed to simplify programming education by using familiar Chinese characters, reflecting the country's emphasis on language localization in technology.
curious_fact: ChineseBASIC was one of the first programming languages to use Chinese characters, making it a pioneering effort in language localization.

name: ChinesePython
description: ChinesePython is a fictional programming language designed to incorporate Chinese characters into its syntax, aiming to make coding more accessible to Chinese speakers. It is a playful concept rather than a practical language.
connectionsExplanation: ChinesePython is inspired by Python, a widely-used high-level programming language. It introduces Chinese characters into Python's syntax, making it a unique and experimental language.
curious_fact: ChinesePython was created as a thought experiment to explore the integration of non-Latin scripts into programming languages.

name: ChipmunkBasic
description: ChipmunkBasic is a BASIC dialect designed for educational purposes, particularly for children. It was created to be simple and easy to understand, making it an ideal language for beginners to learn programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: ChipmunkBasic is a derivative of the classic BASIC language, which was developed in the late 1960s. It inherits the simplicity and educational focus of BASIC, aiming to make programming accessible to young learners.
curious_fact: ChipmunkBasic was developed to be user-friendly and visually appealing, featuring a graphical interface that resembles a chipmunk, hence its name.

name: ChucK
description: ChucK is a programming language for sound synthesis and composition. It is designed to facilitate real-time audio programming and is particularly popular in academic and research settings for its expressive and flexible syntax.
connectionsExplanation: ChucK was developed at Princeton University by Perry R. Cook and Ge Wang. It is inspired by the concept of time-based programming, allowing for precise control over audio events. ChucK's design is rooted in the need for a language that could handle the complexities of real-time audio processing more intuitively than existing languages.
curious_fact: ChucK uses a 'now' keyword to represent the current time, enabling precise timing in audio synthesis.

name: Church
description: Church is a theoretical programming language developed by Alonzo Church in the 1930s. It is based on lambda calculus and is foundational in the study of computability theory. Despite its theoretical nature, it has influenced the development of functional programming languages.
connectionsExplanation: Church's lambda calculus is a formal system in mathematical logic for expressing computation based on function abstraction and application. It laid the groundwork for functional programming languages and is considered a precursor to modern programming paradigms.
curious_fact: Church numerals are a way of representing natural numbers using only functions.
example: let zero = \f.\x. x; let one = \f.\x. f x; let two = \f.\x. f (f x);

name: CiMPLE
description: CiMPLE is a domain-specific language (DSL) designed for managing and automating IT infrastructure. It is particularly suited for configuration management and system administration tasks.
connectionsExplanation: CiMPLE was developed as a modern alternative to traditional scripting languages for IT management. It draws inspiration from imperative programming paradigms and is designed to be more readable and maintainable for system administrators.
curious_fact: CiMPLE was created by the OpenPegasus project to simplify the management of complex IT environments.

name: Ciao
description: Ciao is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It is a simple, interpreted language that resembles a mix of BASIC and Python, with a focus on readability and ease of learning.
connectionsExplanation: Ciao was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts. It draws inspiration from popular languages like BASIC and Python, aiming to provide a gentle learning curve without the complexities of more advanced languages.
curious_fact: Ciao was developed by a team of educators to make programming accessible to children.

name: Cilk
description: Cilk is a data-parallel programming language designed to simplify the process of writing parallel programs. It was developed at MIT to make it easier for programmers to write efficient parallel code without requiring detailed knowledge of parallel hardware.
connectionsExplanation: Cilk was developed at MIT as an extension of C to support parallel programming. It introduced constructs that allowed for easy parallelization of loops and recursive functions, making it a pioneering language in the field of parallel computing.
curious_fact: Cilk was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'fork-join' parallelism, which is now a common pattern in parallel programming.
example: int fib(int n) { if (n < 2) return n; return fib(n-1) + fib(n-2); } int main() { int result = fib(10); return 0; }

name: CilkPlus
description: CilkPlus is an extension of the C and C++ programming languages designed to support parallel programming. It was developed by Intel to simplify the process of writing parallel code for multi-core processors.
connectionsExplanation: CilkPlus extends C and C++, inheriting their paradigms and syntax. It was developed by Intel to address the challenges of parallel programming, aiming to make it easier for developers to write efficient parallel code for multi-core processors.
curious_fact: CilkPlus introduced the 'cilk_for' construct to simplify parallel loops.

name: Cilk++
description: Cilk++ is an extension of C++ designed to support parallel programming. It was developed by MIT to simplify the process of writing parallel code for multi-core processors.
connectionsExplanation: Cilk++ extends C++ and is inspired by the Cilk programming language, which was developed at MIT to support parallel programming. Cilk++ aims to provide similar capabilities but within the C++ language, making it easier for C++ developers to write parallel code.
curious_fact: Cilk++ was one of the first attempts to bring parallel programming constructs directly into a mainstream language.
example: int fib(int n) { if (n < 2) return n; return fib(n-1) + fib(n-2); } int main() { int result = cilk_spawn fib(10); cilk_sync; return result; }

name: Cirq
description: Cirq is an open-source Python library for writing, manipulating, and optimizing quantum circuits and running them on quantum computers or simulators.
connectionsExplanation: Cirq was developed by Google Quantum AI Lab and is part of the broader ecosystem of quantum computing tools. It builds upon Python's extensive scientific computing libraries to provide a user-friendly interface for quantum algorithm development.
curious_fact: Cirq supports both hardware-accelerated and pure Python simulators, making it versatile for both research and educational purposes.
example:
import cirq
qubit = cirq.GridQubit(0, 0)
circuit = cirq.Circuit(cirq.H(qubit), cirq.measure(qubit))
print(circuit)

name: Citrine
description: Citrine is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid development and ease of use. It emphasizes simplicity and readability, making it accessible to beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: Citrine was developed as a successor to the popular language Blueberry, aiming to address some of its limitations and introduce new features. It draws inspiration from both functional and object-oriented paradigms, making it a versatile choice for various programming tasks.

moons.name: Citrine Framework
moons.description: A comprehensive framework for building web applications with Citrine.

moons.name: Citrine Lib
moons.description: A collection of libraries for common programming tasks in Citrine.

curious_fact: Citrine was named after the gemstone due to its bright and clear syntax.

name: Claire
description: Claire is a modern, high-level programming language designed for simplicity and ease of use. It emphasizes readability and developer productivity, making it an ideal choice for beginners and experienced programmers alike.
connectionsExplanation: Claire was developed as a response to the growing demand for more accessible programming languages. It draws inspiration from Python and JavaScript, aiming to combine their strengths while addressing common pain points in modern software development.
curious_fact: Claire was created by a team of educators and software developers who wanted to make programming more approachable for students and hobbyists.

name: Clarion
description: Clarion is a high-level, database-centric programming language designed for rapid application development. It is known for its ease of use and efficient handling of database operations.
connectionsExplanation: Clarion was developed by SoftVelocity (formerly Clarion Software) in 1984. It is a descendant of earlier database management systems and was designed to simplify the development of database applications. Clarion's focus on database operations and ease of use has made it popular in certain niches, particularly in the business and accounting sectors.

moons.name: Clarion 2000
moons.description: An updated version of Clarion with improved features and support for modern development environments.

moons.name: Clarion 2004
moons.description: Further enhancements to the Clarion platform, including better integration with Windows and additional tools.

curious_fact: Clarion was one of the first languages to popularize the concept of 'rapid application development' (RAD) in the database management field.
example:
PROGRAM HelloWorld
  DISPLAY 'Hello, World!' TO SCREEN
END

name: Clascal
description: Clascal is a programming language developed by Niklaus Wirth as a successor to Pascal. It aimed to improve Pascal's efficiency and add new features suitable for systems programming. Clascal was never widely adopted, but it influenced the development of later languages.
connectionsExplanation: Clascal evolved from Pascal, which was developed by Niklaus Wirth in the late 1960s. Wirth sought to address limitations in Pascal, particularly in its handling of complex data structures and system-level programming, leading to the creation of Clascal.
curious_fact: Clascal was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'type extension', allowing for more flexible data structures.
example: program HelloWorld; begin writeln('Hello, World!'); end.

name: Clean
description: Clean is a purely functional programming language developed at the University of Nijmegen in the Netherlands. It emphasizes immutability and lazy evaluation, making it suitable for applications requiring high reliability and performance.
connectionsExplanation: Clean evolved from the functional programming paradigm, specifically influenced by Haskell and Miranda. It was designed to address the limitations of earlier functional languages by providing a more efficient and practical implementation.
curious_fact: Clean was one of the first languages to implement lazy evaluation efficiently, significantly impacting the development of modern functional programming languages.
example:
module Main where
main = putLine "Hello World"

name: Clojure
description: Clojure is a dialect of the Lisp programming language, designed to be a general-purpose language on the Java platform. It emphasizes code as data, immutability, and functional programming.
connectionsExplanation: Clojure was created by Rich Hickey as a modern dialect of Lisp, aiming to leverage the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) for performance and interoperability. It draws from the Lisp family, particularly Scheme, and integrates seamlessly with Java libraries.

moons.name: clojure.core
moons.description: The core library of Clojure, providing essential functions and macros.

moons.name: clojure.spec
moons.description: A library for specifying data and function contracts.

curious_fact: Clojure is homoiconic, meaning its code is represented in the same data structures that it uses to represent data.

name: ClojureScript
description: ClojureScript is a dialect of the Clojure programming language that compiles to JavaScript. It allows developers to use the same language for both the server and client sides of web applications, leveraging the power of functional programming.
connectionsExplanation: ClojureScript is a direct descendant of Clojure, which itself is a dialect of Lisp. ClojureScript extends Clojure's capabilities by compiling to JavaScript, enabling it to run in web browsers. This makes it a powerful tool for building modern web applications with the same language used on the server.

moons.name: Reagent
moons.description: A simple, easy, and powerful way to create UI components in ClojureScript.

moons.name: Om
moons.description: A ClojureScript interface to Facebook's React.

curious_fact: ClojureScript is known for its seamless integration with JavaScript libraries and its ability to leverage the full power of the JVM ecosystem.

name: ClosetoMetal
description: ClosetoMetal is a fictional programming language designed to bridge the gap between high-level abstractions and low-level hardware control. It aims to provide developers with the flexibility of high-level languages while allowing fine-grained access to system resources.
connectionsExplanation: ClosetoMetal was inspired by the need for a language that could efficiently utilize modern hardware capabilities without sacrificing ease of use. It draws inspiration from languages like C and Rust, which emphasize performance and control over system resources.
curious_fact: ClosetoMetal was created by a team of former hardware engineers who wanted to simplify the process of writing low-level system code.

name: Cluster
description: Cluster is a parallel programming language designed for high-performance computing. It allows developers to write programs that can run on multiple processors or computers simultaneously, making it ideal for scientific and engineering applications.
connectionsExplanation: Cluster was developed as an extension of the C programming language, incorporating features that facilitate parallel processing. It builds upon the foundational concepts of C, adding constructs specifically tailored for distributed computing environments.
curious_fact: Cluster was one of the first languages to introduce explicit parallel constructs into mainstream programming.

name: Cobra
description: Cobra is a statically typed, object-oriented programming language designed to be easy to read and write. It was created by Charles E. H. T. Cox III and aims to provide a balance between simplicity and power. Cobra is known for its clean syntax and its ability to interoperate with C#.
connectionsExplanation: Cobra was designed to be a modern, clean alternative to C# and Java. It draws inspiration from Python's readability and C#'s performance, aiming to provide a language that is both easy to use and efficient. Cobra's syntax is designed to be intuitive, with a focus on reducing boilerplate code and improving developer productivity.

moons.name: Cobra.NET
moons.description: A .NET implementation of Cobra, allowing it to run on the .NET framework.

curious_fact: Cobra was named after the cobra snake, symbolizing its ability to strike quickly and efficiently.

class Hello
    def main
        print "Hello, world!"

name: CoffeeScript
description: CoffeeScript is a programming language that transpiles to JavaScript. It aims to expose the good parts of JavaScript in a simple way, while adding syntactic sugar inspired by Ruby, Python, and Haskell.
connectionsExplanation: CoffeeScript was created by Jeremy Ashkenas as a way to make JavaScript more readable and enjoyable to write. It was influenced by Ruby's syntax and Python's simplicity, aiming to provide a more elegant and concise way to write JavaScript code.

moons.name: Decaffeinate
moons.description: A tool that converts CoffeeScript to JavaScript.

curious_fact: CoffeeScript was named after the author's love for coffee and his desire to make JavaScript more enjoyable.

name: ColdFusion Markup Language
description: ColdFusion Markup Language (CFML) is a server-side scripting language used for building dynamic web applications. It was developed by Allaire Corporation and later acquired by Adobe Systems. CFML is known for its ease of use and integration with databases, making it popular in the early days of web development.
connectionsExplanation: CFML evolved from the need to simplify web application development by combining HTML with server-side scripting. It was influenced by languages like JavaScript and Perl, but its unique syntax and features set it apart. CFML's popularity peaked in the late 1990s and early 2000s, but has since declined due to the rise of more modern frameworks and languages.

moons.name: Railo
moons.description: An open-source CFML server and framework.

moons.name: Lucee
moons.description: An open-source CFML server and framework.

curious_fact: CFML was one of the first languages to popularize the concept of server-side scripting for web development.

name: ColonyFramework
description: ColonyFramework is a modern, open-source framework designed for building distributed applications. It emphasizes scalability, modularity, and ease of use, making it suitable for both small-scale projects and large-scale enterprise solutions.
connectionsExplanation: ColonyFramework was inspired by the need for a more efficient and flexible approach to distributed computing. It draws from the principles of microservices architecture and incorporates elements from popular frameworks like Spring and Django to provide a robust development environment.

moons.name: ColonyWeb
moons.description: A web development module for ColonyFramework, providing tools for building dynamic web applications.

moons.name: ColonyData
moons.description: A data management module for handling large datasets and providing efficient data processing capabilities.

curious_fact: ColonyFramework was developed by a team of researchers at MIT to address the challenges of modern distributed systems.

name: ColorBASIC
description: ColorBASIC is an enhanced version of Microsoft BASIC designed for the Radio Shack TRS-80 Color Computer. It introduced color graphics capabilities and was widely used in educational settings during the 1980s.
connectionsExplanation: ColorBASIC evolved from the original Microsoft BASIC, which was developed in the late 1960s. It was specifically tailored for the TRS-80 Color Computer, incorporating color graphics features that were not present in earlier BASIC versions.
curious_fact: ColorBASIC was one of the first programming languages to support color graphics on home computers.

name: Comet
description: Comet is a futuristic programming language designed for high-performance computing and artificial intelligence applications. It features a unique syntax inspired by celestial mechanics, making it stand out in the programming landscape.
connectionsExplanation: Comet was developed as a research project at a leading university in the United States. Its design draws inspiration from the principles of celestial mechanics, aiming to optimize performance and efficiency in complex computations.
curious_fact: Comet's syntax includes celestial-themed keywords like 'orbit', 'galaxy', and 'star', reflecting its inspiration from space.

name: Comfy
description: Comfy is a fictional programming language designed for ease of use and rapid development. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax and features aimed at simplifying complex tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Comfy was created as a modern alternative to existing scripting languages, aiming to provide a more intuitive and user-friendly experience. While it draws inspiration from languages like Python and JavaScript, it introduces its own set of features and syntax to cater to a specific niche market.
curious_fact: Comfy was developed in a weekend hackathon by a group of language enthusiasts.

name: Commodore BASIC
description: Commodore BASIC is a dialect of the BASIC programming language developed by Commodore Business Machines for their 8-bit computers. It was widely used in the 1980s and is known for its simplicity and ease of use on home computers like the PET, VIC-20, and Commodore 64.
connectionsExplanation: Commodore BASIC evolved from Dartmouth BASIC, which was one of the earliest versions of BASIC. It was designed to be user-friendly and accessible, making it popular among hobbyists and beginners in the early days of personal computing.
curious_fact: Commodore BASIC was one of the first programming languages to be widely available on home computers, helping to popularize personal computing in the 1980s.

name: Common Intermediate Language
description: Common Intermediate Language (CIL) is a low-level assembly-like language used by the .NET framework. It is a platform-independent language that is compiled to bytecode and executed by the Common Language Runtime (CLR). CIL is designed to be a common language for all .NET languages, allowing for interoperability and code sharing.
connectionsExplanation: CIL was developed by Microsoft as part of the .NET initiative to create a platform-independent language that could be used by multiple programming languages. It is a direct descendant of the Common Language Specification (CLS), which defines a set of rules that all .NET languages must follow to ensure interoperability.

moons.name: C#
moons.description: C# is a modern, object-oriented programming language developed by Microsoft as part of the .NET framework. It is designed to be easy to use and highly productive, with features such as garbage collection, type safety, and support for asynchronous programming.

moons.name: Visual Basic
moons.description: Visual Basic is a programming language developed by Microsoft that is part of the .NET framework. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, with a syntax that is similar to natural language. Visual Basic is often used for developing Windows applications and web services.

moons.name: F#
moons.description: F# is a functional-first programming language developed by Microsoft as part of the .NET framework. It is designed to be highly expressive and concise, with features such as pattern matching, type inference, and support for functional programming paradigms.

curious_fact: CIL was originally called Microsoft Intermediate Language (MSIL) before being renamed to Common Intermediate Language.
example:
IL_0000: ldstr "Hello, World!"
IL_0005: call void [mscorlib]System.Console::WriteLine(string)
IL_000a: ret

name: Common Lisp
description: Common Lisp is a dialect of the Lisp programming language, standardized in the late 1980s. It is known for its advanced features such as dynamic typing, automatic garbage collection, and a rich macro system. Despite its age, it remains influential in areas like artificial intelligence and symbolic computation.
connectionsExplanation: Common Lisp was developed as a successor to several Lisp dialects, aiming to unify and improve upon them. It was standardized by X3J13, a committee formed by the American National Standards Institute (ANSI), which included many of the leading Lisp programmers of the time. This standardization effort was a significant milestone in the Lisp community, leading to greater interoperability and adoption.

moons.name: SBCL
moons.description: Steel Bank Common Lisp is an efficient, native-code compiler for Common Lisp.

moons.name: CLISP
moons.description: GNU CLISP is a Common Lisp implementation with an interpreter, compiler, debugger, and foreign language interface.

curious_fact: Common Lisp was one of the first languages to support closures and garbage collection.

name: Common Lisp Music
description: Common Lisp Music (CLM) is a Common Lisp library for computer music. It provides a powerful and flexible environment for sound synthesis, composition, and analysis.
connectionsExplanation: Common Lisp Music is built on top of Common Lisp, which itself evolved from the LISP programming language. CLM extends Lisp's capabilities to handle music-specific tasks, making it a specialized tool for musicians and sound engineers.
curious_fact: CLM was developed at Stanford University and is known for its ability to generate complex musical compositions programmatically.

name: Common Workflow Language
description: Common Workflow Language (CWL) is a specification for describing data analysis workflows and tools in a way that is independent of specific execution environments. It is designed to be portable and reusable across different platforms and tools.
connectionsExplanation: CWL was developed to address the need for a standardized way to describe workflows in bioinformatics and other data-intensive fields. It builds on the principles of previous workflow languages but aims to be more flexible and interoperable.

moons.name: CWL Runner
moons.description: A tool for executing CWL workflows.

moons.name: cwltool
moons.description: A reference implementation of CWL for command line execution.

curious_fact: CWL was developed by the Globus team at the University of Chicago and Argonne National Laboratory.
example:
cwlVersion: v1.0
tools: []
inputs: []
outputs: []
steps: []

name: Communication Access Programming Language
description: Communication Access Programming Language (CAPL) is a programming language designed for the development of diagnostic and measurement applications for vehicle networks. It is primarily used in the automotive industry for testing and simulation of CAN (Controller Area Network) and LIN (Local Interconnect Network) protocols.
connectionsExplanation: CAPL was developed by Vector Informatik, a German company, and is closely related to the CANoe and CANalyzer tools, which are used for automotive network analysis. It is based on the C programming language and extends its capabilities to handle vehicle-specific tasks.
curious_fact: CAPL is one of the few languages specifically designed for automotive network diagnostics.

name: CompilerDescriptionLanguage
description: CompilerDescriptionLanguage (CDL) is a fictional programming language designed to describe the behavior and structure of compilers. It is intended for use in academic and research settings to facilitate the specification and analysis of compiler components.
connectionsExplanation: CompilerDescriptionLanguage is a hypothetical language created for the purpose of describing compilers. It does not have a direct historical predecessor but is inspired by the need for formal and precise language specifications in computer science.
curious_fact: CDL is a fictional language created to explore the formalization of compiler specifications.

name: ComponentPascal
description: ComponentPascal is an extension of the Pascal programming language designed to support component-based software development. It introduces features that facilitate the creation and management of reusable components, making it particularly useful for building complex applications.
connectionsExplanation: ComponentPascal evolved from standard Pascal, incorporating object-oriented principles and component-based architecture. This evolution was driven by the need for more modular and reusable code in software development, especially in environments where components could be easily integrated and managed.
curious_fact: ComponentPascal was one of the early languages to popularize the use of components in software development, influencing later languages and frameworks.

name: Computest
description: Computest is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool for beginners to understand basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Computest was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct lineage from existing languages. It is inspired by the principles of modern programming languages but is entirely fictional.
curious_fact: Computest uses a unique syntax that resembles natural language, making it easier for beginners to grasp.

name: ConScript
description: ConScript is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is not widely used outside of academic settings.
connectionsExplanation: ConScript was created as an educational tool to introduce programming concepts in a straightforward manner. It is not derived from any existing language but inspired by the simplicity of BASIC and the structured approach of Pascal.
curious_fact: ConScript was developed by a team of educators aiming to make programming accessible to beginners.

name: ConcurrentC
description: ConcurrentC is an extension of the C programming language designed to simplify the development of concurrent and parallel applications. It introduces constructs and libraries to manage threads and synchronization more efficiently than traditional C.
connectionsExplanation: ConcurrentC was developed as an extension of C to address the challenges of concurrent programming. It builds upon the foundational principles of C, adding features specifically tailored for managing threads and synchronization, making it a direct descendant of C.

moons.name: ThreadLib
moons.description: A library for managing threads and synchronization in ConcurrentC applications.

moons.name: ParallelAlgos
moons.description: A collection of parallel algorithms optimized for ConcurrentC.

curious_fact: ConcurrentC was one of the first languages to introduce lightweight threads directly into the language syntax.

name: ConcurrentEuclid
description: ConcurrentEuclid is a modern programming language designed for high-performance concurrent computing. It is inspired by Euclidean geometry principles, aiming to simplify complex parallel algorithms. The language is particularly suited for scientific computing and real-time systems.
connectionsExplanation: ConcurrentEuclid draws inspiration from Euclidean geometry to simplify the design of concurrent algorithms, making it a unique addition to the programming landscape. Its roots can be traced back to paradigms that emphasize clarity and efficiency in parallel processing.

moons.name: EuclidLib
moons.description: A library for geometric computations in ConcurrentEuclid.

moons.name: ParallelEuclid
moons.description: A framework for building concurrent applications using ConcurrentEuclid.

curious_fact: ConcurrentEuclid uses geometric proofs to optimize parallel execution paths.

name: Concurrent Haskell
description: Concurrent Haskell is an extension of the Haskell programming language that provides a rich set of abstractions for concurrent and parallel programming. It is built on top of GHC (Glasgow Haskell Compiler) and is known for its lightweight threads and strong type system, making it suitable for developing high-performance concurrent applications.
connectionsExplanation: Concurrent Haskell evolved from the Haskell programming language, which itself is a purely functional language. Haskell's strong type system and lazy evaluation model provide a solid foundation for concurrent programming. The introduction of Concurrent Haskell in 1994 marked a significant step in making functional programming more practical for real-world applications that require concurrency.

moons.name: STM
moons.description: Software Transactional Memory (STM) is a concurrency control mechanism in Concurrent Haskell that allows for safe and efficient concurrent programming without explicit locks.

moons.name: MVar
moons.description: MVar (mutable variable) is a synchronization primitive in Concurrent Haskell that provides a way to safely share data between threads.

curious_fact: Concurrent Haskell introduced the concept of Software Transactional Memory, which simplifies concurrent programming by allowing developers to write concurrent code as if it were sequential.
example:
main = do
  forkIO $ putStrLn "Hello"
  forkIO $ putStrLn "World"
  threadDelay 1000000

name: Concurrent ML
description: Concurrent ML is a programming language designed to support concurrent programming. It extends Standard ML with constructs for concurrency, making it suitable for building concurrent and parallel applications.
connectionsExplanation: Concurrent ML was developed as an extension of Standard ML, incorporating concurrency features to address the challenges of parallel programming. It was influenced by earlier work in concurrent programming languages and aimed to provide a more structured approach to concurrency compared to earlier languages.
curious_fact: Concurrent ML introduced the concept of 'channels' for communication between concurrent processes, which influenced later concurrent programming paradigms.

name: Concurrent Pascal
description: Concurrent Pascal is an extension of the Pascal programming language designed to support concurrent programming. It was developed by Tony Hoare at the Programming Research Group of the Oxford University Computing Laboratory.
connectionsExplanation: Concurrent Pascal was developed as an extension of Pascal to support concurrent programming. It introduced concepts like monitors and processes, which were influential in the development of later concurrent programming languages.
curious_fact: Concurrent Pascal was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of monitors, which are used to manage concurrent access to shared resources.
example: program HelloWorld; begin writeln('Hello, World!'); end.

name: Concurrent Prolog
description: Concurrent Prolog is an extension of the Prolog programming language designed to support concurrent programming. It introduces constructs for parallel execution and communication between processes, making it suitable for applications requiring high concurrency.
connectionsExplanation: Concurrent Prolog evolved from Prolog, which was developed in the late 1970s. It extends Prolog's logic programming paradigm to handle concurrent processes, addressing the need for parallel computation in the 1980s.
curious_fact: Concurrent Prolog was one of the early attempts to integrate concurrency into logic programming languages.
example:
:- module(concurrent_example, [main/0]).
main :-
    spawn(worker, W),
    send(W, message, 'Hello, World!').

worker :-
    receive(message, Msg),
    write(Msg), nl.

name: ConstraintHandlingRules
description: ConstraintHandlingRules is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on teaching constraint programming. It is not widely used outside of academic settings.

curious_fact: ConstraintHandlingRules was created to simplify the teaching of constraint programming concepts.

name: ContinuousFunctionChart
description: ContinuousFunctionChart is a fictional programming language designed for visualizing mathematical functions in real-time. It is particularly suited for educational purposes and scientific simulations.
connectionsExplanation: ContinuousFunctionChart was inspired by the need for a language that could seamlessly integrate mathematical computations with graphical representations. While it is fictional, it draws parallels with languages like MATLAB and Python, which are widely used for similar tasks.

name: ControlLanguage
description: ControlLanguage is a specialized programming language designed for industrial control systems. It was developed to simplify the programming of complex control logic in manufacturing environments.
connectionsExplanation: ControlLanguage was developed in the mid-1990s as a response to the need for more intuitive and efficient programming of industrial control systems. It evolved from earlier PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) languages and aimed to provide a higher level of abstraction and ease of use.
curious_fact: ControlLanguage was one of the first languages to introduce a graphical programming interface for industrial control systems.

name: Converge
description: Converge is a modern, functional programming language designed to be simple and expressive. It emphasizes immutability and pure functions, making it suitable for concurrent programming. Despite its innovative features, Converge has not gained widespread adoption.
connectionsExplanation: Converge was developed as an attempt to create a language that combines the best features of functional programming with modern concurrency models. It draws inspiration from Haskell and Erlang, aiming to provide a more accessible and practical approach to functional programming.
curious_fact: Converge introduced a unique syntax for pattern matching that was inspired by natural language.

name: Cool

connectionsExplanation: Cool is inspired by Smalltalk and Objective-C, aiming to provide a clean and intuitive object-oriented syntax. It was developed as part of a research project to explore new paradigms in systems programming.
curious_fact: Cool was one of the first languages to introduce automatic memory management and garbage collection in a systems programming context.

name: CorVision

connectionsExplanation: CorVision was developed as a research project at a leading university in the United States. It draws inspiration from existing languages like Python and C++, but introduces unique paradigms for handling complex visual data processing.
curious_fact: CorVision uses a novel syntax that mimics human vision pathways for efficient data processing.

name: Coral66
description: Coral66 is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It was created to demonstrate the principles of programming in a simple and intuitive way, making it accessible for beginners. Despite its fictional nature, Coral66 has been used in various educational settings to teach basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Coral66 was inspired by the early educational programming languages of the 1960s, which aimed to simplify the learning process for beginners. It shares conceptual similarities with BASIC and LOGO, both of which were designed to be easy to understand and use. Coral66's design reflects the desire to create a language that could be taught without requiring extensive prior knowledge of computer science.
curious_fact: Coral66 was created to be a stepping stone for students transitioning from pseudocode to actual programming languages.

name: Cornell University Programming Language
description: A specialized programming language developed at Cornell University for educational purposes. It focuses on teaching programming concepts through a unique syntax and semantics designed to enhance understanding.
connectionsExplanation: The Cornell University Programming Language was created as an in-house tool for teaching programming concepts to students. It draws inspiration from modern programming paradigms but introduces novel features to facilitate learning.

name: CosmicOS
description: CosmicOS is a fictional programming language designed for space exploration and interstellar communication. It is highly abstract and focuses on high-level operations suitable for complex simulations and data processing in cosmic environments.
connectionsExplanation: CosmicOS was inspired by the need for a language that could handle the vast complexities of space communication and data analysis. It draws parallels with existing languages like Python and C++, but with a unique syntax and paradigms tailored for cosmic applications.
curious_fact: CosmicOS includes a built-in function to convert Earth-based time to galactic coordinates.

name: Croc
description: Croc is a stack-based esoteric programming language created by David Morgan-Mar in 2000. It is known for its unique syntax and minimalist approach, making it a popular choice among esoteric programming enthusiasts.
connectionsExplanation: Croc is an esoteric programming language that does not have a direct historical predecessor. It is inspired by the concept of stack-based languages and the minimalist philosophy often found in esoteric programming languages.
curious_fact: Croc uses a stack-based architecture and a unique set of commands to manipulate data.

name: Cryptol
description: Cryptol is a domain-specific language for specifying cryptographic algorithms. It is designed to be both expressive and precise, allowing for the concise description of complex cryptographic protocols.
connectionsExplanation: Cryptol was developed by Galois, Inc., and is influenced by Haskell and functional programming paradigms. It is used primarily in the verification and validation of cryptographic software.
curious_fact: Cryptol is used by the NSA to verify the correctness of cryptographic implementations.

name: Crystal
description: Crystal is a statically typed, compiled language with syntax inspired by Ruby. It aims to be a modern, fast, and productive language for building scalable applications.
connectionsExplanation: Crystal was created by Manas Sarougi and is heavily inspired by Ruby. It shares Ruby's elegant syntax and dynamic features while compiling to efficient native code. This makes it a unique choice for developers looking for the speed of compiled languages with the ease of Ruby.

curious_fact: Crystal can compile to native code, making it faster than Ruby while maintaining its dynamic nature.

name: Csound
description: Csound is a computer music programming language and software synthesizer system. It is widely used for algorithmic composition and sound synthesis.
connectionsExplanation: Csound was developed by Barry Vercoe at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in 1986. It is a direct descendant of the MUSIC V family of computer music languages, which were developed in the 1960s and 1970s. Csound's syntax and capabilities have been influenced by the need for precise control over sound synthesis and composition.

name: Cub
description: Cub is a minimalist programming language designed to be as simple as possible while still being Turing-complete. It is known for its small size and ease of implementation.
connectionsExplanation: Cub is inspired by the desire to create a language that is minimalistic and easy to understand, drawing influences from the simplicity of Brainfuck and the minimalism of esoteric programming languages.
curious_fact: Cub is one of the smallest Turing-complete programming languages, with only 16 commands.

name: Cuis Smalltalk
description: Cuis Smalltalk is a modern, open-source implementation of the Smalltalk programming language. It is known for its simplicity, portability, and educational value, making it a popular choice for teaching programming concepts and for hobbyists.
connectionsExplanation: Cuis Smalltalk is a direct descendant of the original Smalltalk-80 language, which was developed at Xerox PARC in the 1970s. It inherits the dynamic, object-oriented nature of Smalltalk and aims to provide a clean, efficient, and easy-to-understand environment for programming.

moons.name: Pharo

moons.name: Squeak

curious_fact: Cuis Smalltalk runs on a wide range of platforms, including smartphones and embedded systems, thanks to its lightweight and efficient design.

name: Cuneiform
description: Cuneiform is one of the earliest known forms of writing, developed by the ancient Sumerians in Mesopotamia. It was used for administrative, literary, and religious purposes and was the dominant writing system of the region for over three millennia.
connectionsExplanation: Cuneiform is not a programming language but an ancient writing system. It predates the development of modern programming languages by thousands of years. Its influence can be seen in the development of alphabetic writing systems, which eventually led to the creation of various scripts used in modern programming languages.
curious_fact: Cuneiform was written on clay tablets using a reed stylus, which created wedge-shaped impressions in the clay.

name: Curl
description: Curl is a command-line tool for transferring data with URLs. It supports various protocols like HTTP, FTP, and SMTP. Despite its age, Curl remains widely used for scripting and automation tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Curl was developed by Daniel Stenberg in 1997. It was inspired by the need for a simple and efficient way to transfer data over the internet. Curl's design was influenced by the Unix philosophy of small, single-purpose tools.
curious_fact: Curl was originally named 'filemaker' but was later renamed to avoid confusion with another software product.

name: CurlyWas
description: CurlyWas is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is inspired by the curly brace syntax of languages like C and Java but with a unique twist in its semantics.
connectionsExplanation: CurlyWas was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts. While it shares syntax similarities with C and Java, its design focuses on clarity and ease of learning, making it distinct from its predecessors.
curious_fact: CurlyWas was developed in a weekend hackathon by a group of computer science students.

name: Curry
description: Curry is a purely functional programming language developed by Simon Peyton Jones and Philip Wadler. It is based on Haskell and emphasizes the use of currying in function definitions. Curry is known for its strong static typing and lazy evaluation.
connectionsExplanation: Curry is a direct descendant of Haskell, which itself is a purely functional language inspired by Miranda. It inherits the functional paradigm and currying concept from Haskell, making it a specialized language within the functional programming family.
curious_fact: Curry is named after the mathematician Haskell Curry, who made significant contributions to combinatory logic.

name: Curry Notation System
description: Curry Notation System is a theoretical programming language developed by Haskell Curry. It is a formal system for expressing computations using combinatory logic, focusing on function application and abstraction. The language is primarily of academic interest and is not widely used in practical applications.
connectionsExplanation: Curry Notation System is a foundational concept in the development of functional programming languages. It was inspired by the work of Alonzo Church and Haskell Curry on lambda calculus, which laid the groundwork for modern functional programming paradigms.
curious_fact: Curry Notation System was one of the early attempts to formalize computation using combinatory logic, influencing the development of functional programming languages.
example: let add = \x -> \y -> x + y; let result = add 3 4;

name: Cybil
description: Cybil is a modern, high-level programming language designed for parallel computing and artificial intelligence applications. It aims to simplify the development of complex algorithms and data processing tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Cybil was developed as a successor to the Cython language, inheriting its syntax and performance optimizations. It introduces new features tailored for AI and parallel processing, making it a specialized tool in the field.

moons.name: Cybil-Parallel
moons.description: A parallel processing framework for Cybil, enabling efficient execution of multi-threaded applications.

curious_fact: Cybil was named after the Cyborg from the 'Cyberpunk' series, symbolizing its fusion of human and machine capabilities.

name: CycL
description: CycL is a high-level programming language developed by Cycorp for the Cyc artificial intelligence project. It is designed to express knowledge in a way that can be easily understood and processed by machines.
connectionsExplanation: CycL was developed as part of the Cyc project, which aimed to create a comprehensive knowledge base and reasoning system. It is not directly derived from any other language but is influenced by Lisp in its syntax and structure.
curious_fact: CycL is one of the earliest languages designed specifically for artificial intelligence and knowledge representation.

name: Cyclone
description: Cyclone is a systems programming language developed at Bell Labs as a safer alternative to C. It aims to provide the performance and control of C while incorporating modern safety features to prevent common programming errors.
connectionsExplanation: Cyclone was developed as an evolution of the C programming language, inheriting its low-level control and performance characteristics. It was designed to address the safety issues prevalent in C by introducing features such as automatic memory management and type safety, making it a direct descendant of C.
curious_fact: Cyclone was one of the first languages to introduce automatic memory management while maintaining C's performance.

name: Cypher Query Language
description: Cypher is a declarative graph query language developed by Neo4j. It is designed to efficiently query and update graph databases, making it particularly suited for applications that model complex relationships.
connectionsExplanation: Cypher was developed by Neo4j, a graph database company, to provide a more intuitive and expressive way to work with graph data. It is based on the concept of pattern matching, allowing users to specify complex queries in a readable and concise manner.
curious_fact: Cypher is named after the Cypher Cipher, a historical encryption method.

name: Cython
description: Cython is a programming language that makes writing C extensions for Python as easy as Python itself. It is a superset of the Python language that additionally supports calling C functions and declaring C types on variables and class attributes. Cython is used to speed up Python code by generating C code that can be compiled to a Python extension module.
connectionsExplanation: Cython was developed to bridge the gap between Python and C, allowing Python code to be compiled into C extensions. It is particularly useful for performance-critical applications where Python's dynamic nature is a bottleneck. Cython's syntax is a superset of Python, making it easy for Python developers to learn and use.

description: Disk Extended Color BASIC is an extended version of the popular Color BASIC programming language, designed for the Apple II series of computers. It introduced additional commands for disk operations and enhanced graphics capabilities, making it suitable for more advanced programming tasks on the Apple II platform.
connectionsExplanation: Disk Extended Color BASIC evolved from the standard Color BASIC, which was a dialect of Dartmouth BASIC. It was specifically tailored to take advantage of the Apple II's hardware capabilities, particularly its color graphics and disk drive. This evolution allowed programmers to create more sophisticated applications and games that leveraged the Apple II's unique features.
curious_fact: Disk Extended Color BASIC was one of the first languages to introduce disk-based file handling in a consumer-oriented BASIC dialect.

name: Distributed Application Specification Language
description: Distributed Application Specification Language (DASL) is a specialized language designed for specifying distributed applications. It focuses on defining the interactions and data flows between components in a distributed system.
connectionsExplanation: DASL was developed as an extension of XML to provide a more structured and efficient way to specify distributed applications. It builds upon the XML standard, which is widely used for data interchange, to offer a more application-specific syntax.
curious_fact: DASL was one of the early attempts to standardize the specification of distributed applications, aiming to simplify the development of complex systems.
example: <application><component name="server"><interface type="http" port="8080"/></component><component name="client"><interface type="http" port="8080"/></component></application>

name: DocuDraft
description: DocuDraft is a fictional programming language designed for rapid documentation generation. It is inspired by Markdown and structured text formats, aiming to simplify the creation of technical and user manuals.
connectionsExplanation: DocuDraft was created as an internal tool at a tech company to streamline documentation processes. It draws inspiration from Markdown's simplicity and adds structured elements to enhance automation and consistency in document generation.
curious_fact: DocuDraft uses a unique syntax that allows for the embedding of executable code snippets directly within documentation, enabling real-time updates and examples.

name: Dodo0

name: Dog

name: DuAL

name: Dylan

name: E
description: E is a programming language designed for secure distributed computing. It is part of the E family of languages, which includes E, E-on, and E-on++.
connectionsExplanation: E was developed as part of the Cap project at Digital Equipment Corporation in the late 1990s. It is inspired by the concept of capability-based security and is designed to facilitate secure communication between distributed systems.
curious_fact: E was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'ambient authority', which allows for fine-grained control over permissions in distributed systems.
example: def hello(name: String): String { return "Hello, " + name + "!" }

name: ECL
description: ECL, or Embeddable Common Lisp, is a Common Lisp implementation designed to be easily embedded into C programs. It is known for its efficiency and compatibility with C, making it a popular choice for applications that require both Lisp and C functionalities.
connectionsExplanation: ECL is a descendant of Common Lisp, which itself evolved from the earlier Lisp programming languages. It was developed to provide a more efficient and embeddable version of Common Lisp, catering to the needs of applications that require Lisp-like capabilities within a C environment.

name: ECLiPSe
description: ECLiPSe is a declarative logic programming language developed in the UK. It is particularly suited for constraint logic programming and is used in various applications such as scheduling, configuration, and optimization problems.
connectionsExplanation: ECLiPSe evolved from earlier logic programming systems and is closely related to Prolog. It extends Prolog with constraint solving capabilities, making it a powerful tool for domains that require complex constraint handling.

moons.name: CHIP
moons.description: CHIP is a constraint handling library for ECLiPSe, providing additional constraint solving capabilities.

curious_fact: ECLiPSe was one of the first systems to integrate constraint logic programming with Prolog.
example:
:- lib(ic).

gcd(X, Y, G) :-
    X #> 0,
    Y #> 0,
    G #= gcd(X, Y, G).

gcd(X, Y, G) :-
    X #=< Y,
    G #= gcd(Y, X, G).

gcd(X, Y, G) :-
    X #> Y,
    D #= X mod Y,
    G #= gcd(Y, D, G).

gcd(_, 0, G) :-
    G #= 1.

name: EFL

name: Emacs Lisp
