connectionsExplanation: MAGMA-Lisp was inspired by the need for a Lisp variant that could handle complex scientific computations more efficiently. It evolved from Common Lisp, incorporating features that allowed for better performance in scientific and engineering applications.
curious_fact: MAGMA-Lisp introduced the 'parallel-map' function, which significantly sped up computations by distributing tasks across multiple processors.

name: MAIBasicFour
description: MAIBasicFour is a specialized dialect of BASIC designed for educational purposes in the UK. It was developed to introduce programming concepts to students in a simplified manner.
connectionsExplanation: MAIBasicFour evolved from standard BASIC, adapting its syntax and features to better suit the educational needs of British schools in the 1980s.
curious_fact: MAIBasicFour was one of the first educational programming languages to incorporate structured programming elements into its syntax.

name: MAPPER
description: MAPPER is a specialized programming language designed for mapping and geographic information systems (GIS). It was developed in the early 1970s to facilitate the creation and manipulation of digital maps. Despite its age, MAPPER remains relevant in niche GIS applications.
connectionsExplanation: MAPPER was developed as a specialized tool for GIS applications, evolving from earlier mapping technologies and influencing subsequent GIS software. Its roots lie in the need for efficient map data processing and visualization.
curious_fact: MAPPER was one of the first languages to introduce structured data handling for geographic information.
example:
MAPPER
BEGIN
  MAP CREATE "world_map"
  MAP LOAD "world_data"
  MAP DISPLAY
END

name: MARKIV
description: MARKIV is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing structured programming concepts. It was created to teach students the basics of programming in a simplified yet comprehensive manner.
connectionsExplanation: MARKIV was developed as an educational tool in the 1980s, inspired by structured programming paradigms. It aimed to provide a clear and straightforward syntax for beginners, making it easier for students to grasp fundamental programming concepts without the complexities of more advanced languages.
curious_fact: MARKIV was one of the first languages to introduce a simple, graphical interface for beginners.

name: MATH-MATIC
description: MATH-MATIC is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing mathematical operations and algorithms. It is intended to simplify complex mathematical concepts for beginners and students.
connectionsExplanation: MATH-MATIC was inspired by the need for a language that could demystify mathematics for non-experts. It draws from the paradigms of functional programming and procedural languages, aiming to provide a clear and concise syntax for mathematical expressions.
curious_fact: MATH-MATIC was created by a team of educators and mathematicians to make learning math more accessible.

name: MATLAB
description: MATLAB is a high-level programming language and interactive computing environment used for numerical computations, algorithm development, data analysis, and visualization. It is widely used in academia and industry for scientific and engineering applications.
connectionsExplanation: MATLAB was developed by MathWorks in the late 1970s and early 1980s. It was inspired by the LINPACK and EISPACK libraries for numerical linear algebra, which were written in Fortran. MATLAB's syntax and functionality have been influenced by the need for ease of use and efficient numerical computation, making it a descendant of the broader family of numerical computing tools that emerged from the scientific computing community.

moons.name: Simulink
moons.description: A graphical programming environment for modeling, simulating, and analyzing multidomain dynamic systems.

moons.name: MATLAB Toolbox
moons.description: A collection of specialized functions and applications for specific tasks, such as signal processing, image processing, and control systems.

curious_fact: MATLAB was originally developed by Cleve Moler, who later co-founded MathWorks.

name: MatrixMath
description: MatrixMath is a specialized programming language designed for high-performance matrix operations, particularly in fields like computer graphics, scientific computing, and machine learning. It is known for its concise syntax and efficient execution.
connectionsExplanation: MatrixMath was developed as an extension of the C++ language, incorporating advanced linear algebra libraries to provide a more streamlined environment for matrix computations. Its design leverages the performance optimizations found in C++ while abstracting away much of the boilerplate code typically associated with matrix operations.

moons.name: MatrixLib
moons.description: A comprehensive library for matrix operations within the MatrixMath ecosystem.

curious_fact: MatrixMath was created by a team of mathematicians and computer scientists who aimed to simplify complex matrix calculations for researchers and engineers.

name: MBASIC
description: MBASIC, short for Microsoft BASIC, is a dialect of the BASIC programming language developed by Microsoft for the Altair 8800 microcomputer. It was one of the first high-level programming languages available for personal computers and played a significant role in the early popularization of microcomputers.
connectionsExplanation: MBASIC is a direct descendant of Dartmouth BASIC, which was developed in the early 1960s. It was specifically designed for the Altair 8800, one of the first commercially successful personal computers, and was instrumental in Microsoft's early success in the software industry.
curious_fact: MBASIC was one of the first languages to be sold as a commercial product for personal computers.

name: MC#
description: MC# is a specialized programming language designed for creating Minecraft mods. It is a C# dialect that leverages the Minecraft Forge modding framework, allowing developers to extend the game's functionality with custom code.
connectionsExplanation: MC# is a dialect of C# specifically tailored for Minecraft modding. It inherits from C# to provide a familiar syntax and powerful features while integrating seamlessly with the Minecraft Forge framework, which is the primary modding platform for Minecraft.
curious_fact: MC# was created to simplify the process of modding Minecraft, making it accessible to a broader audience of developers.
example: public class HelloWorldMod : Mod { public override void Init() { Chat.Message("Hello, Minecraft world!"); } }

name: MCPL
description: MCPL, or MultiParadigm Compiler Language, is a programming language developed in the early 1970s. It was designed to be a versatile language that could be used for a wide range of applications, including systems programming, scientific computing, and business applications. MCPL was influenced by several other languages of its time, including BCPL and ALGOL.
connectionsExplanation: MCPL was developed as a successor to BCPL and was influenced by the structured programming paradigm. It aimed to provide a more flexible and powerful language for systems programming while maintaining the simplicity and efficiency of BCPL.
curious_fact: MCPL was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of nested functions.

name: MDL
description: MDL (Modeling Language) is a programming language developed at MIT in the 1960s. It was designed for symbolic computation and artificial intelligence applications. MDL is known for its ability to handle complex symbolic expressions and is particularly suited for tasks involving symbolic mathematics and knowledge representation.
connectionsExplanation: MDL was developed at MIT in the late 1960s as part of the early efforts in artificial intelligence and symbolic computation. It was influenced by the need to create a language that could effectively handle symbolic data and complex mathematical expressions, which was a significant departure from the numerical computation focus of earlier languages like FORTRAN.
curious_fact: MDL was one of the first languages to support symbolic computation, which was crucial for early AI research.
example: define factorial(n) = if n = 0 then 1 else n * factorial(n - 1)

name: MIDAS
description: MIDAS is a high-level programming language developed by the University of California, Berkeley, in the 1980s. It was designed for scientific and engineering applications, particularly in the fields of geophysics and seismology. MIDAS is known for its ease of use and powerful data manipulation capabilities.
connectionsExplanation: MIDAS was developed as part of the Berkeley Seismology Laboratory's efforts to create a user-friendly language for seismic data analysis. It was influenced by the need for a language that could handle complex scientific computations efficiently.

moons.name: MIDAS Seismic
moons.description: A specialized library for seismic data processing.

curious_fact: MIDAS was one of the first languages to incorporate automatic memory management for scientific applications.

name: MIIS
description: MIIS is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: MIIS was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct lineage from existing programming languages. It is inspired by the simplicity of BASIC and the structured approach of Pascal, aiming to provide a gentle learning curve for new programmers.
curious_fact: MIIS stands for 'Minimal Instruction Set System', emphasizing its simplicity in design.

name: MIMIC
description: MIMIC is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not widely used in industry but is popular in academic settings for teaching basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: MIMIC was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts without the complexity of more advanced languages. It draws inspiration from BASIC and Python, aiming to provide a gentle learning curve.
curious_fact: MIMIC was developed by a team of educators to make programming accessible to children.

name: MINC
description: MINC (Medical Imaging NetCDF) is a file format and software library for storing and manipulating medical images. It is designed to be flexible and extensible, supporting a wide range of image types and metadata.
connectionsExplanation: MINC is a specialized format for medical imaging, built on top of the NetCDF library. It evolved from the need to standardize and efficiently handle large medical datasets, particularly in neuroimaging.
curious_fact: MINC was developed at the McConnell Brain Imaging Centre in Montreal, Canada.

name: MIT/GNU Scheme
description: MIT/GNU Scheme is an implementation of the Scheme programming language, which is a dialect of Lisp. It is known for its educational use and is widely used in computer science education.
connectionsExplanation: MIT/GNU Scheme is a direct descendant of the original Scheme language, which was developed at MIT in the late 1970s. It inherits the functional programming paradigm and Lisp syntax from its ancestors.
curious_fact: MIT/GNU Scheme is one of the oldest implementations of Scheme and is still actively maintained.

name: MIVAScript
description: MIVAScript is a scripting language developed by Miva Systems for use with their e-commerce platform. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, making it popular among small businesses and developers who need to create dynamic web pages and applications.
connectionsExplanation: MIVAScript was developed as a proprietary language for Miva Systems' e-commerce platform, which was first released in 1996. It is not derived from any other language but was created to meet the specific needs of web development and e-commerce solutions.

moons.name: Miva Merchant
moons.description: A popular e-commerce platform that uses MIVAScript for scripting and customization.

curious_fact: MIVAScript was one of the first scripting languages specifically designed for e-commerce.

name: MIXAL
description: MIXAL is an assembly language designed by Donald Knuth for his book 'The Art of Computer Programming'. It is based on the hypothetical MIX computer and is used for educational purposes to illustrate computer architecture and assembly language programming.
connectionsExplanation: MIXAL is directly inspired by the MIX computer model, which was created by Donald Knuth to serve as a theoretical machine for his seminal work in computer science. It is not a direct descendant of any existing assembly language but is instead a pedagogical tool designed to teach the principles of computer architecture and assembly language programming.
curious_fact: MIXAL is so well-suited for teaching computer architecture that it has been used in university courses for decades.
example:
LDA ONE
STA SUM
HLT
ONE: 1
SUM: 0

name: ML
description: ML (Meta Language) is a family of functional programming languages developed at the University of Edinburgh in the 1970s. It is known for its strong static typing and influence on modern functional languages like Haskell and OCaml.
connectionsExplanation: ML was developed as part of the Edinburgh LCF (Logic for Computable Functions) project, which aimed to create a formal system for reasoning about programs. It was influenced by the earlier functional language Lisp and the imperative language Algol 68.

moons.name: OCaml
moons.description: OCaml is a dialect of ML that adds object-oriented features and a foreign function interface.

moons.name: Standard ML
moons.description: Standard ML is a dialect of ML that is standardized and widely used in academia and industry.

curious_fact: ML was one of the first languages to introduce polymorphic types, allowing functions to operate on multiple types.
example: fun factorial 0 = 1 | factorial n = n * factorial (n - 1);

name: MLAB
description: MLAB is a domain-specific programming language designed for mathematical and scientific computations. It was developed in the 1980s and is known for its capabilities in numerical analysis and data visualization.
connectionsExplanation: MLAB was developed as a specialized tool for scientific computing, influenced by earlier languages like FORTRAN and MATLAB. It aimed to provide a more user-friendly environment for mathematical modeling and data analysis.
curious_fact: MLAB was one of the early languages to incorporate graphical user interfaces for scientific data visualization.

name: MLisp
description: MLisp is a dialect of Lisp developed in the early 1980s. It was designed to be a more efficient and practical version of Lisp, focusing on performance and ease of use. MLisp introduced several optimizations and extensions to the Lisp language, making it suitable for both scripting and application development.
connectionsExplanation: MLisp evolved from the original Lisp language, which was developed in the late 1950s. It shares many core principles and syntax with Lisp but incorporates improvements and innovations to address the limitations of earlier versions. MLisp's development was influenced by the growing need for a more efficient Lisp dialect in the 1980s.

moons.name: MLisp-2
moons.description: MLisp-2 is an extended version of MLisp with additional features and optimizations.

curious_fact: MLisp was one of the first Lisp dialects to introduce garbage collection as a standard feature.

name: MOLOG
description: MOLOG is a logic programming language developed in the 1970s. It was designed to facilitate the creation of expert systems by allowing users to express knowledge in a declarative manner. MOLOG is based on first-order logic and is known for its ability to handle complex reasoning tasks.
connectionsExplanation: MOLOG is a descendant of early logic programming languages like Lisp and Prolog. It was developed at the University of Edinburgh, which was a hub for early AI research. MOLOG's design was influenced by the need to create more user-friendly and efficient tools for building expert systems.
curious_fact: MOLOG was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'meta-inference', allowing programs to reason about their own reasoning processes.
example:
assert(knows(alice, programming)).
assert(knows(bob, logic)).
query(knows(alice, programming)).

name: MOO
description: MOO is a prototype-based, object-oriented programming language designed for creating virtual worlds, particularly for interactive fiction and multi-user domains. It was developed at LambdaMOO, a virtual reality community.
connectionsExplanation: MOO evolved from earlier prototype-based languages and was influenced by Smalltalk's object-oriented paradigm. It was specifically designed to facilitate the creation of collaborative, text-based virtual environments, setting it apart from other programming languages of its time.
curious_fact: MOO was used to create LambdaMOO, one of the first virtual worlds on the internet, which operated from 1989 to 2000.

name: MOTIS
description: MOTIS is a modern, high-performance programming language designed for efficient transportation network analysis. It is particularly suited for applications in urban planning and traffic management.
connectionsExplanation: MOTIS was developed as a specialized language to address the computational challenges of transportation network analysis. It draws inspiration from imperative and functional paradigms, aiming to provide a balance between performance and ease of use for complex data processing tasks.
curious_fact: MOTIS stands for 'Multi-Objective Transportation Information System'.

name: MPD
description: MPD is a music player daemon that allows remote control of audio playback. It is written in C and is highly configurable, supporting various audio formats and plugins.
connectionsExplanation: MPD is not a programming language but a music player daemon. It is written in C, which is a general-purpose programming language. The connection to C reflects its implementation rather than a direct lineage from a programming language.
curious_fact: MPD was originally developed by Robert William Fuller and has since become a popular choice for headless music servers.

name: MSBASIC for Macintosh
description: MSBASIC for Macintosh was a version of Microsoft BASIC designed specifically for Apple Macintosh computers. It was one of the first programming languages available for the Macintosh and played a significant role in the early development of software for the platform.
connectionsExplanation: MSBASIC for Macintosh evolved from the original BASIC language, which was developed in the late 1960s. It was part of the broader trend of bringing BASIC to personal computers, including the Apple II, which paved the way for its adaptation to the Macintosh in 1984.
curious_fact: MSBASIC for Macintosh was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of a graphical user interface (GUI) in a BASIC environment.

name: MSTWorkshop
description: MSTWorkshop is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, particularly for teaching concepts in computer science and software engineering. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool for instructors to demonstrate programming principles in a controlled environment.
connectionsExplanation: MSTWorkshop was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct lineage from existing programming languages. It is inspired by various paradigms to provide a comprehensive learning experience.
curious_fact: MSTWorkshop includes a unique feature that allows students to visualize the execution of code in real-time, enhancing understanding of programming concepts.

name: MSX BASIC
description: MSX BASIC is a dialect of the BASIC programming language used on MSX computers, a popular home computer series in Japan and other parts of Asia during the 1980s. It was designed to be compatible with MS-DOS and included features like sprites and sound capabilities.
connectionsExplanation: MSX BASIC evolved from earlier versions of BASIC, particularly influenced by Dartmouth BASIC and Microsoft BASIC. It was developed by ASCII Corporation and Microsoft for the MSX standard, aiming to provide a standardized BASIC environment across different hardware manufacturers.
curious_fact: MSX BASIC was one of the first BASIC dialects to support sprites, which were essential for video game development on the MSX platform.

name: MShell
description: MShell is a modern, lightweight shell scripting language designed for rapid development and deployment of system automation tasks. It aims to provide a more intuitive and efficient alternative to traditional shell scripting languages.
connectionsExplanation: MShell was developed as an evolution of Unix shell scripting, incorporating modern language features and syntax improvements to enhance productivity and ease of use. It draws inspiration from languages like Python and Ruby, focusing on readability and simplicity.
curious_fact: MShell introduced a unique feature called 'auto-completion chaining' which allows for more efficient command execution and error handling.

name: MUDDL
description: MUDDL is a domain-specific language designed for database manipulation and query. It was developed in the 1970s to provide a more intuitive and user-friendly way to interact with databases compared to traditional SQL.
connectionsExplanation: MUDDL was developed at the University of California, Berkeley, as part of the Berkeley DB project. It was influenced by the need for a more accessible language for database users who were not necessarily programmers. While it never gained widespread adoption, MUDDL played a role in the evolution of database query languages.
curious_fact: MUDDL was one of the early attempts to create a more user-friendly database language, predating the widespread adoption of SQL.
example: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE department = 'Sales';

name: MUF
description: MUF (Multi-User Forth) is a Forth-based programming language designed for use in multi-user environments, particularly in the LambdaMOO virtual world system. It is known for its simplicity and efficiency in handling complex interactions in MOO environments.
connectionsExplanation: MUF is a direct descendant of Forth, a stack-based language developed by Charles Moore in the 1970s. It was specifically designed to enhance the capabilities of MOO systems, which require efficient handling of multiple users and complex interactions.
curious_fact: MUF was developed to run on the LambdaMOO server, which is one of the oldest and most influential MOO systems.

name: MUMPS
description: MUMPS (Massachusetts General Hospital Utility Multi-Programming System) is a high-level programming language designed for healthcare applications. It is known for its ability to handle large databases and its use in hospital information systems.
connectionsExplanation: MUMPS was developed at Massachusetts General Hospital in the 1960s. It was designed to handle large databases and complex healthcare applications, making it a pioneer in the field of medical software. Its unique syntax and data management capabilities have influenced several modern programming languages, particularly in the healthcare sector.

moons.name: Caché
moons.description: Caché is a modern object-oriented database and application server that extends the MUMPS language with object-oriented features.

moons.name: GT.M
moons.description: GT.M is a high-performance, open-source implementation of the MUMPS language, known for its reliability and scalability.

curious_fact: MUMPS is one of the oldest programming languages still in widespread use today, particularly in healthcare systems.

name: MUSIC-N
description: MUSIC-N is a computer language designed for music composition and synthesis. It was developed at Bell Labs in the 1960s and is notable for its ability to generate complex musical patterns and scores programmatically.
connectionsExplanation: MUSIC-N was one of the early languages designed specifically for music composition, evolving from earlier music synthesis languages like MUSIC. It influenced later music programming languages and systems.
curious_fact: MUSIC-N was used to compose the soundtrack for the film 'Tron'.
example: note C4 4; note D4 4; note E4 4; note F4 4;

name: MXML
description: MXML (Matisse XML) is a declarative markup language used for building user interfaces in Adobe Flex applications. It allows developers to design UI components using XML syntax, which are then compiled into ActionScript code.
connectionsExplanation: MXML is a direct descendant of ActionScript and is used in conjunction with the Adobe Flex framework. It provides a way to define user interfaces declaratively, making it easier to design complex UIs. MXML files are compiled into ActionScript classes, which are then executed by the Flash Player.
curious_fact: MXML was introduced as part of Adobe Flex, which was later succeeded by Apache Flex.

name: Macintosh Common Lisp
description: Macintosh Common Lisp (MCL) is a dialect of Common Lisp specifically developed for Apple's Macintosh computers. It was one of the first Lisp environments to run on the Macintosh platform, providing a powerful development environment for Lisp programmers.
connectionsExplanation: Macintosh Common Lisp evolved from Common Lisp, which itself is a direct descendant of Lisp. MCL was specifically tailored to run on Apple's Macintosh operating system, leveraging the graphical capabilities of the Macintosh to provide a rich development environment.
curious_fact: MCL was one of the first Lisp environments to support object-oriented programming, predating CLOS (Common Lisp Object System) by several years.

name: Maclisp
description: Maclisp is an early dialect of the Lisp programming language developed at MIT. It was influential in the development of Common Lisp and other Lisp dialects.
connectionsExplanation: Maclisp was developed at MIT in the early 1960s and is considered a direct descendant of Lisp. It introduced several features that influenced later Lisp dialects, including Common Lisp.
curious_fact: Maclisp was one of the first Lisp dialects to support structured programming constructs.

name: MacroML
description: MacroML is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid development and deployment of machine learning models. It emphasizes ease of use and integration with existing data science workflows.
connectionsExplanation: MacroML was inspired by the need for a more accessible and efficient language for machine learning practitioners. It draws from the paradigms of functional programming and domain-specific languages, aiming to provide a seamless experience for both beginners and experts.

moons.name: TensorKit
moons.description: A library for tensor operations and machine learning model building.

moons.name: ModelFlow
moons.description: A framework for defining and training machine learning models.

curious_fact: MacroML was developed in collaboration with leading universities and tech companies to address the growing demand for accessible machine learning tools.

name: Magik
description: Magik is an object-oriented programming language developed by ICL (International Computers Ltd.) in the 1980s. It was designed to be used in the ICL's 2900 Series mainframe computers. Magik is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among developers working on ICL systems.
connectionsExplanation: Magik was developed as an object-oriented language for ICL's 2900 Series mainframes. It was designed to be user-friendly and efficient, catering to the needs of the mainframe environment. While it was not widely adopted outside of ICL, it played a significant role in the development of object-oriented programming concepts within the company.
curious_fact: Magik was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'live objects' in a commercial environment.

name: Mama
description: Mama is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Mama was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical lineage. It is inspired by modern programming languages but is not derived from any specific existing language.
curious_fact: Mama was developed by a group of educators to make programming accessible to children.

name: Mana
description: Mana is a fictional programming language designed for use in the Mana World video game series. It is a simple, interpreted language with a focus on ease of use for game scripting.
connectionsExplanation: Mana was created specifically for the Mana World game series, making it a derivative of the game's scripting needs rather than evolving from traditional programming languages.
curious_fact: Mana was one of the first languages designed for video game scripting, influencing many subsequent game development tools.

name: Managed Extensions for C++
description: Managed Extensions for C++ was a language extension for C++ that allowed developers to write code that could interoperate with the Common Language Runtime (CLR) of Microsoft's .NET framework. It aimed to provide C++ developers with the ability to use .NET features while maintaining C++ syntax and performance.
connectionsExplanation: Managed Extensions for C++ was introduced as a bridge between C++ and the .NET framework, allowing C++ developers to leverage .NET's managed code features. It was a direct evolution from standard C++ and was intended to facilitate the transition of existing C++ applications to the .NET environment.
curious_fact: Managed Extensions for C++ was deprecated in Visual Studio 2015, replaced by C++/CLI.

name: Manchester Scene Description Language
description: Manchester Scene Description Language (MSDL) was a programming language developed at the University of Manchester in the early 1960s. It was designed to describe scenes and objects in a way that could be interpreted by a computer to generate images. MSDL was part of the early efforts in computer graphics and was used in research projects at the time.
connectionsExplanation: Manchester Scene Description Language (MSDL) was developed as part of the early computer graphics research at the University of Manchester. It was influenced by the need to describe scenes and objects in a structured way for image generation. While it did not directly evolve into modern programming languages, it contributed to the foundational concepts in computer graphics.
curious_fact: MSDL was one of the first languages to focus on scene description for computer graphics, predating modern 3D modeling languages by several decades.

name: MapBasic
description: MapBasic is a procedural programming language developed by Pitney Bowes Software for use with its MapInfo GIS software. It allows users to automate tasks, create custom functions, and extend the capabilities of MapInfo.
connectionsExplanation: MapBasic is a direct descendant of BASIC, specifically designed to integrate with MapInfo GIS software. It inherits the procedural paradigm from BASIC and is tailored for geographic information system (GIS) applications.
curious_fact: MapBasic was one of the first languages to integrate GIS capabilities directly into a programming environment.

name: MapLibre Style Specification
description: MapLibre Style Specification is a JSON-based language used to define the visual appearance of maps. It is an open-source project that allows developers to create custom map styles for web and mobile applications.
connectionsExplanation: MapLibre Style Specification is derived from the Mapbox Style Specification, which it aims to be compatible with. It inherits the same JSON-based structure and purpose, focusing on defining map styles. The specification is part of the broader ecosystem of map-related technologies, building on the foundational work of Mapbox and contributing to the open-source mapping community.
curious_fact: MapLibre Style Specification supports the use of expressions to dynamically style map elements based on data attributes.

name: Mapbox Style Specification
description: Mapbox Style Specification is a JSON-based language used to define the visual appearance of maps on the Mapbox platform. It allows developers to customize map styles, including colors, fonts, and layers.
connectionsExplanation: Mapbox Style Specification is a JSON-based language used to define the visual appearance of maps on the Mapbox platform. It allows developers to customize map styles, including colors, fonts, and layers.
curious_fact: Mapbox Style Specification is not a programming language in the traditional sense but a JSON schema used to define map styles.

name: Maple
description: Maple is a high-performance symbolic computation system developed by the Symbolic Computation Group at the University of Waterloo. It is widely used in education and research for mathematical problem-solving, algorithm development, and data analysis.
connectionsExplanation: Maple evolved from the SYMBAL system, which was developed in the late 1970s at the University of Waterloo. It was designed to provide a comprehensive environment for symbolic mathematics, building upon earlier symbolic computation systems like MACSYMA.

moons.name: MapleSim
moons.description: A physical modeling and simulation tool for engineering and scientific applications.

moons.name: Maple T.A.
moons.description: An online testing and assessment system for mathematics and STEM courses.

curious_fact: Maple was one of the first systems to implement the concept of a 'worksheet' interface, which allowed users to mix symbolic mathematics with text and graphics.

name: Mary
description: Mary is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It was created to introduce beginners to programming concepts without overwhelming them with complex syntax.
connectionsExplanation: Mary was inspired by the simplicity of BASIC and Python, aiming to retain the ease of learning while introducing modern programming paradigms. Its development was influenced by the growing trend of teaching programming in schools and the need for a language that could bridge the gap between beginners and more advanced languages.
curious_fact: Mary was named after the first computer programmer, Ada Lovelace, who foresaw the potential of computers to go beyond calculations.

name: Mathcad
description: Mathcad is a computer algebra system developed by MathSoft, later acquired by PTC. It is designed for engineering, science, and mathematics, offering a WYSIWYG interface for mathematical notation and calculations.
connectionsExplanation: Mathcad evolved from earlier symbolic computation systems and was influenced by the need for a more intuitive and user-friendly approach to mathematical software. It was one of the first systems to popularize the use of a graphical interface for mathematical expressions.
curious_fact: Mathcad was one of the first software tools to allow engineers to document calculations with live mathematical expressions.

name: Mathematica
description: Mathematica is a symbolic computation system developed by Wolfram Research. It is renowned for its ability to perform complex mathematical calculations, data analysis, and visualization. Mathematica is widely used in scientific, engineering, and mathematical fields.
connectionsExplanation: Mathematica was developed by Stephen Wolfram, who was inspired by the concept of a universal computational language. It is not directly derived from any other language but shares paradigms with functional programming languages due to its emphasis on symbolic computation and functional programming constructs.

moons.name: Wolfram Language
moons.description: Wolfram Language is the programming language used in Mathematica, designed for symbolic computation and general programming.

curious_fact: Mathematica was originally called 'Symbolic Mathematics System' and was first released in 1988.

name: Maudesystem
description: Maudesystem is a reflective language based on rewriting logic, designed for specifying and implementing concurrent systems. It is particularly suited for formal verification and analysis of complex systems.
connectionsExplanation: Maudesystem evolved from the earlier Maude language, which was developed at SRI International. It builds upon the principles of rewriting logic to provide a more powerful and flexible framework for concurrent system specification.
curious_fact: Maudesystem is one of the first languages to use rewriting logic, a formalism that combines equational logic and operational semantics.
example:
mod MAUDE is
  sorts Nat .
  subsort Nat < Top .
  op 0 : -> Nat .
  op s : Nat -> Nat .
  vars N M : Nat .
  eq s(N) + M = s(N + M) .
endm

name: Mavka
description: Mavka is a modern, statically-typed programming language designed for educational purposes, particularly for teaching programming to children in Ukraine. It emphasizes simplicity and readability, making it an accessible tool for beginners.
connectionsExplanation: Mavka was developed as part of the Ukrainian educational initiative to introduce programming to young students. It draws inspiration from existing educational languages like Scratch and Python, aiming to provide a gentle learning curve while maintaining a structured approach.
curious_fact: Mavka's name means 'little bird' in Ukrainian, symbolizing the language's approachable and lightweight nature.

name: Max
description: Max is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax and minimalistic approach.
connectionsExplanation: Max is a fictional language created for educational purposes, designed to be simple and easy to learn. It does not have a direct historical predecessor but draws inspiration from modern scripting languages, aiming to introduce programming concepts in an accessible manner.
curious_fact: Max was created to help beginners understand programming concepts without getting overwhelmed by complex syntax.

name: MaxScript
description: MaxScript is a scripting language developed by Autodesk for use with its 3D modeling and animation software, 3ds Max. It allows users to automate tasks, create custom tools, and extend the functionality of the software.
connectionsExplanation: MaxScript is a direct descendant of VisualScript, which was used in earlier versions of 3ds Max. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, making it accessible to both beginners and experienced users in the 3D graphics industry.
curious_fact: MaxScript can be used to create complex animations and effects by scripting the behavior of objects in 3ds Max.

name: Maya Embedded Language
description: Maya Embedded Language (MEL) is a scripting language used for automating tasks within Autodesk Maya, a popular 3D computer animation software. It allows users to create custom tools, automate repetitive tasks, and extend the functionality of Maya.
connectionsExplanation: MEL is derived from the C programming language, which is evident in its syntax and structure. It was developed by Autodesk to provide a more accessible scripting environment for artists and animators who may not be proficient in traditional programming languages.
curious_fact: MEL scripts can be compiled into bytecode for faster execution, which is a unique feature for a scripting language.

name: MeLa
description: MeLa is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is inspired by the Logo programming language but introduces unique features to cater to modern educational needs.
connectionsExplanation: MeLa was created as an educational tool, drawing inspiration from Logo's turtle graphics and command-based approach. While it shares some conceptual similarities with Logo, MeLa introduces new syntax and features to enhance its utility in contemporary educational settings.
curious_fact: MeLa was developed to teach programming concepts through interactive and engaging exercises.

name: Mercury
description: Mercury is a purely functional logic programming language designed for high-performance computing. It is known for its efficiency and strong static typing, making it suitable for applications requiring high reliability and speed.
connectionsExplanation: Mercury evolved from the logic programming paradigm, specifically inspired by Prolog. It was developed at the University of Melbourne in Australia to address the limitations of Prolog in terms of performance and efficiency, aiming to provide a more robust and high-performance alternative.

moons.name: Mercury Compiler
moons.description: The official compiler for the Mercury programming language, providing tools for compiling and executing Mercury programs.

moons.name: Mercury Runtime
moons.description: The runtime environment for Mercury programs, responsible for executing compiled Mercury code efficiently.

curious_fact: Mercury was one of the first languages to introduce determinism annotations, which help the compiler optimize performance by ensuring that functions have predictable behavior.
example: :- module hello. :- interface. :- import_module io. :- pred main(io::di, io::uo) is det. :- implementation. main(!IO) :- io::write_string("Hello, World!\n", !IO).

name: Mesa
description: Mesa is a programming language developed at Xerox PARC in the 1970s. It was designed to be a high-level language for the Xerox Alto computer, which was one of the first personal computers. Mesa introduced several innovative features, including dynamic typing and garbage collection, which influenced later languages like Smalltalk and Java.
connectionsExplanation: Mesa was developed at Xerox PARC and was influenced by the Smalltalk project. It served as a precursor to Smalltalk, which in turn influenced the development of modern object-oriented programming languages.
curious_fact: Mesa was one of the first languages to use dynamic typing and garbage collection in a high-level language.

name: MetEnglish
description: MetEnglish is a fictional programming language designed to explore the intersection of natural language and computational logic. It aims to provide a more intuitive and human-friendly approach to programming by leveraging the richness of the English language.
connectionsExplanation: MetEnglish is inspired by the desire to make programming more accessible to non-technical users. While it draws no direct lineage from existing languages, it shares conceptual similarities with natural language processing (NLP) frameworks and domain-specific languages (DSLs) that aim to bridge the gap between human language and machine understanding.
curious_fact: MetEnglish was created by a team of linguists and computer scientists who believe that programming should be as accessible as writing a story.

name: MetaPost
description: MetaPost is a graphics programming language designed by John D. Hobby in 1984. It is used for creating high-quality PostScript pictures, especially for mathematical and technical illustrations. MetaPost is part of the TeX system and is known for its precision and flexibility in drawing.
connectionsExplanation: MetaPost evolved from the earlier Metafont language, which was also developed by John D. Hobby. Both languages are integral parts of the TeX typesetting system, designed to provide precise control over the appearance of text and graphics. MetaPost extends Metafont's capabilities by adding features for more complex graphics and mathematical illustrations.
curious_fact: MetaPost is written in Web, a literate programming system developed by Donald Knuth.

name: MetaQuotes Language
description: MetaQuotes Language (MQL) is a high-level programming language developed by MetaQuotes Software Corp. for creating trading robots, custom indicators, scripts, and function libraries for use in the MetaTrader trading platform. It is primarily used in the financial markets for automated trading.
connectionsExplanation: MetaQuotes Language was developed specifically for the MetaTrader trading platform, making it a specialized language for financial market automation. It is not derived from any traditional programming language but is designed to be user-friendly for traders and developers in the financial sector.

moons.name: MQL4
moons.description: MQL4 is the fourth version of MetaQuotes Language, introduced in 2009, which added support for object-oriented programming and improved functionality for trading robots.

moons.name: MQL5
moons.description: MQL5 is the fifth version of MetaQuotes Language, released in 2010, which further enhanced the language with C++-like syntax, improved performance, and additional features for trading automation.

curious_fact: MQL was created to democratize trading automation, allowing traders with little programming knowledge to create their own trading robots.

name: Metal Shading Language
description: Metal Shading Language (MSL) is a high-level shading language developed by Apple Inc. for use with Apple's Metal graphics and compute framework. It is designed to provide high performance and fine-grained control over GPU hardware.
connectionsExplanation: Metal Shading Language is a direct evolution of the High-Level Shading Language (HLSL) used in Microsoft's DirectX framework. It was developed to provide a more efficient and flexible shading language for Apple's hardware, particularly for their GPUs. MSL is closely integrated with Metal, allowing developers to write high-performance graphics and compute shaders for Apple's devices.
curious_fact: Metal Shading Language was introduced alongside Metal at WWDC 2014, marking a significant shift in Apple's approach to graphics and compute programming.

name: Metaobject Protocol
description: Metaobject Protocol (MOP) is a feature of some object-oriented programming languages that allows the manipulation of the language's structure and behavior at runtime. It provides a way to define and modify classes, methods, and other language constructs dynamically.
connectionsExplanation: Metaobject Protocol was introduced in the Common Lisp Object System (CLOS) in 1986. It allows for the creation of highly flexible and extensible systems by enabling the programmer to define and modify the language's behavior at runtime. This feature is inspired by the need for more dynamic and customizable object-oriented systems compared to earlier languages.
curious_fact: Metaobject Protocol allows Lisp to be self-modifying, meaning it can change its own structure and behavior during execution.
example:
;; Define a class with a metaclass
(defclass my-class () () (:metaclass my-metaclass))

;; Define a metaclass
(defclass my-metaclass (standard-class) ())

name: MexScript
description: MexScript is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid application development in Mexico. It aims to simplify coding for both beginners and experienced developers by providing a clean syntax and powerful features.
connectionsExplanation: MexScript was developed as a response to the growing need for a more accessible programming language in Mexico. It draws inspiration from JavaScript and Python, combining their ease of use with additional features tailored to the local market.

moons.name: MexScript Framework
moons.description: A comprehensive framework for building web and desktop applications with MexScript.

curious_fact: MexScript was created by a team of Mexican developers who wanted to bridge the gap between local needs and global programming standards.

name: MicroLuaDS
description: MicroLuaDS is a specialized Lua-based scripting language designed for embedded systems and microcontrollers. It focuses on minimalism and efficiency, making it ideal for resource-constrained environments.
connectionsExplanation: MicroLuaDS is derived from Lua, a lightweight scripting language known for its simplicity and embeddability. It inherits Lua's syntax and semantics but introduces optimizations and features tailored for microcontroller applications, such as reduced memory footprint and faster execution.
curious_fact: MicroLuaDS compiles to bytecode that runs on a custom virtual machine optimized for microcontrollers.

name: MicroBlocks
description: MicroBlocks is a visual programming language designed for beginners, particularly children, to learn programming concepts through a block-based interface. It is inspired by Scratch but focuses on simplicity and ease of use, making it accessible for younger audiences.
connectionsExplanation: MicroBlocks is a visual programming language that evolved from the Scratch programming environment, which itself is inspired by Logo. It shares the block-based paradigm but is tailored for younger users, offering a simpler interface and more straightforward learning curve.
curious_fact: MicroBlocks was developed to introduce programming to children as young as 5 years old, focusing on intuitive design and interactive learning.

name: MicroMondes
description: MicroMondes is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by the concept of creating small, self-contained worlds or 'micro-mondes' within the code, making it an engaging tool for beginners.
connectionsExplanation: MicroMondes was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical lineage. It draws inspiration from the broader category of educational programming languages, which aim to simplify complex concepts for beginners.
curious_fact: MicroMondes was developed to teach programming concepts through the creation of miniature digital worlds.

name: Microalg
description: Microalg is a niche, esoteric programming language designed for educational purposes and as a thought experiment in minimalism. It focuses on simplicity and the exploration of basic computational concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Microalg was inspired by the minimalist philosophy of the Forth programming language, aiming to reduce complexity while maintaining functionality. It shares a similar approach to stack-based operations but with a unique syntax and a smaller instruction set.
curious_fact: Microalg was created by a single developer in a weekend as a proof of concept for minimalistic language design.

name: Microsoft BASIC
description: Microsoft BASIC, developed by Microsoft in collaboration with Altair 8800 computer manufacturer, was one of the first high-level programming languages to be widely adopted for personal computers. It was designed to be easy to learn and use, making it accessible to hobbyists and non-programmers.
connectionsExplanation: Microsoft BASIC evolved from Dartmouth BASIC, which was one of the earliest versions of the BASIC programming language. It was developed in the late 1960s at Dartmouth College. Microsoft BASIC was a significant step in the popularization of personal computing, as it came bundled with the Altair 8800, one of the first mass-produced personal computers.

moons.name: QuickBASIC
moons.description: QuickBASIC was an enhanced version of Microsoft BASIC that included an integrated development environment (IDE) and was designed to be more efficient and user-friendly.

moons.name: Visual Basic
moons.description: Visual Basic was a graphical programming environment that allowed users to create Windows applications using BASIC. It was a significant evolution from QuickBASIC and played a crucial role in the development of Windows-based software.

curious_fact: Microsoft BASIC was one of the first programming languages to be sold as a product, rather than being distributed for free.

name: Microsoft C
description: Microsoft C was an early implementation of the C programming language developed by Microsoft in the early 1980s. It was notable for its integration with the MS-DOS operating system and was widely used for developing software for IBM PC and compatible systems.
connectionsExplanation: Microsoft C was developed as an extension of the C language to better suit the needs of the MS-DOS environment. It introduced several features that were not present in the original C language, such as support for segmented memory and interrupt handling, which were essential for programming in the DOS environment.
curious_fact: Microsoft C was one of the first C compilers to support the 8086 processor, which was crucial for the development of early PC software.

name: Microsoft Macro Assembler
description: Microsoft Macro Assembler (MASM) is an assembler developed by Microsoft for the x86 architecture. It is used for writing low-level code and system-level programming, particularly in the development of operating systems and device drivers.
connectionsExplanation: MASM is a direct descendant of the Macro Assembler for the x86 architecture, which was developed by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC) in the 1970s. Microsoft acquired the rights to the assembler and continued its development, making it a key tool for Windows system programming.
curious_fact: MASM is known for its ability to generate highly optimized machine code, making it a popular choice among developers working on performance-critical applications.
example:
section .data
    hello db 'Hello, World!', 0
section .text
global _start
_start:
    mov eax, 4          ; sys_write
    mov ebx, 1          ; file descriptor 1 is stdout
    mov ecx, hello      ; message to write
    mov edx, 13         ; message length
    int 0x80            ; call kernel
    mov eax, 1          ; sys_exit
    xor ebx, ebx        ; exit code 0
    int 0x80            ; call kernel

name: Microsoft Pascal
description: Microsoft Pascal is a version of the Pascal programming language developed by Microsoft in the early 1980s. It was designed to be compatible with UCSD Pascal and was included with the MS-DOS operating system. Microsoft Pascal was widely used in educational settings and for small business applications.
connectionsExplanation: Microsoft Pascal evolved from UCSD Pascal, which was a widely used version of Pascal in educational institutions. Microsoft's version was tailored to work with the MS-DOS environment, making it accessible to a broader audience.
curious_fact: Microsoft Pascal was one of the first programming languages to be included with a personal computer operating system.
example: program HelloWorld; begin writeln('Hello, World!'); end.

name: Microsoft Small Basic
description: Microsoft Small Basic is a beginner-friendly programming language developed by Microsoft. It is designed to introduce programming concepts to beginners and students with a simple and intuitive syntax.
connectionsExplanation: Microsoft Small Basic was created to make programming accessible to beginners. It is inspired by BASIC, a language that has a long history in education. Small Basic simplifies many concepts of programming, making it easier for new learners to understand and experiment with.
curious_fact: Microsoft Small Basic was discontinued in 2010, but its legacy lives on through educational resources and community projects.

name: Microsoft Visual Programming Language
description: Microsoft Visual Programming Language (MVPL) was an early attempt by Microsoft to create a visual programming environment. It aimed to simplify software development by allowing users to create programs using graphical icons and flowcharts rather than traditional text-based code. MVPL was part of Microsoft's efforts to democratize programming and make software development more accessible.
connectionsExplanation: MVPL was developed during a time when Microsoft was exploring various ways to simplify programming for non-technical users. It was influenced by the growing trend of visual programming tools and integrated development environments (IDEs) that were becoming popular in the early 1990s. MVPL was not widely adopted, but it contributed to Microsoft's understanding of user-friendly programming interfaces.
curious_fact: MVPL was one of the first attempts by Microsoft to create a visual programming language, predating modern tools like Visual Basic.
example: Create a new project and drag-and-drop components to design your application.

name: Milk
description: Milk is a domain-specific programming language designed for creating graphical animations and interactive applications. It is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among beginners and hobbyists.
connectionsExplanation: Milk was developed as a tool for creating animations and interactive content, influenced by the need for a language that could handle graphical operations efficiently. It is not directly derived from any other language but shares some paradigms with scripting languages like JavaScript and Python.

moons.name: Milk Graphics
moons.description: A library for handling graphical operations within the Milk language.

moons.name: Milk Interactivity
moons.description: A module for adding interactive elements to Milk applications.

curious_fact: Milk was one of the first languages to popularize the use of 'milk syntax' for animations.

name: Mind
description: Mind is an esoteric programming language designed to simulate human thought processes. It uses a unique syntax and paradigm that mimics cognitive functions, making it highly abstract and challenging to understand.
connectionsExplanation: Mind was created as an exploration into the nature of consciousness and computation. While it does not have a direct lineage from traditional programming languages, it draws inspiration from cognitive science and artificial intelligence theories.
curious_fact: Mind is one of the few programming languages that attempts to model human thought patterns, making it a unique tool for studying cognitive processes.

name: MiniZinc
description: MiniZinc is a high-level constraint modeling language designed for specifying constraint satisfaction and optimization problems. It is widely used in academia and industry for solving complex combinatorial problems.
connectionsExplanation: MiniZinc evolved from the Zinc modeling language, which was developed as part of the Gecode constraint programming system. MiniZinc aims to provide a more user-friendly and expressive syntax while maintaining the power and flexibility of Zinc.

moons.name: Chuffed
moons.description: A constraint solver that supports MiniZinc.

moons.name: Gecode
moons.description: A constraint programming toolkit that supports MiniZinc.

curious_fact: MiniZinc was developed to make constraint programming more accessible to non-experts.
example: var int: x; constraint x >= 0; solve satisfy;

name: MinimalBASIC
description: MinimalBASIC is a simplified version of the BASIC programming language, designed to be minimalistic and easy to learn. It was created to introduce beginners to programming with a reduced feature set compared to standard BASIC.
connectionsExplanation: MinimalBASIC evolved from the original BASIC language, which was developed in the late 1960s. It retains the core principles of BASIC while removing unnecessary features to focus on simplicity and ease of use.
curious_fact: MinimalBASIC was one of the first languages to introduce structured programming concepts to beginners.

name: Mirah
description: Mirah is a dynamic, optionally-typed programming language that compiles to Java bytecode. It aims to provide a more concise and expressive syntax compared to Java, while maintaining compatibility with the Java ecosystem.
connectionsExplanation: Mirah was created by Charles Nutter, one of the core developers of JRuby. It draws inspiration from Ruby and aims to offer a similar syntax and dynamic features while targeting the Java Virtual Machine (JVM). This makes it a direct descendant of Ruby, with the added benefit of Java's extensive libraries and platform support.
curious_fact: Mirah was designed to be a bridge between Ruby's dynamic nature and Java's performance and ecosystem.

name: Miranda
description: Miranda is a purely functional programming language developed by David Turner in 1985. It was designed to be a small, elegant language that emphasizes simplicity and correctness. Miranda is known for its lazy evaluation and strong static typing, making it a popular choice for teaching functional programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Miranda is a direct descendant of David Turner's earlier work on the programming language KRC, which was itself inspired by the lambda calculus. Miranda's design was influenced by the need for a language that could be used to teach functional programming principles effectively.
curious_fact: Miranda was one of the first languages to popularize the use of lazy evaluation in functional programming.

name: Misty
description: Misty is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax that emphasizes readability and intuitive design.
connectionsExplanation: Misty was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts without the complexities of existing languages. It draws inspiration from Python's syntax and JavaScript's dynamic nature, aiming to bridge the gap between simple and powerful programming paradigms.
curious_fact: Misty was developed by a team of educators who wanted to create a language that could be learned in a single semester.

name: Mizar
description: Mizar is a formal language and proof-checking system for writing mathematical proofs. It is based on first-order logic and set theory, and it is used primarily in academia for formalizing mathematical proofs.
connectionsExplanation: Mizar was developed as part of the Mizar Project at the University of Warsaw in Poland. It is inspired by the work of mathematicians and logicians who sought to formalize mathematical proofs to ensure their correctness. Mizar is a direct descendant of the logical systems developed in the 20th century, particularly those that aimed to provide a rigorous foundation for mathematics.
curious_fact: Mizar is one of the oldest formal proof languages still in use today.

name: MobileBASIC
description: MobileBASIC is a BASIC dialect designed for mobile devices, particularly for use on PDAs and early smartphones. It allows developers to create applications with a simple syntax, making it accessible for beginners.
connectionsExplanation: MobileBASIC evolved from traditional BASIC languages, adapting its syntax and features to suit the constraints and capabilities of mobile devices in the late 1990s and early 2000s.
curious_fact: MobileBASIC was one of the first languages to bring BASIC to mobile platforms, enabling users to write applications without needing a full-fledged development environment.

name: Mocklisp
description: Mocklisp is a fictional Lisp-like programming language designed for educational purposes. It mimics the syntax and structure of Lisp but introduces unique features for teaching programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Mocklisp is inspired by Lisp, a pioneer language from the 1950s. It shares the functional programming paradigm and list-based data structures, making it a suitable educational tool for introducing Lisp concepts in a simplified manner.
curious_fact: Mocklisp was created to help beginners understand Lisp without overwhelming them with its complexity.

name: Modelica
description: Modelica is a high-level, object-oriented, equation-based language for modeling complex physical systems. It is particularly suited for multi-domain modeling and simulation.
connectionsExplanation: Modelica was developed by the Modelica Association, an international organization, and it is based on the principles of object-oriented modeling. It is designed to facilitate the modeling of complex systems by allowing users to define models using a combination of equations and object-oriented constructs.

moons.name: OpenModelica
moons.description: An open-source Modelica development environment.

moons.name: Dymola
moons.description: A commercial Modelica development environment.

curious_fact: Modelica was named after the Swedish physicist and engineer Carl Wilhelm Scheele, who discovered oxygen.

name: Modula
description: Modula is a family of programming languages developed by Niklaus Wirth. It is known for its structured programming approach and modular design, which influenced many subsequent languages. Modula-2 is the most well-known version.
connectionsExplanation: Modula was developed by Niklaus Wirth, who also created Pascal. It is considered a direct descendant of Pascal, as it shares many of its design principles and syntax. Modula's modular approach and structured programming concepts have had a lasting impact on the development of modern programming languages.

moons.name: Modula-2
moons.description: Modula-2 is an extension of Modula, adding more features and improving the language's capabilities. It is widely used in systems programming and is known for its clarity and efficiency.

moons.name: Modula-3
moons.description: Modula-3 is a successor to Modula-2, designed to support modern programming paradigms and improve upon the language's strengths. It includes features like garbage collection and exception handling.

curious_fact: Modula was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of modules, which are self-contained units of code that can be compiled separately and linked together.
example: MODULE HelloWorld; IMPORT Out; PROCEDURE Main; BEGIN Out.String("Hello, World!"); Out.Ln; END Main; END HelloWorld.

name: Modula-2
description: Modula-2 is a structured programming language developed by Niklaus Wirth. It is known for its simplicity and efficiency, and it was designed to be a successor to Pascal. Modula-2 introduced the concept of modules, which allowed for better code organization and encapsulation.
connectionsExplanation: Modula-2 was developed by Niklaus Wirth as a successor to Pascal. It introduced the concept of modules, which significantly influenced the development of later languages such as Modula-3 and Ada. The modular approach in Modula-2 laid the groundwork for modern software engineering practices.
curious_fact: Modula-2 was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of modules, which is now a standard feature in many modern programming languages.

name: Modula-2+
description: Modula-2+ is an extended version of Modula-2, developed to enhance the language's capabilities for system programming and software development. It introduces additional features and optimizations to improve performance and usability.
connectionsExplanation: Modula-2+ is a direct descendant of Modula-2, which was developed by Niklaus Wirth at ETH Zurich in the early 1980s. It builds upon the structured programming principles of Modula-2, adding new features to address limitations and improve the language's utility in modern software development.
curious_fact: Modula-2+ was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of modules with separate compilation, significantly influencing modern programming paradigms.

name: Modula-3
description: Modula-3 is a systems programming language developed at the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology (ETH Zurich) in the late 1980s. It is designed to be a successor to Modula-2, with a focus on safety, modularity, and efficiency. Modula-3 is statically typed and supports object-oriented programming, exception handling, and garbage collection.
connectionsExplanation: Modula-3 evolved from Modula-2, which itself was inspired by Pascal. It was designed to address the limitations of Modula-2 and incorporate modern programming paradigms. The language was developed as part of the Modula-3 project, which aimed to create a robust and efficient systems programming language for the next generation of computing environments.

moons.name: Concurrent Modula-3
moons.description: An extension of Modula-3 that adds support for concurrent programming.

moons.name: Limbo
moons.description: A systems programming language developed at Bell Labs, inspired by Modula-3.

curious_fact: Modula-3 was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of exception handling as a first-class language feature.
example: MODULE HelloWorld; IMPORT IO; PROCEDURE Main(); BEGIN IO.Put("Hello, World!\n"); END Main; END HelloWorld.

name: Mojo
description: Mojo is a high-performance, statically typed programming language designed for scientific computing and machine learning. It aims to provide a seamless integration with existing Python libraries while offering native performance.
connectionsExplanation: Mojo is inspired by the need for high-performance computing in scientific and machine learning domains. It draws from the paradigms of statically typed languages like C++ and Rust, while also incorporating dynamic features from Python. This makes it a unique language that bridges the gap between high-level and low-level programming.

moons.name: MojoML
moons.description: A machine learning library for Mojo.

moons.name: MojoSci
moons.description: A scientific computing library for Mojo.

curious_fact: Mojo was developed by a team of researchers at Stanford University to address the performance limitations of existing Python-based scientific computing frameworks.

name: Mondrian
description: Mondrian is a fictional programming language inspired by the Dutch painter Piet Mondrian's works. It is designed to be visually appealing and focuses on simplicity and minimalism in both syntax and execution.
connectionsExplanation: Mondrian was created as a thought experiment to explore the intersection of art and programming. While it has no direct historical predecessors, it draws inspiration from the structured and clean aesthetic of early programming languages like FORTRAN and COBOL.
curious_fact: Mondrian's syntax is based on the primary colors and lines found in Mondrian's paintings, with commands like 'red', 'blue', and 'yellow' representing colors and 'line' for drawing lines.
example:
red
line
blue
line
yellow
line

name: MonkeyX
description: MonkeyX is a programming language designed for game development, offering a simple syntax and easy integration with various game engines. It is particularly popular among hobbyists and small teams looking to create games without the complexity of larger game development environments.
connectionsExplanation: MonkeyX is inspired by BASIC and is designed to be accessible for beginners. It evolved from the Monkey programming language, which was itself inspired by BlitzBasic. The language's simplicity and focus on game development make it a unique entry in the programming landscape.

moons.name: Monkey2
moons.description: Monkey2 is an updated version of MonkeyX, offering improved performance and additional features for game development.

curious_fact: MonkeyX was created by Mark Sibly, who also developed the original Monkey language.

name: MoonScript
description: MoonScript is a lightweight scripting language that compiles to Lua. It aims to provide a more readable and concise syntax while maintaining the performance and flexibility of Lua.
connectionsExplanation: MoonScript was created to enhance the readability of Lua code by introducing a more Python-like syntax. It compiles directly to Lua, allowing developers to leverage the performance of Lua while benefiting from a cleaner syntax.
curious_fact: MoonScript was inspired by languages like Python and CoffeeScript, aiming to make Lua more accessible to developers familiar with these languages.

name: Mosellanguage
description: Mosellanguage is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It emphasizes simplicity and readability, making it an ideal choice for beginners. Despite its fictional nature, it incorporates elements from various real-world programming paradigms to provide a comprehensive learning experience.
connectionsExplanation: Mosellanguage was created as an educational tool to introduce programming concepts in a straightforward manner. While it does not have a direct historical predecessor, it draws inspiration from several paradigms, including procedural and object-oriented programming, to offer a well-rounded learning experience.
curious_fact: Mosellanguage was developed by a team of educators aiming to simplify complex programming concepts for students.

name: Motoko
description: Motoko is a programming language developed by the Internet Computer Foundation (ICF) for building decentralized applications on the Internet Computer blockchain. It is designed to be safe, efficient, and easy to use, with a focus on security and performance.
connectionsExplanation: Motoko was developed as part of the Internet Computer project, which aims to create a decentralized cloud platform. It is inspired by modern programming languages like JavaScript and Rust, combining their features to provide a robust environment for blockchain applications.
curious_fact: Motoko is one of the first languages specifically designed for blockchain applications, emphasizing security and immutability.
example: actor Hello { public func greet(name : Text) : async Text { return "Hello, " # name # "!"; } }

name: Mouse
description: Mouse is a minimalist programming language designed by Charles H. Moore in 1979. It is known for its simplicity and minimal instruction set, making it suitable for educational purposes and as a base for other languages.
connectionsExplanation: Mouse is considered a precursor to Forth, as both languages emphasize simplicity and minimalism. Moore's design philosophy in Mouse influenced the development of Forth, which is known for its stack-based architecture and simplicity.
curious_fact: Mouse was originally designed to be a language for teaching programming concepts to children.

name: Move
description: Move is a programming language designed for developing distributed systems, particularly for the Facebook platform. It is a statically typed language that compiles to C++ and is used for building scalable and efficient services.
connectionsExplanation: Move was developed by Facebook to address the challenges of building large-scale distributed systems. It is inspired by languages like C++ and Java but is tailored to Facebook's specific needs, such as safety and performance in distributed environments.
curious_fact: Move was created to ensure safety and security in Facebook's distributed systems, incorporating features like memory safety and type safety.
example: module Hello { public fun main() { println(&"Hello, World!"); } }

name: Mozart Programming System
description: Mozart is a concurrent constraint programming system developed at the Technical University of Vienna. It is designed for writing concurrent and distributed applications, particularly in the field of artificial intelligence and real-time systems.
connectionsExplanation: Mozart evolved from earlier constraint programming systems and is influenced by the Oz programming language. It extends the capabilities of constraint logic programming with support for concurrency and distribution, making it suitable for complex, real-time applications.
curious_fact: Mozart was named after Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, reflecting its sophisticated and elegant design.

name: Muddle
description: Muddle is a programming language developed in the early 1970s at the University of Cambridge. It was designed to be a simple and easy-to-learn language for teaching programming concepts. Despite its simplicity, Muddle has a unique syntax and features that set it apart from other languages of its time.
connectionsExplanation: Muddle was developed as a teaching language at the University of Cambridge in the early 1970s. It was influenced by the desire to create a language that was accessible to beginners while still being powerful enough for educational purposes. Muddle's design reflects the paradigms of the time, particularly in its approach to simplicity and clarity.
curious_fact: Muddle was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of structured programming in a teaching context.

name: Mulan
description: Mulan is a fictional programming language inspired by the Chinese epic poem 'The Ballad of Mulan.' It is designed to be a playful and educational language, focusing on simplicity and cultural references.
connectionsExplanation: Mulan is inspired by the cultural significance of the 'Ballad of Mulan' in Chinese history, which tells the story of a young woman who disguises herself as a man to serve in the military. This inspiration is reflected in the language's design and syntax, which aim to be accessible and engaging for learners.
curious_fact: Mulan's syntax includes commands like 'hooray' and 'yay' to encourage positive reinforcement during coding.

name: MultiLisp
description: MultiLisp is an extension of Common Lisp designed to support multiple programming paradigms, including object-oriented programming. It was developed to address the limitations of traditional Lisp in handling complex software systems.
connectionsExplanation: MultiLisp evolved from Common Lisp, which itself is a direct descendant of Lisp. It was created to enhance Lisp's capabilities by integrating object-oriented features, making it more versatile for large-scale software development.
curious_fact: MultiLisp was one of the first Lisps to support object-oriented programming, predating languages like CLOS.
example:
;; MultiLisp Hello World
(defclass hello-world ()
  ((message :initform "Hello, World!")))

(defmethod print-message ((hw hello-world))
  (format t "~a~%" (slot-value hw 'message)))

(let ((hw (make-instance 'hello-world)))
  (print-message hw))

name: MusicMacroLanguage
description: MusicMacroLanguage is a specialized programming language designed to facilitate the composition and manipulation of music through macro-based programming. It allows musicians and composers to automate repetitive tasks and create complex musical structures with ease.
connectionsExplanation: MusicMacroLanguage was developed in the early 1980s as a tool for musicians to enhance their creative process. It was inspired by the need to automate repetitive tasks in music composition, drawing parallels with macro languages used in text processing. While not widely adopted, it represents an early attempt to integrate programming concepts into music creation.
curious_fact: MusicMacroLanguage was one of the first attempts to use programming for music composition, predating modern digital audio workstations.

name: Music Markup Language
description: Music Markup Language (MML) is a text-based language designed to represent musical scores in a human-readable format. It allows musicians and composers to encode music using a simple syntax that can be easily parsed by software.
connectionsExplanation: Music Markup Language was developed as a way to standardize the representation of musical scores, making it easier for software to process and display music. It is inspired by the simplicity and readability of HTML, adapting its principles to the domain of music notation.
curious_fact: MML uses a compact syntax to represent musical notes, making it ideal for embedding music in web pages and other digital media.
example: T120 L8 C4 E4 G4 C5

name: Myrddin
