description: Myrddin is a statically typed, compiled systems programming language designed for performance and simplicity. It is known for its minimal syntax and efficient execution, making it suitable for low-level programming tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Myrddin was created by Sean Barrett as a successor to the C programming language, aiming to provide a more modern and safer alternative. It draws inspiration from C but introduces several improvements and simplifications in syntax and type system.
curious_fact: Myrddin's name is a reference to the Welsh word for 'magic', reflecting its innovative approach to programming.

name: NARS2000
description: NARS2000 is a non-monotonic logic system designed for reasoning under uncertainty. It is based on the principles of Non-Axiomatic Reasoning System (NARS) and is used in artificial intelligence and cognitive modeling.
connectionsExplanation: NARS2000 is a modern evolution of the NARS system, which was developed in the 1990s. It builds upon the principles of non-monotonic logic to provide a more robust framework for reasoning under uncertainty, making it a significant advancement in the field of artificial intelligence.
curious_fact: NARS2000 is capable of learning and adapting its reasoning processes based on new information, making it a powerful tool for dynamic environments.
example: open NARS2000; run; (input reasoning tasks here)

name: NATURAL
description: NATURAL is a high-level programming language developed by Software AG in 1964. It is designed for business applications and is known for its English-like syntax, making it accessible to non-programmers. NATURAL is widely used in Europe, particularly in Germany, for developing business software.
connectionsExplanation: NATURAL was developed in the 1960s as part of the early efforts to create programming languages that were more accessible to non-technical users. It was influenced by the desire to reduce the complexity of programming and to make it more intuitive for those without a strong technical background. NATURAL's syntax is designed to resemble natural language, which was a significant departure from the more formal and technical syntax of earlier programming languages.
curious_fact: NATURAL was one of the first programming languages to use English-like syntax, making it easier for non-programmers to understand and use.

name: NCAR Command Language
description: NCAR Command Language (NCL) is a scripting language developed by the National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR) for analyzing and visualizing meteorological and oceanographic data. It is designed to be user-friendly and efficient for scientific data processing.
connectionsExplanation: NCL was developed as a specialized tool for atmospheric and oceanographic research, building upon earlier scripting languages used in scientific computing. Its primary purpose is to provide a powerful and flexible environment for data analysis and visualization, particularly in the field of meteorology.
curious_fact: NCL is known for its ability to handle large datasets and produce high-quality scientific visualizations.

name: NCL

It is built on top of the NCAR Graphics libraries and is designed to work seamlessly with data from various scientific instruments and models.

name: NEAT
description: NEAT (NeuroEvolution of Augmenting Topologies) is a genetic algorithm for the evolution of artificial neural networks. It is particularly known for its ability to evolve complex topologies and has been applied in various fields such as robotics and game AI.
connectionsExplanation: NEAT is inspired by the principles of natural evolution, specifically the evolution of neural networks. It was developed by Kenneth Stanley and Risto Miikkulainen at the University of Texas at Austin. NEAT builds upon the concept of genetic algorithms but introduces mechanisms to evolve the structure of neural networks, not just their weights.
curious_fact: NEAT was one of the first algorithms to demonstrate the evolution of complex neural network topologies, leading to breakthroughs in artificial intelligence research.

name: NESFab

name: NESL

connectionsExplanation: NESL was developed as part of the Carnegie Mellon University's work on parallel computing. It was influenced by the need to create a language that could express parallel algorithms in a clear and concise manner, drawing inspiration from functional programming paradigms.
curious_fact: NESL was one of the first languages to introduce nested data parallelism, allowing for more efficient parallel computation.
example: fun map(f, xs) = if null xs then [] else f(head xs) :: map(f, tail xs)

name: NPL

name: NPR

name: NSBasic

name: NWScript

name: NeoSign0fMisery

name: Nessus Attack Scripting Language
description: Nessus Attack Scripting Language (Nasl) is a scripting language developed by Tenable Network Security for writing security checks and plugins for the Nessus vulnerability scanner. It is designed to be easy to use and allows users to automate security assessments.

It is not directly derived from any other language but was created to integrate seamlessly with the Nessus platform, allowing for the creation of custom security checks.
curious_fact: Nasl scripts are executed on the Nessus scanner itself, which can sometimes lead to performance issues if not optimized.

name: Net.Data
description: Net.Data is a programming language developed by Microsoft in the mid-1990s. It was designed to simplify the creation of dynamic web content and was part of the Microsoft Internet Information Server (IIS) suite. Net.Data was intended to be a more accessible alternative to traditional server-side scripting languages like ASP.
connectionsExplanation: Net.Data was developed by Microsoft as part of their strategy to dominate the web server market in the mid-1990s. It was designed to be more accessible to web developers who were not familiar with complex server-side scripting languages. Net.Data was a precursor to ASP.NET, which was introduced later and became more popular.
curious_fact: Net.Data was one of the early attempts by Microsoft to provide a simpler way to create dynamic web pages, predating ASP.NET.

name: NetLogo
description: NetLogo is a multi-agent programmable modeling environment for simulating natural and social phenomena. It is particularly suited for modeling complex systems and agent-based simulations.

curious_fact: NetLogo's 'turtle' metaphor allows users to think about agents moving around in a space, making it intuitive for simulating behaviors in nature and society.

name: NetRexx
description: NetRexx is a high-level programming language that combines the ease of use of Rexx with the power and efficiency of Java. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, with a syntax that is similar to natural language. NetRexx is often used for scripting and automation tasks.

name: Netwide Assembler
description: Netwide Assembler (NASM) is an 80x86 assembler designed for portability and modularity. It is widely used for writing low-level system software and drivers.
connectionsExplanation: NASM was developed to provide a more flexible and efficient alternative to existing assemblers. It supports multiple object file formats and is known for its syntax that is similar to Intel syntax, making it popular among developers working with x86 architectures.
curious_fact: NASM is written in C and assembly, showcasing its own assembly capabilities.
example:
section .data
    hello db 'Hello, World!', 0
section .text
global _start
_start:
    mov eax, 4          ; sys_write
    mov ebx, 1          ; file descriptor 1 is stdout
    mov ecx, hello      ; message to write
    mov edx, 13         ; message length
    int 0x80            ; call kernel
    mov eax, 1          ; sys_exit
    xor ebx, ebx        ; exit code 0
    int 0x80            ; call kernel

name: Newspeak

Newsqueak's design principles, such as simplicity and efficiency, have influenced the development of languages like Go.

name: OBJ

name: OBJ2

name: OBJ3
