moons.description: A high-performance system programming language developed by Apple.

moons.name: Objective-C
moons.description: A general-purpose, object-oriented programming language that adds Smalltalk-style messaging to the C programming language.

curious_fact: Source was introduced as part of Apple's efforts to modernize its software development ecosystem.

name: SpaceProgrammingLanguage
description: SpaceProgrammingLanguage is a fictional programming language designed for space exploration and interstellar communication. It emphasizes efficiency and compactness, making it ideal for environments with limited resources.
connectionsExplanation: SpaceProgrammingLanguage was created as a specialized language for space missions, drawing inspiration from existing low-level programming languages like Assembly and C. Its design focuses on minimizing code size and maximizing performance, which are crucial for long-distance communication and resource-constrained environments.
curious_fact: SpaceProgrammingLanguage uses a unique binary-based syntax that can be transmitted over long distances with minimal error.

name: Speakeasy
description: Speakeasy is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It was created in the early 1960s to introduce beginners to programming concepts without the complexity of existing languages.
connectionsExplanation: Speakeasy was developed as an educational tool in the 1960s, aiming to simplify programming for beginners. While it is not directly derived from any specific language, it shares some conceptual similarities with early educational languages like BASIC, which were also designed to be accessible to newcomers.
curious_fact: Speakeasy was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'speaking' to computers, allowing users to input commands in a more natural language format.

name: Speedcoding
description: Speedcoding is a fictional programming language designed for rapid development and execution. It is inspired by the need for ultra-fast coding in competitive programming and software development challenges.
connectionsExplanation: Speedcoding was created as a hypothetical language to explore the concept of ultra-fast coding. While it is not based on any existing language, it draws inspiration from paradigms seen in languages like C++ and Python, aiming to combine their performance and ease of use.
curious_fact: Speedcoding compiles directly to machine code, offering near-instantaneous execution.

name: Spice
description: Spice is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by BASIC and Python, aiming to introduce beginners to programming concepts in a fun and interactive way.
connectionsExplanation: Spice was created as an educational tool to make programming accessible to beginners. While it draws inspiration from BASIC and Python, it introduces unique features to engage young learners and encourage creativity.
curious_fact: Spice includes a built-in game engine to teach programming through play.

name: SpiceLisp
description: SpiceLisp is a modern dialect of Lisp designed for high-performance computing and rapid prototyping. It introduces several optimizations and extensions to the traditional Lisp model, making it particularly suitable for scientific and engineering applications.
connectionsExplanation: SpiceLisp evolved from Common Lisp, inheriting its powerful macro system and dynamic typing. It aims to bridge the gap between Lisp's flexibility and the performance needs of contemporary computing environments.
curious_fact: SpiceLisp was developed by a team of researchers at MIT to enhance Lisp's capabilities in parallel processing.

name: SpiderBasic
description: SpiderBasic is a high-level programming language designed for Windows, Linux, and macOS. It is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among beginners and hobbyists. The language is derived from the BASIC family and is designed to be intuitive and accessible.
connectionsExplanation: SpiderBasic evolved from the BASIC programming language family, which has a long history dating back to the 1960s. It is designed to be accessible and easy to learn, making it suitable for beginners and hobbyists. The language's syntax is similar to other BASIC dialects, which allows for a smooth transition for those familiar with BASIC.
curious_fact: SpiderBasic was developed by a single individual, making it a unique example of a language created by an individual rather than a team.

name: Split-C
description: Split-C is a fictional programming language designed to explore the concept of splitting code execution into multiple parallel streams. It is inspired by modern concurrency models and aims to simplify parallel programming for developers.
connectionsExplanation: Split-C was inspired by the need to address the complexities of parallel programming in modern computing environments. It draws conceptual parallels with languages like C and Rust, which emphasize performance and control over system resources.
curious_fact: Split-C introduced the 'split' keyword, allowing developers to divide code execution into independent streams.
example: int main() { split { printf("Hello"); } split { printf(" World"); } return 0; }

name: Spoon
description: Spoon is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It emphasizes simplicity and readability, making it an ideal language for beginners to learn programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Spoon was created as an educational tool to introduce programming concepts in a straightforward manner. It is not derived from any existing language but was inspired by the simplicity of BASIC and the structured approach of Pascal.
curious_fact: Spoon was invented by a group of educators to make programming accessible to children.

name: Squeak
description: Squeak is an open-source, object-oriented programming environment and runtime system. It is based on the Smalltalk programming language and is known for its live coding capabilities and graphical user interface. Squeak is often used in educational settings and for rapid prototyping.
connectionsExplanation: Squeak is a direct descendant of Smalltalk, which was developed in the 1970s at Xerox PARC. It inherits Smalltalk's object-oriented paradigm and live coding environment, making it a significant evolution in the Smalltalk lineage.

moons.name: Pharo
moons.description: Pharo is a modern continuation of the Squeak project, focusing on performance and modern development practices.

moons.name: VisualWorks
moons.description: VisualWorks is a commercial Smalltalk environment that has influenced and been influenced by Squeak.

curious_fact: Squeak runs entirely in a virtual machine, allowing it to be highly portable across different operating systems.

name: Squirrel
description: Squirrel is a high-level imperative programming language designed for embedding into applications. It is lightweight and fast, making it suitable for game development and scripting.
connectionsExplanation: Squirrel was developed by Alberto Demichelis as a lightweight scripting language for game development. It is inspired by C and Lua, aiming to provide a balance between performance and ease of use. Squirrel's design allows it to be easily integrated into existing applications, making it a popular choice for game scripting.

moons.name: Nutella
moons.description: A popular extension for Squirrel that adds support for JSON and HTTP requests.

curious_fact: Squirrel's name comes from the nut, reflecting its lightweight and efficient nature.

name: StaDyn
description: StaDyn is a statically and dynamically typed programming language designed for high-performance computing. It combines the safety and predictability of static typing with the flexibility of dynamic typing, making it suitable for both systems programming and rapid application development.
connectionsExplanation: StaDyn was developed as an evolution of modern statically typed languages like C++ and Java, aiming to address the performance bottlenecks and development speed issues found in these languages. It draws inspiration from the dynamic typing features of languages like Python and Ruby, while maintaining strong static type checking to ensure robustness and performance.

moons.name: StaDyn-Web
moons.description: A web framework for building high-performance web applications using StaDyn.

moons.name: StaDyn-DB
moons.description: A database library for StaDyn, providing efficient data storage and retrieval mechanisms.

curious_fact: StaDyn was created by a team of researchers from Stanford University and Google, aiming to bridge the gap between static and dynamic typing.

name: Stackless Python
description: Stackless Python is a variant of the Python programming language that implements microthreads, also known as 'tasklets', which are lightweight threads that do not require a separate stack. This makes it suitable for high-concurrency applications without the overhead of traditional threads.
connectionsExplanation: Stackless Python evolved from the standard Python language, specifically Python 2.2. It was developed to address the limitations of the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) in the standard Python implementation, allowing for more efficient concurrency. The project was initiated by Christian Tismer and others in the Python community.
curious_fact: Stackless Python was one of the inspirations for the development of the Go programming language, which also emphasizes lightweight concurrency.

name: Stagecast Creator
description: Stagecast Creator is a visual programming environment designed for children and beginners. It allows users to create animations and interactive stories by dragging and dropping objects and programming their behaviors. The language is based on a simple, block-based syntax that represents commands and actions.
connectionsExplanation: Stagecast Creator was developed by Parallax Software in 1996. It was one of the early educational programming tools aimed at introducing children to programming concepts through visual and interactive means. The language's design was inspired by LOGO, which used a turtle graphics approach to teach programming, but Stagecast Creator extended this concept to include more complex interactions and animations.
curious_fact: Stagecast Creator was one of the first tools to introduce children to programming through visual interfaces, influencing later educational programming languages like Scratch.

name: Stan
description: Stan is a probabilistic programming language designed for statistical inference. It is particularly suited for Bayesian inference and is widely used in academia and industry for statistical modeling and data analysis.
connectionsExplanation: Stan was developed as a successor to JAGS and WinBUGS, aiming to provide a more efficient and flexible platform for Bayesian analysis. It is built on top of the C++ library Eigen and uses automatic differentiation to compute gradients, which is a key feature for efficient Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampling.

moons.name: PyStan
moons.description: Python interface for Stan.

moons.name: RStan
moons.description: R interface for Stan.

moons.name: CmdStan
moons.description: Command-line interface for Stan.

curious_fact: Stan's name comes from the Stanislaus River, which flows through the campus of Stanford University where the language was developed.

name: Standard ML
description: Standard ML (SML) is a general-purpose, purely functional programming language developed by Robin Milner and others at the University of Edinburgh. It is known for its strong static type system and formal semantics, making it a popular choice for teaching and research in programming language theory.
connectionsExplanation: Standard ML evolved from earlier functional programming languages like ML and Lisp. It was designed to be a more robust and standardized version of ML, incorporating ideas from both functional and imperative paradigms. The language's formal semantics and strong type system have influenced many modern functional programming languages.

moons.name: SML/NJ
moons.description: The Standard ML of New Jersey implementation is one of the most widely used compilers for Standard ML.

moons.name: Moscow ML
moons.description: Moscow ML is another popular implementation of Standard ML, known for its efficiency and portability.

curious_fact: Standard ML was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of polymorphic types, which allows functions to operate on multiple types.
example: fun factorial 0 = 1 | factorial n = n * factorial (n - 1);

name: Standard ML of New Jersey
description: Standard ML of New Jersey (SML/NJ) is a popular implementation of the Standard ML programming language. It is known for its robust type system and is widely used in academic settings and research.
connectionsExplanation: SML/NJ is a direct descendant of Standard ML, which was developed in the early 1980s. It was one of the first implementations to popularize the language and has since been influential in the development of other functional programming languages.

moons.name: MLton
moons.description: MLton is a whole-program optimizing Standard ML compiler.

moons.name: MLKit
moons.description: MLKit is a Standard ML to C compiler.

curious_fact: SML/NJ was developed at Princeton University and has been influential in the field of functional programming.
example: fun factorial 0 = 1 | factorial n = n * factorial (n - 1);

name: Stanford Artificial Intelligence Language
description: Stanford Artificial Intelligence Language (SAIL) was a Lisp-based programming language developed at Stanford University in the 1970s. It was designed to support artificial intelligence research and development, providing advanced features for symbolic computation and data manipulation.
connectionsExplanation: SAIL was developed as a direct descendant of Lisp, which was one of the pioneering programming languages of the 1950s. It evolved to meet the specific needs of AI research, incorporating features that facilitated symbolic processing and knowledge representation.
curious_fact: SAIL was one of the first languages to support garbage collection and dynamic typing, which were revolutionary for its time.

name: Stanislaus
description: Stanislaus is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It emphasizes simplicity and readability, making it an ideal language for beginners to learn the fundamentals of programming.
connectionsExplanation: Stanislaus was created as an educational tool to introduce programming concepts in a straightforward manner. It draws inspiration from modern programming languages like Python and JavaScript, focusing on ease of use and intuitive syntax.
curious_fact: Stanislaus was named after the fictional character Stanislaus P. McFeely from the 'Programming Adventures' series.

name: StarLogo
description: StarLogo is a programming language and modeling environment designed for exploring the behavior of decentralized systems, such as ant colonies, bird flocks, and traffic jams. It is particularly suited for educational purposes, allowing users to create simulations of complex systems through simple rules.
connectionsExplanation: StarLogo was developed at the MIT Media Lab in the early 1990s. It was inspired by Logo, a language designed for children to learn programming, but StarLogo extended Logo's capabilities to model multi-agent systems. This makes it a direct descendant of Logo, with the primary goal of facilitating the study of emergent phenomena in artificial life and complex systems.
curious_fact: StarLogo was one of the first programming languages to popularize the concept of agent-based modeling, which is now widely used in various scientific disciplines.

name: Starlark
description: Starlark is a simple, Python-like language designed for configuration and automation. It is used primarily in Google's build system, Bazel, to define build rules and tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Starlark was developed by Google as an extension language for Bazel. It is inspired by Python, sharing many of its syntax and features, but is designed to be safe and efficient for use in build systems.
curious_fact: Starlark is designed to be a small language with a minimal standard library, focusing on simplicity and safety.

name: Stata
description: Stata is a statistical software package used in the fields of economics, sociology, political science, and epidemiology. It is known for its comprehensive set of statistical tools and its user-friendly interface.
connectionsExplanation: Stata was developed by StataCorp, a company founded by William Gould, Robert Hall, and J. Joseph Newton. It is not directly derived from any other language but shares some similarities with other statistical languages like R and SAS due to its focus on data analysis.

moons.name: Stata/MP
moons.description: A version of Stata designed for multi-core processors.

moons.name: Stata/SE
moons.description: A version of Stata with a larger dataset capacity.

curious_fact: Stata was originally developed on a Macintosh SE and later ported to other platforms.

name: Stateflow
description: Stateflow is a graphical programming environment for modeling and simulating state machines and hydraulic systems. It is widely used in the automotive, aerospace, and industrial automation industries for developing control algorithms.
connectionsExplanation: Stateflow was developed by The MathWorks, Inc., and is tightly integrated with MATLAB and Simulink. It extends the capabilities of these tools by providing a visual interface for designing state machines and flow charts, which are essential for control system development.
curious_fact: Stateflow introduced the concept of hierarchical state machines, allowing for more complex and modular control logic.

name: Stencyl
description: Stencyl is a visual game development platform that allows users to create 2D games without writing code. It uses a drag-and-drop interface and a visual scripting system, making it accessible to beginners and hobbyists.
connectionsExplanation: Stencyl was developed by Amanieu d'Anjou and released in 2008. It is not directly derived from any traditional programming language but is inspired by the concept of visual programming languages like Scratch. Stencyl's visual scripting system is designed to be intuitive and user-friendly, allowing users to create games by connecting blocks that represent actions and conditions.
curious_fact: Stencyl supports both 2D and HTML5 game development, allowing games to be published on multiple platforms.

name: Storyboard
description: Storyboard is a visual programming language designed for creating interactive multimedia applications. It allows users to design and develop software using a drag-and-drop interface, making it accessible to non-programmers. Despite its innovative approach, Storyboard has not gained widespread adoption beyond niche markets.
connectionsExplanation: Storyboard was developed by a team of software engineers at a major technology company in the United States. It was inspired by the need to simplify the process of creating interactive applications, particularly for those without extensive programming knowledge. The language is based on a paradigm that emphasizes visual design over traditional text-based coding.

moons.name: Storyboard Pro
moons.description: An advanced version of Storyboard with additional features for professional developers.

curious_fact: Storyboard was one of the first languages to popularize the use of visual scripting in software development.

name: Strand
description: Strand is a concurrent programming language designed for writing distributed systems. It is based on the actor model and is known for its simplicity and efficiency in handling concurrent tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Strand was developed by the same team that created Erlang, which is also based on the actor model. Strand inherits many of its concepts and paradigms from Erlang, making it a direct descendant in the realm of concurrent programming languages.
curious_fact: Strand was inspired by the need for a more accessible and efficient language for building distributed systems compared to Erlang.

name: Strongtalk
description: Strongtalk is an object-oriented programming language developed by Object Arts Corporation. It is a direct descendant of Smalltalk and was designed to be a high-performance, scalable, and robust environment for building applications. Strongtalk introduced several innovations, including a just-in-time (JIT) compiler and a sophisticated garbage collector.
connectionsExplanation: Strongtalk evolved from Smalltalk, inheriting its object-oriented paradigm and dynamic nature. It was developed to address the performance limitations of earlier Smalltalk implementations, aiming to provide a more efficient and scalable platform for application development.
curious_fact: Strongtalk was one of the first languages to implement a JIT compiler, significantly improving execution speed.

name: Structured Audio Orchestra Language
description: Structured Audio Orchestra Language (SAOL) is a programming language designed for the creation of audio synthesis and composition. It was developed by the European Computer Manufacturers Association (ECMA) as part of the ECMA-230 standard. SAOL is used in conjunction with the Score Interchange File Format (SIF) to define complex audio compositions.
connectionsExplanation: Structured Audio Orchestra Language (SAOL) was developed as part of the ECMA-230 standard, which aimed to provide a standardized way of creating audio content. It is closely related to the Score Interchange File Format (SIF), which is used to define the structure and timing of audio compositions. SAOL is designed to be used in conjunction with SIF to create complex audio works.
curious_fact: SAOL was one of the first languages to use a declarative approach to audio synthesis, allowing composers to focus on the sound they wanted to create rather than the specific algorithms to generate it.
example: instr 1 { kamp = 1.0; kfreq = 440.0; aout = oscil(kamp, kfreq, 1); out aout; }

name: Stylus
description: Stylus is a revolutionary stylesheet language that compiles to CSS. It is designed to be expressive and elegant, allowing developers to write CSS in a more natural, readable, and maintainable way. Stylus supports variables, nesting, mixins, and functions, making it a powerful tool for modern web development.
connectionsExplanation: Stylus was created by LearnBoost, a company based in the United States, and it was inspired by the need for a more expressive and flexible alternative to traditional CSS. Stylus is part of the broader ecosystem of CSS preprocessors, which include Sass and Less, but it offers unique features such as indentation-based syntax and advanced scripting capabilities.

moons.name: Nib
moons.description: Nib is a library of Stylus mixins and functions that provide additional functionality and convenience for web development.

moons.name: Rupture
moons.description: Rupture is a responsive design library for Stylus, offering a set of mixins for creating media queries and managing responsive layouts.

curious_fact: Stylus was one of the first CSS preprocessors to support indentation-based syntax, which was later adopted by other languages like Sass.
example:
body
  font 12px Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
  color #333

name: SubL
description: SubL (SubLanguage) is a Lisp dialect developed by Cycorp for the Cyc artificial intelligence project. It is designed to be a high-level language for building complex knowledge-based systems.
connectionsExplanation: SubL evolved from Common Lisp and is specifically tailored for the needs of the Cyc project, which aims to build a comprehensive knowledge base. It shares many features with Common Lisp but includes additional constructs for representing and manipulating knowledge.
curious_fact: SubL is used in the Cyc project, one of the largest and longest-running AI research projects.

name: Subtext
description: Subtext is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It was created to introduce beginners to programming concepts without the complexity of more traditional languages.
connectionsExplanation: Subtext was inspired by BASIC and Python, aiming to maintain the simplicity of BASIC while incorporating modern programming paradigms found in Python. Its development was driven by the need for a language that could be easily understood and used by beginners.
curious_fact: Subtext was created as part of a university curriculum to teach programming fundamentals.

name: Sue
description: Sue is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool for beginners to understand basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Sue was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical lineage. It is inspired by modern scripting languages like Python and JavaScript, aiming to provide a gentle learning curve for newcomers to programming.
curious_fact: Sue was named after the character Sue from the TV show 'The IT Crowd' to add a touch of humor to the language.

name: Suika2
description: Suika2 is a 2D game engine developed in Japan, primarily used for creating visual novels and adventure games. It is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among hobbyists and small developers.
connectionsExplanation: Suika2 is a derivative of the Suika engine, which was developed in Japan. It inherits the simplicity and ease of use of its predecessor, making it a natural evolution in the realm of 2D game development for visual novels and adventure games.
curious_fact: Suika2 supports scripting in Lua, allowing for dynamic game content and interactions.

name: Suneido
description: Suneido is a database-centric programming language developed in Australia. It is designed to simplify the development of database applications, offering integrated database management and scripting capabilities.
connectionsExplanation: Suneido was developed as a proprietary language for a specific application, and while it shares some similarities with other languages in its approach to database integration, it does not have a direct lineage from mainstream programming languages. Its development was driven by the need for a tool tailored to the requirements of its original application.
curious_fact: Suneido was originally developed for a database management system and later evolved into a full-fledged programming language.

name: SuperBASIC
description: SuperBASIC is an enhanced version of the BASIC programming language, designed to be more user-friendly and powerful for beginners and hobbyists. It was developed by Acorn Computers in the early 1980s to provide a more advanced environment for their popular BBC Micro computer.
connectionsExplanation: SuperBASIC evolved from the original BASIC language, which was developed in the late 1960s. It was specifically designed to improve upon the simplicity and accessibility of BASIC, making it more suitable for educational purposes and home computing. SuperBASIC introduced several enhancements, including improved graphics capabilities and a more structured syntax, which helped to popularize BASIC among a new generation of programmers.
curious_fact: SuperBASIC was one of the first languages to introduce a structured programming approach to BASIC, making it easier to write and maintain complex programs.

name: SuperCollider
description: SuperCollider is a platform for audio synthesis and algorithmic composition. It features a powerful object-oriented language and a real-time environment for creating complex soundscapes.
connectionsExplanation: SuperCollider evolved from earlier sound synthesis languages like Max/MSP and Pure Data. It was developed by James McCartney and others at IRCAM in Paris, aiming to provide a more flexible and powerful environment for sound synthesis and algorithmic composition.

moons.name: SC3
moons.description: A version of SuperCollider with improved performance and features.

moons.name: Quarks
moons.description: A package manager for SuperCollider, allowing easy installation of additional libraries and extensions.

curious_fact: SuperCollider was originally developed for real-time sound synthesis in IRCAM's research environment.

name: SuperNova
description: SuperNova is a cutting-edge, high-performance programming language designed for parallel computing and real-time data processing. It was developed by a team of researchers at the University of California, Berkeley, aiming to address the limitations of existing languages in handling large-scale data and complex algorithms efficiently.
connectionsExplanation: SuperNova was inspired by the need for a language that could leverage modern multi-core processors and distributed computing environments. Its design incorporates elements from functional programming paradigms to enhance parallel execution and data immutability, making it suitable for applications in artificial intelligence, scientific simulations, and financial modeling.

moons.name: Parallel Lib
moons.description: A library for parallel programming in SuperNova, providing tools and abstractions for efficient data processing and task distribution.

moons.name: Realtime Framework
moons.description: A framework for building real-time applications in SuperNova, supporting low-latency data processing and event-driven architectures.

curious_fact: SuperNova's syntax is designed to be minimalistic, focusing on readability and expressiveness in parallel programming.

name: SuperPascal
description: SuperPascal is an extended version of Pascal designed to enhance its capabilities for system programming and large-scale software development. It introduced several new features and optimizations over the original Pascal language.
connectionsExplanation: SuperPascal evolved from Pascal, which was developed by Niklaus Wirth in the late 1960s. SuperPascal aimed to address the limitations of Pascal by adding support for more advanced data structures and programming paradigms, making it suitable for larger and more complex projects.

moons.name: SuperPascal Compiler
moons.description: A high-performance compiler for SuperPascal, supporting advanced optimizations and features.

moons.name: SuperPascal IDE
moons.description: An integrated development environment specifically tailored for SuperPascal, providing a comprehensive set of tools for development.

curious_fact: SuperPascal was one of the first languages to introduce object-oriented features into Pascal, paving the way for future object-oriented programming languages.
example: program HelloWorld; begin writeln('Hello, World!'); end.

name: SuperTalk
description: SuperTalk is an object-oriented programming language developed by Apple Computer in the 1980s. It was designed to be a high-level language for creating applications on the Macintosh operating system. SuperTalk was an extension of HyperCard, which allowed users to add scripting capabilities to HyperCard stacks.
connectionsExplanation: SuperTalk was developed as an extension of HyperCard, which was a popular multimedia authoring tool on the Macintosh. HyperCard's scripting language, HyperTalk, was the direct predecessor to SuperTalk. SuperTalk aimed to enhance HyperTalk with more advanced object-oriented features, making it suitable for developing more complex applications.
curious_fact: SuperTalk was one of the first languages to introduce object-oriented programming concepts to a wide audience through HyperCard.

name: SuperbaseDatabase
description: SuperbaseDatabase is a fictional, high-performance database management system designed for modern applications. It offers advanced features like real-time data synchronization and AI-driven query optimization.
connectionsExplanation: SuperbaseDatabase was inspired by the need for a more efficient and user-friendly database system in the 2020s. It draws on the principles of NoSQL databases and real-time data processing, aiming to bridge the gap between traditional relational databases and modern application requirements.
curious_fact: SuperbaseDatabase was developed by a team of database experts who aimed to create a system that could handle the complexities of modern data applications with ease.

name: Superplan
description: Superplan is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Superplan was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical lineage. It is inspired by modern programming languages but is not derived from any specific existing language.
curious_fact: Superplan was developed by a team of educators aiming to make programming accessible to children.

name: SurgeScript
description: SurgeScript is a scripting language designed for game development, particularly for the Surge Engine. It is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it accessible for beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: SurgeScript was created to be used with the Surge Engine, a game engine developed by Surge Studio. It is not directly derived from any other language but shares some similarities with JavaScript due to its syntax and event-driven nature.
curious_fact: SurgeScript was designed to be intuitive and easy to learn, aiming to reduce the learning curve for game development.

name: Svelte
description: Svelte is a frontend JavaScript framework that compiles components into highly optimized vanilla JavaScript at build time, resulting in smaller bundle sizes and better performance.
connectionsExplanation: Svelte was created by Rich Harris, who previously worked on the Rollup module bundler. It is designed to be a lightweight alternative to other frameworks like React and Vue.js, focusing on performance and simplicity.
curious_fact: Svelte's unique approach to compiling components at build time allows it to produce highly optimized code, often resulting in smaller bundle sizes compared to other frameworks.

name: Swift
description: Swift is a powerful and intuitive programming language developed by Apple for iOS, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS app development. It is designed to be easy to read and write, offering modern features and safety enhancements.
connectionsExplanation: Swift was introduced in 2014 as a modern replacement for Objective-C, which had been the primary language for Apple's software development for over two decades. Swift was designed to be more concise, safe, and expressive, while also being interoperable with Objective-C, allowing developers to gradually adopt Swift in their projects.

moons.name: SwiftUI
moons.description: A framework for building user interfaces across all Apple platforms.

moons.name: Combine
moons.description: A framework for processing values over time.

curious_fact: Swift was named after the swift bird, symbolizing its speed and efficiency.

name: SwiftLispKit
description: SwiftLispKit is a fictional programming language designed to bridge the gap between Swift and Lisp, combining the performance and safety of Swift with the expressive power of Lisp. It is intended for developers who want to leverage the strengths of both paradigms in a single language.
connectionsExplanation: SwiftLispKit was inspired by the desire to integrate the high-performance capabilities of Swift with the dynamic and expressive nature of Lisp. While it is a fictional language, it draws inspiration from real-world languages like Swift and Common Lisp, aiming to provide a unique development experience.
curious_fact: SwiftLispKit was created as an experiment to explore the possibility of combining Swift's type safety with Lisp's dynamic features.

name: Synchronized Multimedia Integration Language
description: SMIL (Synchronized Multimedia Integration Language) is an XML-based language used for adding timing and synchronization to multimedia presentations on the web. It allows for the coordination of audio, video, images, and text, making it a crucial tool for creating interactive multimedia content.
connectionsExplanation: SMIL was developed by the W3C (World Wide Web Consortium) to address the need for a standardized way to synchronize multimedia elements on the web. It builds upon the foundation of HTML and XML, providing a structured approach to multimedia synchronization.
curious_fact: SMIL was one of the first languages to allow for the synchronization of multimedia elements on the web, paving the way for modern interactive content.

name: SynergyDBL
description: SynergyDBL is a high-level programming language developed by Synergex International Corporation. It is primarily used for business applications, particularly in the financial and healthcare industries. SynergyDBL is known for its ability to run on multiple platforms, including Windows, Linux, and Unix.
connectionsExplanation: SynergyDBL was developed to address the need for a versatile and efficient language for business applications. It evolved from earlier Synergy products and is closely related to Synergy COBOL, which shares many similarities in syntax and functionality.

moons.name: Synergy COBOL
moons.description: A variant of SynergyDBL with COBOL-like syntax.

curious_fact: SynergyDBL was one of the first languages to support cross-platform development in the 1980s.

name: SyntaxDefinitionFormalism
description: SyntaxDefinitionFormalism is a fictional programming language designed to explore the formalization of syntax rules. It is intended for educational purposes and research into language design.
connectionsExplanation: SyntaxDefinitionFormalism is inspired by the principles of formal language theory and compiler design. It draws from the work of Noam Chomsky on generative grammars and the syntax rules defined in the Backus-Naur Form (BNF). The language is not based on any existing programming language but rather on the theoretical foundations of syntax definition.
curious_fact: SyntaxDefinitionFormalism uses a unique syntax that is both minimalistic and highly expressive, allowing for the definition of complex grammatical structures with minimal code.

name: SynthEdit
description: SynthEdit is a visual programming environment designed for audio synthesis and digital signal processing. It allows users to create complex audio effects and synthesizers by connecting modules visually, rather than writing code.
connectionsExplanation: SynthEdit is a specialized tool for audio programming and does not have a direct lineage from traditional programming languages. It is inspired by the concept of visual programming and is used in the field of digital audio workstations (DAWs).
curious_fact: SynthEdit was one of the first tools to popularize visual programming in the audio domain, allowing musicians and sound engineers to create complex audio effects without extensive coding knowledge.
example: Create a new patch and connect a SineOscillator module to an Output module to generate a simple sine wave.

name: SystemBuilder/SB+
description: SystemBuilder/SB+ is a specialized programming language developed by IBM in the 1970s for the System/370 mainframe. It was designed to simplify the creation of system-level programs and utilities.
connectionsExplanation: SystemBuilder/SB+ was developed as part of IBM's efforts to modernize its mainframe systems. It evolved from earlier IBM assembly languages and was intended to provide a higher-level abstraction for system programming tasks.
curious_fact: SystemBuilder/SB+ was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of macro processing in a high-level language context.

name: SystemVerilog
description: SystemVerilog is an extension of Verilog designed for hardware description and verification. It adds object-oriented features, data types, and testbench enhancements to Verilog, making it more suitable for complex hardware designs and verification.
connectionsExplanation: SystemVerilog evolved from Verilog, which was developed in the late 1980s. It was standardized by Accellera Systems Initiative in 2000 to address the limitations of Verilog in handling complex hardware designs and verification. SystemVerilog's introduction marked a significant step in the evolution of hardware description languages, providing more advanced features for modern digital design.

moons.name: UVM
moons.description: Universal Verification Methodology (UVM) is a widely adopted framework for hardware verification using SystemVerilog.

curious_fact: SystemVerilog is used in the design and verification of almost all modern digital chips, including those in smartphones and supercomputers.

name: T
description: T is a programming language developed by Charles H. Moore in 1972 for the Forth computer. It is a minimalist language designed to be simple and efficient, with a focus on direct hardware manipulation.
connectionsExplanation: T is considered a precursor to Forth, which was developed by Charles H. Moore in the early 1970s. T shares many of the same principles and design philosophies as Forth, emphasizing simplicity and direct hardware interaction.
curious_fact: T was one of the first languages to use reverse Polish notation (RPN) for arithmetic operations.

name: T++
description: T++ is a modern, experimental programming language designed to explore new paradigms in software development. It aims to provide a balance between performance and ease of use, targeting both system-level programming and high-level abstractions.
connectionsExplanation: T++ was inspired by the evolution of C++ and aims to incorporate recent advancements in programming language design. It draws from the object-oriented paradigm but introduces innovative features to enhance developer productivity and code maintainability.

moons.name: LibT++
moons.description: A standard library for T++ providing essential tools and utilities.

curious_fact: T++ introduced the concept of 'auto-inference' for variable types, significantly reducing boilerplate code.
example: class HelloWorld { public: void print() { System.out.println("Hello, World!"); } }; int main() { HelloWorld obj; obj.print(); return 0; }

name: TACL
description: TACL, or Time and Accounting Command Language, is a command language developed by DEC for use with their VAX/VMS operating system. It was designed to provide a simple and efficient way to perform system administration tasks and manage user accounts.
connectionsExplanation: TACL was developed by DEC as part of their VAX/VMS environment. It is closely associated with the VMS operating system and was used for system administration and user management tasks. TACL's development reflects the need for a user-friendly command language in the context of VMS.
curious_fact: TACL was one of the first command languages to be integrated into a commercial operating system, influencing the development of similar tools in later systems.

name: TACPOL
description: TACPOL is a hypothetical programming language designed for educational purposes. It is a simple, structured language intended to teach basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: TACPOL was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming. It is not based on any existing language but draws inspiration from early structured programming paradigms.
curious_fact: TACPOL stands for 'Teaching Assembly Code Programming Language'.

name: TADS
description: TADS (Text Adventure Development System) is a programming language and set of tools for creating text-based adventure games. It provides a high-level language with features specifically designed for game development, making it easier to create complex narrative-driven games.
connectionsExplanation: TADS evolved from earlier text adventure systems like ZIL and Inform, but it introduced its own unique features and syntax. It was designed to be more accessible and powerful for game developers, allowing for the creation of rich, interactive text adventures.
curious_fact: TADS was developed by Michael J. Roberts, who also wrote the classic text adventure game 'King's Quest'.

name: TECO
description: TECO, short for Text Editor and Corrector, is an early computer editor developed at MIT in the 1960s. It was one of the first editors to allow users to edit text directly in memory and is considered a precursor to modern text editors.
connectionsExplanation: TECO was developed at MIT in the early 1960s and is considered one of the first text editors. It was influential in the development of later editors and programming languages, particularly in the Unix environment.
curious_fact: TECO was written in assembly language and was used to edit the source code of early operating systems like Multics.

name: TELCOMP
description: TELCOMP is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It was created to introduce basic programming concepts without the complexities of more advanced languages.
connectionsExplanation: TELCOMP was developed in the early 1960s as part of a series of educational tools. It was inspired by the need for a language that could be easily understood by beginners, drawing on the simplicity of early BASIC languages but with a more structured approach.
curious_fact: TELCOMP was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of structured programming in an educational setting.

name: THINK C
description: THINK C was a popular C programming environment developed by Symantec Corporation for the Apple Macintosh. It included an integrated development environment (IDE) and was widely used for developing applications for the Mac OS.
connectionsExplanation: THINK C was a direct descendant of the C programming language, which was developed by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs in the early 1970s. It was specifically tailored for the Apple Macintosh platform, leveraging the Mac's graphical user interface and system architecture.
curious_fact: THINK C was one of the first C compilers to support object-oriented programming features, making it a precursor to C++.

name: TI-BASIC
description: TI-BASIC is a programming language developed by Texas Instruments for their graphing calculators. It is designed to be user-friendly and accessible, making it popular among students and educators for educational purposes.
connectionsExplanation: TI-BASIC is a direct descendant of the BASIC language, specifically tailored for use on Texas Instruments graphing calculators. It evolved from the earlier BASIC languages used in the 1970s and 1980s, adapting its syntax and features to fit the limited resources and capabilities of calculator hardware.
curious_fact: TI-BASIC was one of the first languages to be included on graphing calculators, revolutionizing how students learned mathematics and programming.

name: TI-BASIC
description: TI-BASIC is a programming language developed by Texas Instruments for use on their graphing calculators. It is designed to be user-friendly and accessible, making it popular among students and educators for educational purposes.
connectionsExplanation: TI-BASIC evolved from earlier BASIC dialects and was specifically tailored for the capabilities of Texas Instruments graphing calculators. It shares lineage with other BASIC variants but is optimized for the calculator environment.
curious_fact: TI-BASIC was one of the first languages to be widely used on handheld calculators, revolutionizing educational tools.

name: TI-BASIC83
description: TI-BASIC83 is a programming language developed by Texas Instruments for use on the TI-83 graphing calculator. It is designed to be user-friendly for beginners and allows for basic programming tasks such as creating simple games and mathematical applications.
connectionsExplanation: TI-BASIC83 is a specialized language developed specifically for the TI-83 graphing calculator. It evolved from earlier versions of TI-BASIC used on other Texas Instruments calculators, maintaining a consistent syntax and functionality tailored to the calculator's capabilities.
curious_fact: TI-BASIC83 was one of the first languages to introduce a graphical interface on a graphing calculator, allowing users to create programs with visual elements.

name: TJS2
description: TJS2 is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by JavaScript but with a more structured syntax and additional features aimed at beginners.
connectionsExplanation: TJS2 was created as an educational tool to introduce programming concepts to beginners. While it shares some similarities with JavaScript, it has a more structured syntax and additional features to make learning more intuitive. The language is not widely used outside of educational settings.
curious_fact: TJS2 was developed by a team of educators to simplify the learning curve of JavaScript for students.

name: TLA+
description: TLA+ (Temporal Logic of Actions) is a formal specification language developed by Leslie Lamport. It is used for describing concurrent systems and is particularly popular in academia and research for its rigorous approach to system design.
connectionsExplanation: TLA+ is rooted in formal methods and temporal logic, which are foundational concepts in computer science. It evolved from earlier work in formal specification and verification, aiming to provide a more accessible and expressive language for specifying complex systems.
curious_fact: TLA+ is named after Temporal Logic of Actions, reflecting its focus on describing how systems change over time.
example:
---- MODULE HelloWorld ----
VARIABLE x
Init == x = 0
Next == x' = x + 1
Spec == Init \* [Next]*

name: TMG
description: TMG (Text Manipulation Generator) is a compiler-compiler system developed by James Morris and Robert W. Thompson at Bell Labs in 1966. It is designed to generate parsers for context-free languages and is notable for its use in the development of the UNIX operating system.
connectionsExplanation: TMG is a pioneering compiler-compiler system that influenced the development of subsequent parsing techniques and tools. It was used in the creation of the UNIX operating system, highlighting its significance in early computer science.
curious_fact: TMG was one of the first systems to use a recursive-descent parsing method, which is still widely used today.

name: TNSDL
description: TNSDL (Time Notation Scripting Description Language) is a specialized scripting language designed for creating and managing time-based animations and simulations. It is particularly popular in the gaming industry for its ability to handle complex timing and synchronization tasks efficiently.
connectionsExplanation: TNSDL was developed as an extension of the Lua programming language, incorporating time-based scripting capabilities. It leverages Lua's lightweight and embeddable nature, making it suitable for real-time applications. The language's design focuses on providing a simple yet powerful syntax for defining animations and simulations, which has led to its adoption in various game development projects.

moons.name: TNSDL-UI
moons.description: A library for creating user interfaces with TNSDL, allowing developers to integrate interactive elements into their animations and simulations.

moons.name: TNSDL-Physics
moons.description: A physics engine extension for TNSDL, enabling the simulation of realistic physical interactions within animations.

curious_fact: TNSDL was created by a team of game developers who needed a language that could handle complex timing without sacrificing performance.
example:
function animate() 
  local time = getTime() 
  local position = calculatePosition(time) 
  drawObject(position) 
end

name: TOM
description: TOM is a domain-specific language (DSL) designed for specifying and verifying concurrent systems. It is particularly useful in the context of formal methods for software engineering.
connectionsExplanation: TOM is inspired by the need for a more expressive and formal way to describe concurrent systems, drawing from the paradigms of formal verification and model checking. It is not directly derived from any specific programming language but is influenced by the logical and mathematical foundations of computer science.
curious_fact: TOM uses a unique syntax that combines elements of logic and programming, allowing for precise specification of system behavior.

name: TOMVIEW
description: TOMVIEW is a specialized programming language designed for creating interactive graphical user interfaces. It was developed in the early 1980s and was particularly popular in academic and research environments for its ability to quickly prototype GUIs.
connectionsExplanation: TOMVIEW was developed in the early 1980s, a period when graphical user interfaces (GUIs) were becoming increasingly important. It was influenced by the need for rapid prototyping and ease of use in academic settings. While not directly descended from any specific language, it shares conceptual similarities with early GUI languages like Smalltalk and the X Window System.
curious_fact: TOMVIEW was one of the first languages to popularize the use of object-oriented principles in GUI development.
example: CREATE WINDOW "Hello World" SIZE 300, 200; DISPLAY TEXT "Hello, World!" AT 100, 100;

name: TRAC
description: TRAC (TRAnslation Compiler) is a programming language developed in the early 1970s. It was designed for educational purposes and to demonstrate the principles of compiler construction. TRAC is a simple language that supports basic control structures and data types.
connectionsExplanation: TRAC was developed as part of a compiler construction course at the University of California, Berkeley. It was influenced by the need to create a language that could be easily understood and used to teach the concepts of compilers and interpreters.
curious_fact: TRAC was one of the first languages designed specifically for educational purposes, focusing on teaching compiler principles.

name: TREE-META
description: TREE-META is an experimental programming language designed to explore tree-based data structures and meta-programming. It is primarily used in academic and research settings to study language design and compiler optimization.
connectionsExplanation: TREE-META was inspired by the need to create a language that could efficiently manipulate and optimize tree-based data structures. Its design draws from functional programming paradigms, particularly from languages like Haskell and Lisp, which emphasize immutability and recursion.
curious_fact: TREE-META introduced a unique 'tree-lens' feature that allows for efficient traversal and modification of nested data structures.

name: TTCN
description: TTCN (Telecommunication Test Control Notation) is a standard for specifying and implementing test cases for telecommunication systems. It is widely used in the telecommunications industry for testing protocols and systems.
connectionsExplanation: TTCN was developed by the European Telecommunications Standards Institute (ETSI) to address the need for a standardized testing language for telecommunication systems. It is based on the principles of structured programming and is designed to be used in conjunction with other ETSI standards.
curious_fact: TTCN was one of the first languages designed specifically for testing telecommunication systems, revolutionizing the industry's approach to quality assurance.

name: TTCN-3
description: TTCN-3 (Telecommunication Test Control Notation version 3) is a standard language for specifying and implementing test cases for telecommunication systems. It is designed to be highly expressive and flexible, allowing for the creation of complex test scenarios.
connectionsExplanation: TTCN-3 was developed by the European Telecommunications Standards Institute (ETSI) to address the need for a standardized language for testing telecommunication systems. It evolved from earlier testing languages and is specifically tailored to the requirements of telecommunication protocols and systems.
curious_fact: TTCN-3 is one of the few languages specifically designed for testing purposes, making it unique in the programming landscape.

name: TTM
description: TTM is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and clarity. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool for beginners to understand basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: TTM was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical lineage. It is inspired by modern programming languages that prioritize simplicity and ease of learning.
curious_fact: TTM was developed in a weekend hackathon by a group of educators.

name: TTSneo
description: TTSneo is a futuristic text-to-speech programming language designed for advanced natural language processing and voice synthesis. It is particularly suited for applications requiring high-quality, customizable speech output.
connectionsExplanation: TTSneo was developed as an evolution of existing text-to-speech technologies, aiming to integrate machine learning algorithms for more natural and context-aware speech synthesis. Its design draws inspiration from modern programming paradigms, focusing on ease of use and integration with AI systems.

moons.name: TTSneo Lib
moons.description: A comprehensive library for TTSneo, providing tools and utilities for developers to enhance their applications with advanced speech synthesis features.

curious_fact: TTSneo supports over 50 languages and dialects, making it a versatile choice for global applications.

name: TUTOR
description: TUTOR was an early educational programming language developed at the University of Cambridge. It was designed to be simple and easy to learn, making it suitable for beginners and children. Despite its simplicity, TUTOR was capable of performing complex calculations and was used in various educational settings.
connectionsExplanation: TUTOR was developed as part of the Cambridge University Computing Service's efforts to introduce programming to a wider audience. It was influenced by earlier educational languages like BASIC but was specifically tailored for the Cambridge environment. TUTOR played a role in popularizing programming education in the UK.
curious_fact: TUTOR was one of the first languages to use a structured approach, which influenced later educational programming languages.

name: TUZ-3
description: TUZ-3 is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes and as a thought experiment in language design. It features a unique syntax and semantics that challenge conventional programming paradigms.
connectionsExplanation: TUZ-3 was created as an academic project to explore new ways of programming language design. While it has no direct historical predecessors, it draws inspiration from various paradigms, including functional and procedural programming.
curious_fact: TUZ-3 was developed by a team of linguists and computer scientists who aimed to create a language that could express complex ideas in a simple manner.

name: TXL
description: TXL is a transformation language designed for text processing and analysis. It is particularly suited for tasks involving syntax-directed translation and transformation of programming languages and other structured text.
connectionsExplanation: TXL was developed at the University of Queensland in Australia. It is a descendant of the Paradigm Ancestor, which is a general term for transformation languages that focus on text processing. TXL's design is influenced by the need for a powerful and flexible tool for language analysis and transformation, making it a specialized tool in the domain of text processing.
curious_fact: TXL is known for its ability to handle complex text transformations with a high degree of precision.
example:
PROGRAM HelloWorld
BEGIN
  OUTPUT "Hello, World!"
END

name: TableProducingLanguage
description: TableProducingLanguage is a specialized programming language designed to simplify the creation and manipulation of tabular data. It is particularly useful for data analysts and scientists who need to generate complex tables from raw data.
connectionsExplanation: TableProducingLanguage was developed as an extension of SQL, inheriting its structured query capabilities. It introduces new syntax and functions specifically tailored for table generation and manipulation, making it a niche tool in the data processing domain.
curious_fact: TableProducingLanguage was created to address the gap between SQL and modern data visualization tools, allowing users to generate tables with complex formatting and interactive features.
example: CREATE TABLE example_table AS SELECT * FROM raw_data WHERE condition; DISPLAY example_table;

name: Tcl
description: Tcl (Tool Command Language) is a high-level, general-purpose, interpreted, dynamic programming language. It was developed by John Ousterhout at the University of California, Berkeley. Tcl is known for its simplicity and flexibility, making it popular for scripting and automation tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Tcl was developed as a scripting language for the X Window System, which was a pioneering graphical user interface (GUI) system. Its design was influenced by the need for a language that could easily interface with C programs, making it a foundational language in the evolution of scripting languages.

moons.name: Tk
moons.description: Tk is a toolkit for building graphical user interfaces (GUIs) in Tcl. It provides a comprehensive set of widgets and commands for creating interactive applications.

curious_fact: Tcl was originally named 'Tool Command Language' but was later shortened to 'Tcl'.

name: TclX
description: TclX is an extension to the Tcl scripting language, enhancing its capabilities with additional commands and features. It was developed to provide a more powerful and flexible environment for scripting.
connectionsExplanation: TclX evolved from Tcl, adding a suite of extensions to improve its functionality and usability. It was designed to address the limitations of the original Tcl language by introducing new commands and features that catered to more complex scripting needs.
curious_fact: TclX introduced features like regular expressions and additional control structures, making it a more robust tool for scripting.

name: TeX
description: TeX is a typesetting system created by Donald Knuth to allow precise control over the formatting of documents. It is widely used in academia for publishing scientific papers and books.
connectionsExplanation: TeX was developed as a successor to the SAIL typesetting system and was influenced by METAFONT, which was also created by Knuth. It is not directly derived from any earlier language but is part of the broader family of typesetting systems.
curious_fact: TeX is known for its ability to produce high-quality typesetting, especially for mathematical and scientific documents.

name: Tea
description: Tea is a fictional programming language designed to be a playful and whimsical alternative to traditional languages. It is inspired by the British tea culture and features syntax that resembles tea-related phrases and concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Tea is a fictional language created in 2010, inspired by British tea culture. It does not have a direct historical lineage but draws inspiration from the paradigms of modern scripting languages.
curious_fact: Tea is the only programming language named after a beverage.

name: TekkieUni
description: TekkieUni is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes at the Tekkie University. It is a simple, interpreted language aimed at beginners to help them understand basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: TekkieUni was created as an in-house language for the Tekkie University curriculum. It is not derived from any existing language but is inspired by the need for a straightforward language to teach fundamental programming principles.
curious_fact: TekkieUni was developed in a single weekend by a team of university professors.

name: Telescript
description: Telescript is a programming language developed by Netscape Communications in the early 1990s. It was designed to run on the World Wide Web and was intended to be a simpler alternative to JavaScript. However, it never gained widespread adoption and was largely overshadowed by JavaScript.
connectionsExplanation: Telescript was developed as a competitor to JavaScript during the early days of the web. It was designed to be easier to learn and use, but its limited adoption and the rapid rise of JavaScript led to its eventual obsolescence.
curious_fact: Telescript was one of the first languages to attempt to bring object-oriented programming to the web, but it failed to gain traction.

name: Teml
description: Teml is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid web development. It emphasizes simplicity and ease of use, making it accessible to beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: Teml was inspired by the templating capabilities of languages like Jinja2 and EJS. It aims to provide a streamlined syntax for generating dynamic web content, drawing from the best practices of existing templating engines.

moons.name: Teml-Express
moons.description: A web framework for Teml that simplifies the development of web applications.

curious_fact: Teml was created by a team of web developers who wanted a more intuitive way to handle templates in their projects.

name: TemplateAttributeLanguage
description: TemplateAttributeLanguage is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on the use of templates and attributes to simplify code structure and enhance readability. It is inspired by modern web development practices and aims to provide a clean and intuitive syntax for beginners.
connectionsExplanation: TemplateAttributeLanguage is inspired by the evolution of web development languages, particularly those that emphasize template-based systems like HTML and templating engines used in frameworks such as Angular and React. It draws parallels with attribute-based programming seen in languages like HTML and XML, aiming to merge these concepts into a cohesive and accessible language.
curious_fact: TemplateAttributeLanguage was created to demonstrate the power of declarative programming in web development, making it easier for beginners to understand and write complex web applications.

name: TextExecutiveProgrammingLanguage
description: TextExecutiveProgrammingLanguage (TEPL) is a fictional programming language designed for managing text-based administrative tasks. It was created as a parody of bureaucratic language, aiming to simplify complex text processing through a series of predefined commands.
connectionsExplanation: TEPL was inspired by the need for a more efficient way to handle repetitive text-based tasks in office environments. It draws parallels with early command-line interfaces but with a focus on readability and ease of use for non-technical users.
curious_fact: TEPL was created by a team of office workers who wanted to automate their daily tasks without needing to learn complex programming languages.

name: TextProcessingUtility
description: TextProcessingUtility is a specialized scripting language designed for rapid text manipulation and analysis. It is particularly suited for tasks involving large datasets and complex text processing pipelines.
connectionsExplanation: TextProcessingUtility was developed as a modern extension of shell scripting languages, aiming to provide a more powerful and flexible environment for text processing tasks. It draws inspiration from languages like Perl and Python, incorporating features from both for enhanced performance and ease of use.
curious_fact: TextProcessingUtility introduced a novel syntax for parallel text processing, significantly speeding up operations on multi-core systems.

name: The Join Calculus Language
description: The Join Calculus Language is a programming language designed for concurrent and distributed computing. It is based on the join calculus, a formalism for describing concurrent systems. The language is known for its mathematical rigor and its ability to model complex interactions between concurrent processes.
connectionsExplanation: The Join Calculus Language was developed as an extension of the join calculus, which was introduced by Gordon Plotkin in the early 1990s. It builds upon the theoretical foundations of process calculi, particularly the pi-calculus, to provide a practical framework for concurrent programming.
curious_fact: The Join Calculus Language was one of the first languages to explicitly support the join calculus, a formalism that emphasizes the interaction between concurrent processes.
example: process P(x) = join { case x -> y \ y -> P(y) }

name: Thue
description: Thue is an esoteric programming language created by the Norwegian mathematician and computer scientist Ole-Johan Dahl in 1966. It is notable for its simplicity and its use of string rewriting rules, which are applied repeatedly until no more rules can be applied. Despite its simplicity, Thue is Turing-complete, meaning it can compute anything computable given enough time and memory.
connectionsExplanation: Thue was one of the early esoteric programming languages, predating many others. It was inspired by the work of Emil Post on string rewriting systems and is part of the broader family of esoteric languages that explore minimalistic computation.
curious_fact: Thue was one of the first languages to demonstrate Turing completeness using only string rewriting rules.

name: TigerJython
description: TigerJython is a dialect of the Tiger programming language that runs on the Java platform. It is designed to be a simple and educational language for teaching programming concepts, particularly in the context of Java.
connectionsExplanation: TigerJython is a dialect of the Tiger language, which was developed at ETH Zurich. It inherits its syntax and semantics from Tiger but is implemented on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM), making it accessible to Java developers and students familiar with the Java ecosystem.
curious_fact: TigerJython was created to bridge the gap between educational languages and the practical Java environment.

name: Timber
description: Timber is a Ruby gem that allows developers to log structured data to a variety of destinations, including files, syslog, and external services. It is designed to be simple and flexible, making it easy to integrate into existing Ruby applications.
connectionsExplanation: Timber was created to address the need for better logging practices in Ruby applications. It builds upon the existing logging capabilities of Ruby, providing a more structured and flexible approach to logging. Timber is particularly useful for applications that require detailed logging for monitoring and debugging purposes.
curious_fact: Timber was developed by the team behind Loggly, a cloud-based log management service.
example:
require 'timber'

Timber.with_context(user_id: 123) do
  Timber.info('User logged in')
end

name: Tink
description: Tink is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid application development. It emphasizes simplicity and ease of use, making it accessible to beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: Tink was developed as a successor to the popular scripting language 'Scripto', aiming to improve performance and add modern features. It draws inspiration from JavaScript and Python, combining their strengths to create a versatile language.

moons.name: Tink-Web
moons.description: A framework for building web applications with Tink.

moons.name: Tink-Data
moons.description: A library for handling data and databases in Tink.

curious_fact: Tink was created by a team of former Scripto developers who wanted to address the limitations of the older language.

name: Tiny
description: Tiny is a minimalist esoteric programming language designed to challenge programmers' creativity and understanding of minimalism. It consists of only 16 instructions, making it one of the simplest languages ever created.
connectionsExplanation: Tiny was inspired by the desire to push the boundaries of programming simplicity. It draws no direct lineage from traditional languages but shares a conceptual lineage with other esoteric languages like Brainfuck and Befunge, which also aim to reduce the complexity of programming.
curious_fact: Tiny's entire instruction set can be memorized in less than five minutes.

name: TinyBASIC
description: TinyBASIC is a minimalist version of the BASIC programming language, designed to be small and easy to implement. It was created to demonstrate the simplicity of BASIC and to serve as a teaching tool for beginners.
connectionsExplanation: TinyBASIC is a derivative of the original BASIC language, which was developed in the late 1960s. It shares the same paradigm and syntax as its predecessors but is significantly smaller in size and functionality.
curious_fact: TinyBASIC was one of the first programming languages to be implemented on microcomputers, helping to democratize programming in the early days of personal computing.

name: TinyScheme
description: TinyScheme is a minimalistic Scheme implementation designed for educational purposes and embedded systems. It is a lightweight interpreter that aims to be easy to understand and modify.
connectionsExplanation: TinyScheme is a derivative of the R5RS Scheme standard, which is a widely recognized dialect of the Lisp programming language. It was developed to provide a simple and efficient Scheme interpreter suitable for educational environments and small systems.
curious_fact: TinyScheme is known for its small footprint, making it ideal for systems with limited resources.

name: Toi
description: Toi is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax that encourages logical thinking.
connectionsExplanation: Toi was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts without the complexity of existing languages. It draws inspiration from Python's syntax but introduces its own unique constructs to foster a deeper understanding of programming principles.
curious_fact: Toi was developed by a team of educators aiming to simplify the learning curve for new programmers.

name: Toit
description: Toit is a modern programming language designed for building scalable, distributed systems. It compiles to native code and is known for its performance and ease of use. Toit is particularly suited for IoT and edge computing applications.
connectionsExplanation: Toit was developed by the Toit team, inspired by the need for a language that could efficiently handle the complexities of modern distributed systems. While it does not have a direct lineage from traditional programming languages, it draws on principles of performance and simplicity, similar to languages like C and Rust.
curious_fact: Toit's compiler is written in Go, showcasing its pragmatic approach to language development.

name: ToonTalk
description: ToonTalk is an educational programming language designed to introduce children to programming concepts through an interactive, graphical interface. It uses cartoon characters to represent code elements, making it accessible and engaging for young learners.
connectionsExplanation: ToonTalk was developed by MIT's Media Lab as part of their efforts to make programming more accessible to children. It is not directly derived from any traditional programming language but draws inspiration from educational theories and interactive software design.
curious_fact: ToonTalk was one of the first programming languages designed specifically for children, aiming to demystify programming concepts through playful interaction.

name: Topology and Orchestration Specification for Cloud Applications
description: TOSCA is a standard for defining and orchestrating cloud applications. It provides a blueprint for deploying applications across different cloud environments, ensuring consistency and portability.
connectionsExplanation: TOSCA was developed by the Open Cloud Computing Interface (OCCI) Working Group to address the need for a standardized way to describe and manage cloud applications. It builds on the principles of cloud computing and service orchestration, aiming to simplify the deployment and management of complex applications across various cloud platforms.
curious_fact: TOSCA uses YAML for its syntax, making it human-readable and easy to write.
example:
tosca_definitions_version: tosca_simple_yaml_1_3
topology_template:
  node_templates:
    my_web_server:
      type: tosca.nodes.WebServer
      properties:
        port: 80

name: Torino
description: Torino is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid application development. It emphasizes simplicity and ease of use, making it accessible to beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers. Torino is particularly well-suited for web development and automation tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Torino was developed as a successor to the popular 'Luna' language, aiming to address some of the limitations and complexities found in its predecessor. While Luna was widely adopted, Torino introduces a more streamlined syntax and enhanced performance optimizations, positioning it as a next-generation language in the same paradigm.

moons.name: WebKit
moons.description: A framework for building web applications in Torino.

moons.name: AutomateX
moons.description: A library for automating repetitive tasks with Torino.

curious_fact: Torino was named after the Italian city of Turin, known for its rich history in technology and innovation.

name: TouchDesigner
description: TouchDesigner is a visual programming environment for creating interactive digital content. It allows users to design and prototype interactive experiences using a node-based interface, making it accessible for artists and designers without deep programming knowledge.
connectionsExplanation: TouchDesigner was developed by Derivative Inc., a company founded in the early 2000s. It is not directly derived from any traditional programming language but is inspired by the visual programming paradigms seen in tools like Max/MSP and Pure Data. TouchDesigner's unique approach to visual programming makes it a specialized tool in the digital content creation space.

moons.name: CHOP
moons.description: Channel Operator (CHOP) is a module in TouchDesigner used for processing audio and time-based data.

moons.name: SOP
moons.description: Surface Operator (SOP) is a module in TouchDesigner used for 3D modeling and geometry manipulation.

moons.name: TOP
moons.description: Texture Operator (TOP) is a module in TouchDesigner used for image and video processing.

curious_fact: TouchDesigner was originally developed as a tool for interactive installations and has since evolved into a powerful platform for real-time graphics and digital art.

name: TouchDevelop
