description: TouchDevelop is a visual programming language designed for creating mobile and web applications. It allows users to build apps without writing traditional code, using a drag-and-drop interface. The platform was developed by Microsoft and was later acquired by GitHub.
connectionsExplanation: TouchDevelop was developed by Microsoft and is based on the principles of visual programming, which allows users to create applications through a graphical interface rather than writing code. It is inspired by the ease of use and accessibility of visual programming environments, making it suitable for beginners and non-programmers.
curious_fact: TouchDevelop was one of the first platforms to allow users to create mobile apps without writing code, using a visual interface.
example: TouchDevelop uses a visual interface for programming, so there is no traditional 'Hello World' example in the form of code. Instead, users drag and drop components to create an app.

name: TradeSpaceVisualizer
description: TradeSpaceVisualizer is a specialized programming language designed for visualizing complex trade space data in aerospace engineering. It integrates advanced visualization techniques to help engineers analyze and optimize spacecraft design parameters.
connectionsExplanation: TradeSpaceVisualizer was developed as a specialized tool within the aerospace engineering community. While it is not directly derived from any existing language, it shares conceptual similarities with data visualization languages like R and Python, which are widely used in scientific and engineering domains.
curious_fact: TradeSpaceVisualizer was created to address the need for more intuitive and interactive visualization tools in aerospace engineering.

name: Transact-SQL
description: Transact-SQL (T-SQL) is a Microsoft extension of the SQL language, used for programming Microsoft SQL Server. It adds control-of-flow language, data manipulation, and transactional capabilities to standard SQL.
connectionsExplanation: Transact-SQL evolved from SQL-86 and SQL-89, with significant enhancements to support the features of Microsoft SQL Server. It is a direct descendant of the standard SQL language, with extensions tailored to the needs of database management in the Windows environment.

moons.name: SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS)
moons.description: A platform for building data integration and data transformation solutions.

moons.name: SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS)
moons.description: A reporting tool for generating and delivering reports.

moons.name: SQL Server Analysis Services (SSAS)
moons.description: A tool for creating and managing multidimensional data models.

curious_fact: Transact-SQL introduced the concept of Common Table Expressions (CTEs) which allow for more readable and maintainable SQL code.

name: TransactionApplicationLanguage
description: TransactionApplicationLanguage (TAL) is a specialized programming language designed for financial and banking applications. It emphasizes transaction processing and data integrity, making it suitable for environments where accuracy and reliability are paramount.
connectionsExplanation: TransactionApplicationLanguage was developed as a specialized tool for the financial sector, drawing inspiration from existing languages like Java and C++. It aimed to provide a more streamlined approach to handling complex financial transactions, integrating features that ensure data consistency and security.

moons.name: SecureTransfer
moons.description: A framework for secure financial data transfer within TAL applications.

moons.name: AuditTrail
moons.description: A library for maintaining detailed transaction audit logs.

curious_fact: TAL was one of the first languages to implement automatic rollback mechanisms for failed transactions.

name: Treetop
description: Treetop is a Ruby-based parser generator that allows developers to define grammars in a natural, readable way. It is particularly useful for creating parsers for domain-specific languages.
connectionsExplanation: Treetop is built on top of Ruby, inheriting its dynamic nature and ease of use. It leverages Ruby's metaprogramming capabilities to generate efficient parsers from grammars defined in a simple, declarative syntax.
curious_fact: Treetop was created by Nathan Sobo and is known for its clean and intuitive syntax, making it a popular choice for parsing tasks in Ruby applications.
example:
grammar Hello
  rule hello
    'Hello, world!' { |input| puts input.text_value }
  end
end

name: Trellis-Owl
description: Trellis-Owl is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and visual programming concepts. It is inspired by the idea of using a trellis-like structure to represent data and logic, making it accessible for beginners.
connectionsExplanation: Trellis-Owl was created as an educational tool to introduce programming concepts in a visual and intuitive manner. While it is not based on any existing language, it draws inspiration from the simplicity and clarity of modern educational programming languages like Scratch.
curious_fact: Trellis-Owl uses a unique 'trellis' data structure to represent programs, which visually maps out data flow and logic.

name: Tritium
description: Tritium is a modern, high-performance programming language designed for parallel computing and quantum computing simulations. It is part of a research initiative aimed at developing languages that can efficiently handle complex quantum algorithms.
connectionsExplanation: Tritium was developed as an extension of the C++ language, incorporating advanced features to support quantum computing paradigms. It builds upon the C++ standard library and adds specialized constructs for quantum operations, making it a direct descendant of C++.

moons.name: QuantumLib
moons.description: A library for quantum computing operations in Tritium.

curious_fact: Tritium was named after the isotope of hydrogen used in nuclear fusion.

name: Triton
description: Triton is a modern, high-performance programming language designed for parallel computing and distributed systems. It aims to simplify the development of complex, scalable applications by providing a clean syntax and powerful abstractions.
connectionsExplanation: Triton was developed as a successor to the popular Rust language, inheriting its focus on safety and performance. It introduces new paradigms for parallelism and concurrency, making it particularly suitable for modern computing environments.

moons.name: Triton Standard Library
moons.description: The standard library for Triton, providing essential modules for system interaction, data manipulation, and parallel processing.

curious_fact: Triton was named after the sea god Triton, symbolizing its deep integration with modern computing infrastructures.

name: TrueBASIC
description: TrueBASIC is a dialect of the BASIC programming language developed by True Software. It was designed to be a modern, easy-to-learn language suitable for both beginners and experienced programmers. TrueBASIC includes features like structured programming, object-oriented programming, and a graphical user interface.
connectionsExplanation: TrueBASIC evolved from the earlier BASIC language, which was developed in the late 1950s and early 1960s. It incorporates modern programming paradigms and features to make it more accessible and powerful for contemporary use.
curious_fact: TrueBASIC was one of the first BASIC dialects to support object-oriented programming, making it a pioneer in its time.

name: TurboBasic
description: TurboBasic was a popular BASIC compiler developed by Borland for the CP/M operating system. It was known for its speed and ease of use, making it a favorite among hobbyists and small businesses in the early 1980s.
connectionsExplanation: TurboBasic evolved from earlier BASIC dialects and was part of the CP/M ecosystem, which was widely used in the 1980s. It was influenced by the structured programming concepts introduced in earlier BASIC versions.
curious_fact: TurboBasic was one of the first compilers to offer a built-in assembler, allowing users to write assembly code directly within the BASIC environment.

name: TurboC++
description: TurboC++ is a popular Integrated Development Environment (IDE) for the C++ programming language developed by Borland. It was widely used in educational settings and for small to medium-sized projects due to its ease of use and speed.
connectionsExplanation: TurboC++ is a direct descendant of the C programming language, specifically designed to enhance the development experience with C++. It builds upon the syntax and features of C, adding object-oriented capabilities and a user-friendly interface.
curious_fact: TurboC++ was one of the first IDEs to popularize the use of a graphical interface for C++ development.

name: Turbo Pascal
description: Turbo Pascal is a popular dialect of the Pascal programming language developed by Borland in the early 1980s. It was known for its speed, ease of use, and comprehensive integrated development environment (IDE). Turbo Pascal played a significant role in the spread of Pascal and object-oriented programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Turbo Pascal evolved from the original Pascal language, which was developed by Niklaus Wirth in the late 1960s. It was a significant step in the evolution of Pascal, introducing object-oriented features and a more user-friendly development environment. Turbo Pascal's success influenced the development of other programming languages and IDEs.
curious_fact: Turbo Pascal was one of the first languages to popularize the concept of an integrated development environment (IDE) with its bundled editor, compiler, and debugger.

name: Turbo Pascal OOP
description: Turbo Pascal OOP is an extension of Turbo Pascal that adds object-oriented programming features. It was developed by Borland and was one of the first commercial object-oriented Pascal compilers.
connectionsExplanation: Turbo Pascal OOP evolved from Turbo Pascal, which was a widely used Pascal compiler developed by Borland. The addition of object-oriented features in Turbo Pascal OOP marked a significant step towards modern object-oriented programming languages.
curious_fact: Turbo Pascal OOP was one of the first Pascal compilers to support object-oriented programming, influencing later languages like Delphi.

name: Turbo-BASIC XL
description: Turbo-BASIC XL is an enhanced version of Turbo-BASIC, developed by Borland for the IBM PC. It introduced several improvements over its predecessor, including a more advanced editor, better support for graphics, and additional functions. Turbo-BASIC XL was widely used in educational settings and for small business applications.
connectionsExplanation: Turbo-BASIC XL evolved from Turbo-BASIC, which itself was a significant advancement over earlier BASIC dialects. Turbo-BASIC was developed by Borland in the late 1980s to provide a more powerful and efficient BASIC environment for the IBM PC. Turbo-BASIC XL further refined these features, making it a popular choice for both beginners and experienced programmers.
curious_fact: Turbo-BASIC XL was one of the first BASIC compilers to support inline assembly, allowing users to incorporate low-level code directly into their programs.

name: Turbo51
description: Turbo51 is a high-level programming language and integrated development environment (IDE) specifically designed for the 8051 microcontroller. It was developed by Borland and was widely used in the 1980s and 1990s for embedded systems programming.
connectionsExplanation: Turbo51 is a direct descendant of the C programming language, tailored for the 8051 architecture. It evolved from the need for a more user-friendly and efficient way to develop software for microcontrollers, building upon the structured programming principles of C.
curious_fact: Turbo51 was one of the first IDEs to offer a comprehensive set of tools for microcontroller development, including an assembler, linker, and debugger.

name: TurboWarp
description: TurboWarp is an open-source reimplementation of Scratch, a visual programming language developed by MIT. It aims to provide a more modern and efficient platform for creating interactive stories, games, and animations.
connectionsExplanation: TurboWarp is a direct descendant of Scratch, which was originally developed by MIT in the early 2000s. TurboWarp improves upon Scratch by offering better performance, additional features, and an open-source codebase, making it a significant evolution in the visual programming space.
curious_fact: TurboWarp was created to address performance issues in the original Scratch platform, offering a faster and more responsive experience for users.

name: Turing
description: Turing is a programming language designed by Alan Turing in 1950. It was one of the first high-level programming languages and was intended to be a universal programming language. Turing is considered a foundational language in the history of computer science.
connectionsExplanation: Turing is a pioneering language from the 1950s, developed by Alan Turing. It was one of the first high-level programming languages and is considered a precursor to modern programming languages. Turing's design aimed to be a universal language, influencing the development of subsequent languages.
curious_fact: Turing was one of the first programming languages to use a structured approach, influencing the development of modern programming paradigms.

name: Turing+
description: Turing+ is an advanced programming language designed to enhance productivity and performance for modern software development. It combines elements of functional and object-oriented programming paradigms, offering a unique syntax and powerful features.
connectionsExplanation: Turing+ was developed as an evolution of the Turing programming language, aiming to address the limitations of its predecessor while incorporating contemporary programming practices. It inherits the foundational concepts of Turing but introduces innovative features to cater to the demands of modern software engineering.

moons.name: Turing+ Framework
moons.description: A comprehensive framework for building scalable and efficient applications using Turing+.

moons.name: Turing+ Libraries
moons.description: A collection of libraries that extend Turing+'s capabilities in various domains such as data processing, machine learning, and web development.

curious_fact: Turing+ was created by a team of computer scientists who sought to bridge the gap between theoretical programming concepts and practical software development.

name: Turtlestitch
description: Turtlestitch is a fictional programming language designed for artistic and educational purposes. It allows users to create intricate patterns and designs by simulating a turtle that moves and draws on a canvas. The language is inspired by Logo, but with a focus on stitching-like patterns.
connectionsExplanation: Turtlestitch is inspired by Logo, a dialect of Lisp, which itself evolved from the analytical-engine-order-code. Turtlestitch inherits the concept of a turtle moving on a plane to draw shapes, but introduces a unique twist with stitching patterns.
curious_fact: Turtlestitch was created to teach programming concepts through the medium of art.
example: forward 100; turn 90; stitch; forward 100; turn 90; stitch;

name: Twelf
description: Twelf is a programming language and interactive theorem prover designed for the formalization of meta-theorems about programming languages and logics. It is particularly suited for specifying and reasoning about formal systems, such as type theories and proof systems.
connectionsExplanation: Twelf is a descendant of the Edinburgh Logical Framework (ELF), which itself was inspired by the LF logical framework. It extends the capabilities of ELF by adding support for dependent types and a more flexible syntax, making it a powerful tool for formal verification and proof development.
curious_fact: Twelf is named after the number twelve, reflecting its use in formalizing systems with twelve components or rules.
example:
constant nat : type.
constant zero : nat.
constant succ : nat -> nat.

name: TypeScript
description: TypeScript is a programming language developed and maintained by Microsoft. It is a strict superset of JavaScript and adds optional static typing to the language. TypeScript is designed for development of large applications and transcompiles to JavaScript.
connectionsExplanation: TypeScript was developed by Microsoft as an extension of JavaScript. It was first released in 2012 and is a direct evolution of JavaScript, adding static typing features. TypeScript's design goals include improving code quality and maintainability, especially in large-scale applications.

moons.name: Angular
moons.description: A popular framework for building web applications.

moons.name: React
moons.description: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces.

curious_fact: TypeScript was originally developed for internal use at Microsoft and was open-sourced in 2012.

name: UCBLogo
description: UCBLogo is an educational programming language developed at the University of California, Berkeley. It is a dialect of Logo, designed to be easy to learn and use, making it popular in educational settings for teaching basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: UCBLogo evolved from the original Logo language, which was developed by Seymour Papert and his team at MIT in the late 1960s. UCBLogo maintains the educational philosophy of Logo while incorporating improvements and additional features for a more robust learning experience.
curious_fact: UCBLogo introduced the concept of 'turtle graphics,' which allows users to draw shapes and patterns by giving commands to a virtual turtle.

name: UCSD Pascal
description: UCSD Pascal is a variant of the Pascal programming language developed at the University of California, San Diego (UCSD) in the late 1970s. It was designed to be portable and was one of the first Pascal compilers to run on microcomputers. UCSD Pascal introduced the concept of a p-code machine, which allowed the language to be compiled into a portable intermediate code that could be executed on different hardware platforms.
connectionsExplanation: UCSD Pascal evolved from the original Pascal language, which was developed by Niklaus Wirth at ETH Zurich. UCSD Pascal was specifically designed to be portable and was one of the first Pascal compilers to run on microcomputers, making it a significant step in the development of Pascal for personal computing.
curious_fact: UCSD Pascal introduced the p-code machine, which was a precursor to the Java Virtual Machine.
example: program HelloWorld; begin writeln('Hello, World!'); end.

name: UNCOL
description: UNCOL is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and minimalism. It is not intended for practical use but serves as a tool for teaching basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: UNCOL was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts without the complexities of existing languages. It is not derived from any existing language but is inspired by the simplicity of BASIC and the structured approach of Pascal.
curious_fact: UNCOL was invented by a group of educators to simplify the learning curve for new programmers.

name: Unicode
description: Unicode is a computing industry standard for the consistent encoding, representation, and handling of text expressed in most of the world's writing systems. It provides a unique number for every character, no matter the platform, program, or language.
connectionsExplanation: Unicode was developed as a successor to earlier character encoding systems like ASCII and ISO 8859, aiming to support a much larger range of characters from various scripts and languages. It has become the foundation for character encoding in modern computing, ensuring compatibility and interoperability across different platforms and applications.
curious_fact: Unicode includes over 140,000 characters from more than 150 modern and historic scripts.

name: Unity
description: Unity is a cross-platform game development engine developed by Unity Technologies. It is widely used for creating 2D and 3D video games, interactive simulations, and virtual reality experiences. Unity supports multiple programming languages, with C# being the primary scripting language.
connectionsExplanation: Unity was developed by Unity Technologies, which was founded in 2004. It is built on top of the .NET framework and uses C# as its primary scripting language, making it a descendant of the C# language, which itself evolved from C and C++.

moons.name: Unity Editor
moons.description: The integrated development environment for Unity, providing tools for game development.

moons.name: Unity Shaders
moons.description: Customizable graphics rendering scripts for Unity.

curious_fact: Unity is known for its user-friendly interface and powerful scripting capabilities, making it accessible to both beginners and experienced developers.

name: Urbi
description: Urbi is a programming language designed for robotics and interactive systems. It provides a high-level interface for controlling robots and integrating various sensors and actuators. Despite its specialized nature, Urbi offers a flexible and powerful environment for developers working in the field of robotics.
connectionsExplanation: Urbi was developed by the Flowers team at INRIA, France. It is inspired by the need for a language that could seamlessly integrate with the real-time requirements of robotics. While it does not have direct predecessors in the traditional sense, it draws from the principles of scripting languages and real-time systems.

moons.name: UrbiScript
moons.description: UrbiScript is the core scripting language used within the Urbi framework, designed for real-time interaction and control of robotic systems.

curious_fact: Urbi was one of the first languages to support real-time programming for robotics, allowing for dynamic interaction with the environment.

name: URQ
description: URQ is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by the BASIC language but with a modern twist, making it accessible for beginners while still being powerful enough for small projects.
connectionsExplanation: URQ was created as an educational tool to introduce programming concepts in a straightforward manner. It draws inspiration from BASIC, which was one of the first high-level programming languages, making URQ a modern descendant of this pioneering language family.
curious_fact: URQ was developed in a weekend hackathon to address the need for a beginner-friendly language in the modern era.

name: UWSC
description: UWSC is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool for beginners to understand basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: UWSC was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical lineage. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but is not derived from any existing language.
curious_fact: UWSC was developed in a single weekend by a group of computer science students.

name: Ubercode
description: Ubercode is a high-level programming language designed for rapid application development. It was developed by a team of software engineers in the early 1990s and aimed to simplify the process of creating complex software applications. Despite its innovative features, Ubercode never gained widespread adoption and is now considered a niche language.
connectionsExplanation: Ubercode was developed in the early 1990s as a response to the growing demand for rapid application development tools. It was influenced by the paradigms of object-oriented programming and aimed to provide a more intuitive and user-friendly environment for software developers. However, its niche focus and lack of marketing efforts limited its adoption compared to more mainstream languages.
curious_fact: Ubercode was one of the first languages to introduce a visual programming environment, allowing developers to create applications using drag-and-drop components.

name: Uiua
description: Uiua is a modern array programming language designed for simplicity and expressiveness. It emphasizes concise syntax and functional programming paradigms, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced programmers.
connectionsExplanation: Uiua was created by Chris Lattner, the creator of LLVM and Swift. It draws inspiration from APL and J, but aims to be more accessible and modern. Uiua's design philosophy focuses on simplicity and ease of use, making it a unique addition to the array programming family.
curious_fact: Uiua's syntax is designed to be intuitive, with many operations resembling natural language.

name: Umple
description: Umple is a modeling language and code generator that supports object-oriented programming. It aims to simplify the process of software development by providing a concise and intuitive syntax for defining classes, associations, and other object-oriented concepts.
connectionsExplanation: Umple was developed to address the complexity and verbosity of traditional object-oriented programming languages. It draws inspiration from UML (Unified Modeling Language) and aims to provide a more streamlined approach to modeling and code generation.
curious_fact: Umple was created by Dr. Tim Bell at the University of Canterbury in New Zealand.
example: class HelloWorld { public static void main(String[] args) { System.out.println("Hello, World!"); } }

name: Unicon
description: Unicon is a high-level programming language developed at the University of Arizona. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, with a focus on simplicity and productivity. Unicon is a descendant of Icon, which was itself inspired by SETL.
connectionsExplanation: Unicon evolved from Icon, a language developed at the University of Arizona in the 1970s. Icon was designed to be a simple and efficient language for text processing and system programming. Unicon extends Icon by adding new features and improving its performance, making it suitable for a wider range of applications.
curious_fact: Unicon is known for its ability to handle large datasets efficiently, making it popular in scientific and engineering applications.

name: Uniface
description: Uniface is a 4GL (Fourth Generation Language) developed by Compuware Corporation. It is designed for rapid application development and deployment, particularly in the business environment. Uniface supports multiple platforms and databases, allowing developers to create applications quickly without extensive programming knowledge.
connectionsExplanation: Uniface was developed as a response to the need for more efficient application development in the business sector. It evolved from earlier 4GL concepts and aimed to provide a more user-friendly and productive environment for developers. Uniface's lineage can be traced back to the broader movement of 4GL languages that sought to abstract away the complexities of traditional programming.

moons.name: Uniface UI
moons.description: Uniface UI is a component of Uniface that focuses on user interface development, providing tools and libraries to create visually appealing and interactive applications.

moons.name: Uniface DB
moons.description: Uniface DB is a component that handles database connectivity and management within Uniface applications, allowing seamless integration with various database systems.

curious_fact: Uniface was one of the first 4GLs to support object-oriented programming features, making it a pioneer in its field.

name: Unified Expression Language
description: Unified Expression Language (UEL) is a Java-based expression language used in JavaServer Pages (JSP) and JavaServer Faces (JSF) to create dynamic web content. It simplifies the process of accessing and manipulating data in web applications.
connectionsExplanation: Unified Expression Language (UEL) was introduced as part of the JavaServer Faces (JSF) specification in Java EE 1.2. It evolved from the need to provide a more intuitive and powerful way to handle expressions in JSP and JSF applications, building upon the existing Expression Language (EL) introduced in JSP 2.0.
curious_fact: UEL was designed to be simple and easy to use, allowing developers to write less code and focus more on the application logic.
example: Hello World in Unified Expression Language: \${'Hello World'}

name: Unified Parallel C
description: Unified Parallel C (UPC) is an extension of the C programming language designed to support parallel computing. It provides a simple and efficient way to write parallel programs by adding constructs for parallel loops and shared memory.
connectionsExplanation: Unified Parallel C was developed as an extension of C to facilitate parallel programming. It builds upon the existing C language, adding constructs specifically for parallel execution, making it a direct descendant of C.
curious_fact: UPC introduced the 'shared' keyword to manage shared memory across multiple processors, simplifying parallel programming.

name: Unilang
description: Unilang is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and universality. It aims to be accessible to beginners and serves as a stepping stone to more complex languages.
connectionsExplanation: Unilang was created as an educational tool to introduce programming concepts in a straightforward manner. It draws inspiration from basic syntax and structure found in popular languages like Python and JavaScript, but with a unique set of features tailored for simplicity and ease of learning.
curious_fact: Unilang was developed by a team of educators to make programming more approachable for students of all ages.

name: Universal Test Specification Language
description: Universal Test Specification Language (UTSL) is a fictional programming language designed for specifying and automating software testing processes. It aims to provide a standardized way to define test cases, making it easier to maintain and execute tests across different platforms and environments.
connectionsExplanation: UTSL was inspired by modern testing frameworks and languages like Python's unittest and Java's JUnit. It seeks to integrate the best practices from these frameworks while offering a more streamlined and universal syntax for test specification.
curious_fact: UTSL was created to address the growing need for a unified testing language that could be easily understood and implemented by developers across various programming backgrounds.

name: Unobtrusive JavaScript
description: Unobtrusive JavaScript is a design philosophy that promotes cleaner, more maintainable web development by separating content, structure, and behavior. It emphasizes the use of JavaScript to enhance user experience without interfering with the HTML structure.
connectionsExplanation: Unobtrusive JavaScript emerged as a response to the need for more maintainable and accessible web applications. It builds upon the principles of progressive enhancement and graceful degradation, ensuring that web pages remain functional even if JavaScript is disabled.
curious_fact: Unobtrusive JavaScript was popularized by the Rails framework, which advocated for separating JavaScript from HTML to improve maintainability.

name: UnrealScript
description: UnrealScript is a high-level object-oriented scripting language developed by Epic Games for use in their Unreal Engine. It was designed to be easy to learn and use, allowing game developers to create complex game logic and behaviors without needing to write extensive code.
connectionsExplanation: UnrealScript was developed as a part of the Unreal Engine, which was first released in 1998. It is derived from C++ and shares many of its syntax and features, making it a natural evolution for game development within the Unreal Engine ecosystem.

moons.name: Blueprint
moons.description: Blueprint is a visual scripting system in Unreal Engine that allows developers to create game logic without writing code, using UnrealScript under the hood.

curious_fact: UnrealScript was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'virtual functions' in game development, which allowed for dynamic method overriding.

name: Ur/Web
description: Ur/Web is a purely functional programming language and web programming framework designed to simplify the development of secure, high-performance web applications. It emphasizes type safety and correctness, aiming to eliminate common web vulnerabilities through its strong type system.
connectionsExplanation: Ur/Web was developed by Adam Chlipala at MIT. It is inspired by functional programming languages like Haskell and ML, but with a focus on web development. The language is designed to compile to both client-side and server-side code, ensuring type safety across the entire web application stack.
curious_fact: Ur/Web was one of the first languages to enforce type safety across both client and server sides of web applications.

name: UrduScript
description: UrduScript is a fictional programming language designed to explore the integration of Urdu script in modern programming paradigms. It aims to provide a unique experience for developers familiar with the Urdu language.
connectionsExplanation: UrduScript is inspired by modern scripting languages like Python and JavaScript, but with a unique twist of using the Urdu script. This makes it a specialized language that could be particularly appealing in regions where Urdu is widely spoken.
curious_fact: UrduScript is one of the first languages to use the Urdu script in its syntax, making it a pioneering effort in language localization.

name: V
description: V is a statically typed compiled language designed for simplicity and speed. It aims to be easy to learn and use, with a syntax similar to Go but with a focus on performance and safety.
connectionsExplanation: V was created by Alexey Medvednikov as a response to the complexity and verbosity of existing languages. It draws inspiration from Go, Rust, and C, aiming to combine the simplicity of Go with the performance and safety features of Rust and the low-level control of C.

moons.name: V UI
moons.description: A simple and efficient UI framework for building cross-platform applications.

moons.name: V Web
moons.description: A web framework for building web applications with V.

curious_fact: V is known for its fast compilation times, often compiling in seconds.

name: VAX Macro
description: VAX Macro is an assembly language for the VAX family of computers developed by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). It is used for writing low-level system software and device drivers.
connectionsExplanation: VAX Macro evolved from earlier assembly languages used in DEC systems, particularly those based on the PDP-11 architecture. It was designed to take advantage of the VAX architecture's advanced features, such as virtual memory and complex instruction sets.
curious_fact: VAX Macro was crucial in the development of the VMS operating system, which was widely used in mainframe environments.

name: VIPLE
description: VIPLE is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax and structure.
connectionsExplanation: VIPLE was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts. While it draws inspiration from popular languages like Python and JavaScript, it has its own unique syntax and paradigms, making it distinct from existing languages.
curious_fact: VIPLE was developed by a team of educators aiming to create a language that is both fun and educational for students.

name: VSXu
description: VSXu is a fictional programming language created for the video game series 'Vexx'. It is designed to be a simple and easy-to-learn language for beginners, with a focus on creating visual effects and animations within the game environment.
connectionsExplanation: VSXu was created specifically for the Vexx game series, making it a derivative of the game's internal scripting system. It is not based on any real-world programming language but was inspired by the need for a user-friendly scripting language within the game's development.
curious_fact: VSXu is one of the few programming languages designed specifically for a video game series, highlighting the unique challenges of game development scripting.

name: VScript
description: VScript is a scripting language developed by Valve Corporation for use in their games, particularly for creating game logic and user interfaces. It is designed to be easy to learn and integrate with Valve's Source engine.
connectionsExplanation: VScript was developed as an internal tool by Valve Corporation to facilitate game development within their Source engine. It evolved from the need to provide a scripting language that could be easily integrated with the engine's architecture and was influenced by other scripting languages commonly used in game development.
curious_fact: VScript is known for its integration with Valve's Source engine, allowing developers to script game logic and user interfaces with ease.

name: Vala
description: Vala is a programming language that aims to bring modern programming language features to GNOME developers without imposing an additional runtime. It is designed to work with the GObject system and is syntactically similar to C#.
connectionsExplanation: Vala was created to simplify the development of GNOME applications by providing a more modern syntax and features while maintaining compatibility with the existing GObject-based ecosystem. It compiles to C and uses the GObject type system, making it a natural fit for GNOME projects.

moons.name: GObject
moons.description: Vala uses the GObject type system, which is a core component of GNOME applications.

curious_fact: Vala was developed by Jürg Billeter and is named after the Vala language spoken in Sardinia.

name: Variable Assembly Language
description: Variable Assembly Language is a fictional esoteric programming language designed for educational purposes and as a thought experiment in programming language design. It introduces variable-length instructions and dynamic instruction sets, challenging traditional assembly language paradigms.
connectionsExplanation: Variable Assembly Language is inspired by the concept of dynamic instruction sets found in some modern processors. It aims to explore the flexibility and complexity that can arise from variable-length instructions, drawing parallels with the evolution of assembly languages to accommodate more sophisticated processor architectures.
curious_fact: Variable Assembly Language was created to challenge programmers to think beyond fixed instruction lengths, reflecting the dynamic nature of modern processor design.
example: LOAD 10; STORE A; ADD 5; PRINT A;

name: VarphiLanguage
description: VarphiLanguage is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes and as a thought experiment in language design. It features a unique syntax and semantics that challenge conventional programming paradigms.
connectionsExplanation: VarphiLanguage was created as an academic project to explore new ways of programming. While it has no direct historical predecessors, it draws inspiration from various paradigms, including functional and procedural programming.
curious_fact: VarphiLanguage uses a novel type system based on the mathematical constant phi (1.6180339887).

name: Verse
description: Verse is an esoteric programming language designed by John Colagioia. It is a stack-based language with a unique syntax that resembles poetry, making it both challenging and intriguing for programmers.
connectionsExplanation: Verse was created as a thought experiment to explore the intersection of programming and poetry. It has no direct ancestors but draws inspiration from the minimalist and artistic nature of esoteric programming languages.
curious_fact: Verse is one of the few programming languages that requires the programmer to think in terms of poetic lines and rhymes.
example:
Hello, world!
Verse is a unique language.
It challenges the mind.
Let's see how it goes.

name: Vienna Definition Language
description: Vienna Definition Language (VDL) is a formal specification language developed in the 1970s at the Technical University of Vienna. It is used for defining the syntax and semantics of programming languages and formal systems. VDL is particularly known for its use in the development of the Vienna Development Method (VDM), which is a formal method for software development.
connectionsExplanation: Vienna Definition Language was developed as part of the Vienna Development Method (VDM) project at the Technical University of Vienna in the 1970s. It is closely related to the VDM-SL (Specification Language) and VDM-RT (Runtime Language) components of VDM. VDL provides a formal way to define the syntax and semantics of programming languages, making it a foundational tool in the VDM methodology.
curious_fact: VDL was one of the first languages to use a formal approach to defining programming languages, influencing later formal methods in software development.
example: DEFINITION MODULE HelloWorld; IMPORT IO; BEGIN IO.Put("Hello World"); END HelloWorld.

name: VilniusBASIC
description: VilniusBASIC is a dialect of the BASIC programming language developed in Lithuania in the early 1980s. It was designed to be used in educational settings and was popular in schools and universities across the country during the Soviet era. The language was tailored to fit the needs of the local educational system and included features specific to the Lithuanian context.
connectionsExplanation: VilniusBASIC evolved from the standard BASIC language, which was widely used in educational settings during the 1970s and 1980s. It was developed to meet the specific requirements of the Lithuanian educational system, incorporating features that were relevant to the local curriculum and teaching methods.
curious_fact: VilniusBASIC was one of the first programming languages to be taught in Lithuanian schools, reflecting the country's efforts to localize educational content during the Soviet era.

name: Vimscript
description: Vimscript is the scripting language used to customize and extend the Vim text editor. It is a Turing-complete language designed to be embedded within Vim, allowing users to automate tasks and create complex editor behaviors.
connectionsExplanation: Vimscript is closely tied to the Vim text editor, which was developed by Bram Moolenaar in 1991. Vimscript is essential for scripting and customizing Vim, making it a direct extension of Vim's functionality.
curious_fact: Vimscript is one of the few languages designed specifically for a single application, making it highly specialized.

name: VintageBASIC
description: VintageBASIC is an early dialect of the BASIC programming language, developed by John G. Kemeny and Thomas E. Kurtz at Dartmouth College. It was one of the first high-level programming languages designed to be easy to use, making it accessible to non-programmers. Despite its age, it remains a significant milestone in the history of computing.
connectionsExplanation: VintageBASIC is a direct descendant of the original BASIC language, which was developed at Dartmouth College in 1964. It was one of the first programming languages to be widely adopted for educational purposes, influencing the development of subsequent BASIC dialects and programming languages.
curious_fact: VintageBASIC was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of structured programming, which influenced the development of modern programming languages.

name: Viola
description: Viola is a domain-specific language (DSL) designed for the development of distributed applications. It was created by the Object Management Group (OMG) to simplify the creation of complex, event-driven systems. Viola is particularly suited for environments where real-time data processing and communication are critical.
connectionsExplanation: Viola was developed as part of the OMG's effort to standardize the development of distributed applications. It builds upon the principles of object-oriented programming and event-driven architecture, aiming to provide a more intuitive and efficient way to handle complex interactions between software components.
curious_fact: Viola was one of the first DSLs to focus on distributed application development, setting a precedent for future domain-specific languages.

name: Viper
description: Viper is a statically typed, compiled programming language designed for performance and safety. It is particularly suited for systems programming and aims to provide a balance between simplicity and efficiency.
connectionsExplanation: Viper was developed by the Viper team at Google as part of their efforts to create a safer and more efficient systems programming language. It draws inspiration from languages like Go and Rust, aiming to combine the simplicity of Go with the safety features of Rust.
curious_fact: Viper's compiler is written in V itself, showcasing its self-hosting capability.

name: VisiCalc
description: VisiCalc was the first spreadsheet software for personal computers, developed by Dan Bricklin and Bob Frankston. It revolutionized business computing by allowing users to perform complex calculations and manage data in a tabular format.
connectionsExplanation: VisiCalc was a pioneering application that popularized the spreadsheet concept on personal computers. It was not a programming language but a software application that influenced the development of later spreadsheet software like Microsoft Excel.
curious_fact: VisiCalc was one of the first applications to popularize the use of personal computers in business environments.

name: Visual Basic
description: Visual Basic (VB) is a high-level, event-driven programming language developed by Microsoft. It was designed to be easy to use for non-programmers and was widely adopted for rapid application development (RAD) in the 1990s and early 2000s.
connectionsExplanation: Visual Basic evolved from BASIC and was influenced by the Windows API. It was part of the Visual Studio suite, which helped in its widespread adoption among developers and non-programmers alike.

moons.name: Visual Basic .NET
moons.description: Visual Basic .NET is an evolution of Visual Basic, providing improved language features and integration with the .NET framework.

curious_fact: Visual Basic was one of the first languages to popularize the concept of drag-and-drop programming.
example:
Sub Main()
    MsgBox("Hello, World!")
End Sub

name: Visual Basic 6
description: Visual Basic 6 (VB6) is a 32-bit third-generation event-driven programming language from Microsoft, used for developing graphical user interface (GUI) applications, especially for Windows. It was widely used in the late 1990s and early 2000s for rapid application development.
connectionsExplanation: Visual Basic 6 evolved from earlier versions of Visual Basic, which were developed by Microsoft in the 1990s. It was a significant step forward in terms of features and performance, incorporating many improvements over its predecessors. VB6 was part of the Visual Studio development environment and was known for its ease of use and rapid development capabilities.

moons.name: VBScript
moons.description: VBScript is a lightweight scripting language based on Visual Basic, designed for use in web pages.

curious_fact: Visual Basic 6 was one of the first languages to popularize drag-and-drop programming, making it accessible to non-programmers.

name: Visual Basic Script
description: Visual Basic Script (VBS) is a scripting language from Microsoft that is designed to be easy to use for non-programmers. It is based on Visual Basic and is primarily used for automating tasks within the Windows environment.
connectionsExplanation: Visual Basic Script evolved from Visual Basic, which was developed by Microsoft in the early 1990s. VBS is a lightweight scripting language that allows users to automate tasks in Windows, making it a popular choice for scripting in the Windows environment.
curious_fact: VBS was originally designed to run within Internet Explorer and was widely used for web-based automation.

name: Visual Basic for Applications
description: Visual Basic for Applications (VBA) is an event-driven programming language from Microsoft that is used for automating tasks within Microsoft Office applications. It allows users to create custom functions, automate repetitive tasks, and develop complex applications.
connectionsExplanation: VBA is a direct descendant of Visual Basic, which itself evolved from the BASIC programming language. It was introduced in 1993 as part of Microsoft Office 5.0 and has since been integrated into various versions of Microsoft Office products. VBA is event-driven, meaning it responds to events such as button clicks or data changes within Office applications.

moons.name: Access VBA
moons.description: VBA for Microsoft Access, allowing for database automation and custom forms.

moons.name: Excel VBA
moons.description: VBA for Microsoft Excel, enabling automation of spreadsheet tasks and custom functions.

moons.name: Word VBA
moons.description: VBA for Microsoft Word, allowing for automation of document creation and manipulation.

curious_fact: VBA is one of the most widely used programming languages for automating Microsoft Office tasks.
example:
Sub HelloWorld()
MsgBox "Hello, World!"
End Sub

name: VisualDialogScript
description: VisualDialogScript is a specialized scripting language designed for creating interactive dialog systems in video games and virtual reality applications. It emphasizes visual scripting to simplify the process of dialog creation and management.
connectionsExplanation: VisualDialogScript was developed to address the need for more intuitive and accessible dialog scripting in modern game development. It draws inspiration from existing scripting languages like Lua and Python, but focuses on a visual interface to enhance usability for non-programmers.

moons.name: DialogFlow
moons.description: A framework for building conversational interfaces using VisualDialogScript.

moons.name: VRChatScript
moons.description: A library for integrating VisualDialogScript into VRChat for creating interactive dialog systems.

curious_fact: VisualDialogScript was created by a team of game developers aiming to reduce the learning curve for dialog scripting.

name: Visual FoxPro
description: Visual FoxPro is a relational database management system and programming environment from Microsoft. It is an extension of FoxPro, offering a graphical user interface and support for object-oriented programming.
connectionsExplanation: Visual FoxPro evolved from FoxPro, which was developed by Microsoft in the 1980s. It introduced a graphical interface and object-oriented capabilities, making it a significant step forward in database management systems for desktop applications.

moons.name: VFPX
moons.description: VFPX is a community-driven project that provides additional tools, samples, and resources for Visual FoxPro developers.

curious_fact: Visual FoxPro was one of the first database systems to support ActiveX Data Objects (ADO), which allowed it to interact with other Microsoft technologies.

name: Visual J++
description: Visual J++ was a development environment for building Java applications on Windows. It was developed by Microsoft as part of its Java strategy in the mid-1990s. Visual J++ provided a visual interface for Java development, similar to Visual C++.
connectionsExplanation: Visual J++ was developed by Microsoft to integrate Java development into its Visual Studio environment. It was a direct response to the growing popularity of Java and Microsoft's desire to maintain relevance in the Java market. Visual J++ was based on the Java Development Kit (JDK) and provided a set of tools for developing Java applications on Windows.
curious_fact: Visual J++ was discontinued in 2003 with the release of Visual Studio .NET, which introduced support for Java development through the Java Language Support add-in.

name: VisualLogic
description: VisualLogic is a visual programming language developed by Visual Logic Systems in 1990. It allows users to create programs by arranging icons and connecting them with lines, making it accessible for beginners and non-programmers. Despite its innovative approach, it has not gained widespread adoption.
connectionsExplanation: VisualLogic was developed as a response to the growing demand for programming tools that were more accessible to non-technical users. It was inspired by earlier visual programming languages like Prograph and was positioned as a more user-friendly alternative. However, its niche market and lack of integration with mainstream development environments limited its adoption.
curious_fact: VisualLogic was one of the first languages to popularize the concept of visual programming, aiming to democratize software development.

name: Visual Novel Maker
description: Visual Novel Maker is a software tool designed for creating visual novels and story-based games. It provides a user-friendly interface and scripting capabilities to help users develop their stories without extensive programming knowledge.
connectionsExplanation: Visual Novel Maker is a specialized tool that evolved from the broader category of visual novel engines and software. It draws inspiration from earlier visual novel creation tools and scripting languages, adapting them to be more accessible for non-programmers.
curious_fact: Visual Novel Maker was developed by Komodo Digital, a company known for creating various tools for game development and storytelling.

name: VisualObjects
description: VisualObjects is a database management system and programming language developed by Attachmate Corporation. It was designed to be used with dBASE databases and provided a visual interface for database management and application development.
connectionsExplanation: VisualObjects was developed as an extension to dBASE, which was a popular database management system in the 1980s. It introduced a visual interface to the dBASE environment, allowing users to create and manage databases and applications more intuitively. The language was closely tied to the dBASE ecosystem and was used primarily in business and data processing applications.
curious_fact: VisualObjects was one of the first systems to offer a visual interface for database management, significantly enhancing user productivity in the 1980s.

name: Visual Prolog
description: Visual Prolog is a declarative programming language developed by PDC (Programming Database Center) in Denmark. It is designed for rapid development of complex applications, particularly in the areas of artificial intelligence, expert systems, and database management.
connectionsExplanation: Visual Prolog evolved from Prolog, a logic programming language developed in the 1970s. It incorporates a visual development environment, making it more accessible for developers who prefer a graphical interface. The language is particularly suited for applications that require logical reasoning and rule-based systems.
curious_fact: Visual Prolog was one of the first languages to integrate a visual development environment with logic programming.

name: VisualT#
description: VisualT# is a hypothetical programming language designed to enhance visual programming paradigms, combining elements of C# with advanced graphical interfaces. It aims to simplify software development for non-programmers by providing a drag-and-drop interface for coding.
connectionsExplanation: VisualT# is inspired by the evolution of C# and the increasing demand for visual programming tools. It draws from the syntax and features of C# while incorporating visual elements to make programming more accessible.
curious_fact: VisualT# was developed to democratize programming by allowing users to create software without extensive coding knowledge.

name: Von Neumann and Goldstine Graphing System
description: A pioneering graphing system developed by John von Neumann and his team at the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton, New Jersey. It was one of the first systems to use digital computers for graphical computations and was influential in the development of computer graphics.
connectionsExplanation: The Von Neumann and Goldstine Graphing System was developed during the early days of computer science, predating modern programming languages. It was a significant step towards the graphical capabilities of modern computers, although it was not a programming language itself but rather a system for graphical computations.
curious_fact: The system was one of the first to use digital computers for graphical computations, paving the way for modern computer graphics.

name: WATFIV
description: WATFIV (Write Another Test File in Five) is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It is a simple language with a straightforward syntax, intended to demonstrate basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: WATFIV was created as an educational tool to help beginners understand programming fundamentals. It is not based on any real-world language but draws inspiration from early programming languages like BASIC and FORTRAN.
curious_fact: WATFIV was invented to simplify the learning curve for new programmers by focusing on essential language features.

name: WEB
description: WEB is a programming language created by Donald Ervin Knuth to weave together computer programs and their documentation. It is a precursor to modern literate programming tools and is used to create documents that contain both code and explanations.
connectionsExplanation: WEB was developed by Donald Knuth in 1976 as a tool to integrate source code with its documentation. It is closely related to his other creation, CWEB, which is a version of WEB for the C programming language. WEB is part of Knuth's broader work on the TeX typesetting system and the Art of Computer Programming series.
curious_fact: WEB was one of the first tools to promote literate programming, a concept where code and its documentation are written together.

name: WICS
description: WICS is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and clarity. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool for teaching basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: WICS was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts without the complexities of existing languages. It draws inspiration from the simplicity of BASIC and the structured approach of Pascal, aiming to provide a clean and intuitive syntax.
curious_fact: WICS was developed in a weekend hackathon by a group of educators to address the need for a simple teaching language.

name: WLanguage
description: WLanguage is a high-level programming language developed by Soft & Win, primarily used for Windows applications. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, making it popular among beginners and small businesses in France.
connectionsExplanation: WLanguage was developed by Soft & Win in France in 1984. It is a high-level language that evolved from earlier versions of the same language, which were used in various Windows-based applications. Its development was closely tied to the Windows operating system, making it a specialized tool for Windows application development.

moons.name: WLanguage Desktop
moons.description: A framework for developing desktop applications using WLanguage.

moons.name: WLanguage Web
moons.description: A framework for developing web applications using WLanguage.

curious_fact: WLanguage was one of the first languages to support Unicode natively, allowing for better internationalization.

name: WMLScript
description: WMLScript is a scripting language designed for use with Wireless Markup Language (WML) to add interactivity to mobile web pages. It is primarily used in the context of mobile web applications and was developed by the WAP Forum.
connectionsExplanation: WMLScript was developed to enhance the functionality of WML, which was a markup language for creating content for mobile devices. It was designed to be lightweight and efficient, making it suitable for the limited resources of early mobile devices.
curious_fact: WMLScript was one of the first scripting languages designed specifically for mobile devices.

name: WQL
description: WQL, or Wonder Query Language, is a fictional, esoteric programming language designed for advanced data querying and manipulation. It is known for its unique syntax and powerful capabilities in handling complex data structures.
connectionsExplanation: WQL was inspired by the need for a more expressive and efficient language for querying large datasets. Its design draws from the principles of SQL but introduces novel constructs to enhance performance and flexibility.
curious_fact: WQL was created by a team of data scientists who aimed to simplify complex data queries while maintaining high performance.
example: SELECT * FROM data WHERE condition; // WQL syntax for querying data

name: WSFN
description: WSFN is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not intended for production use and is primarily used in academic settings to introduce beginners to programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: WSFN was created as an educational tool to simplify the learning process for beginners. It draws inspiration from basic programming paradigms and is not derived from any existing language.
curious_fact: WSFN stands for 'Wonderful Simple Fun Notation', reflecting its goal to make programming accessible and enjoyable.

name: WaveGL
description: WaveGL is a modern, high-performance graphics programming language designed for real-time rendering and interactive graphics. It aims to simplify the process of creating complex visual effects and animations.
connectionsExplanation: WaveGL is inspired by WebGL and modern graphics APIs like Vulkan and DirectX. It leverages the power of GPU acceleration to provide efficient rendering capabilities.

moons.name: ShaderWave
moons.description: A shader language extension for WaveGL that allows for advanced visual effects.

moons.name: WaveMath
moons.description: A mathematical library for WaveGL to handle complex calculations efficiently.

curious_fact: WaveGL was developed by a team of former game developers who wanted to streamline the graphics pipeline.

name: WebLanguage
description: WebLanguage is a modern, high-level programming language designed specifically for web development. It aims to simplify the process of building dynamic and interactive web applications. Despite its recent introduction, it has gained traction among developers looking for a more intuitive and efficient way to code for the web.
connectionsExplanation: WebLanguage was developed as a response to the increasing complexity of web development. It draws inspiration from JavaScript and Python, combining their strengths to create a language that is both powerful and easy to learn. The language is designed to work seamlessly with web technologies, making it a natural choice for developers working on web projects.

moons.name: WebLib
moons.description: A comprehensive library for web development in WebLanguage.

moons.name: WebFrame
moons.description: A web framework for building scalable web applications using WebLanguage.

curious_fact: WebLanguage was created by a team of web developers who wanted to streamline the process of building web applications.

name: WebAssembly
description: WebAssembly (often abbreviated as WASM) is a binary instruction format for a stack-based virtual machine. It is designed as a portable compilation target for programming languages, enabling near-native performance on the web for client and server applications.
connectionsExplanation: WebAssembly was developed by a consortium of browser vendors including Mozilla, Google, Microsoft, and Apple. It was designed to complement JavaScript and run alongside it in web browsers, providing a way to write high-performance code that can be executed at near-native speed.

moons.name: Rust
moons.description: Rust is a systems programming language that compiles to WebAssembly, allowing developers to write safe, concurrent, and fast code for the web.

moons.name: AssemblyScript
moons.description: AssemblyScript is a TypeScript-like language that compiles to WebAssembly, providing a familiar syntax for developers while enabling high-performance web applications.

curious_fact: WebAssembly can be used to run games and other performance-intensive applications directly in the browser without the need for plugins.

name: WebGL
description: WebGL is a JavaScript API for rendering interactive 3D and 2D graphics within any compatible web browser without the use of plug-ins. It is based on OpenGL ES and provides a way to draw hardware-accelerated graphics using the HTML canvas element.
connectionsExplanation: WebGL is a direct extension of the HTML5 canvas element, allowing for the rendering of 3D graphics in web browsers. It is built on top of OpenGL ES, which is a subset of the OpenGL API designed for mobile devices. This makes WebGL accessible and powerful for web-based graphics applications.

moons.name: Three.js
moons.description: A popular JavaScript library that provides a higher-level API for WebGL, making it easier to create 3D graphics.

moons.name: Babylon.js
moons.description: Another JavaScript library that offers a comprehensive set of tools and features for 3D graphics and animations in WebGL.

curious_fact: WebGL was standardized by the Khronos Group, the same organization behind OpenGL.

name: WebGPU Shading Language
description: WebGPU Shading Language (WGSL) is a high-level shading language designed for writing GPU-accelerated graphics and compute shaders. It is part of the WebGPU API, which aims to provide a modern, efficient, and cross-platform graphics and compute API for the web.
connectionsExplanation: WGSL is inspired by and closely resembles the syntax and semantics of GLSL (OpenGL Shading Language), which is widely used in the graphics industry. This similarity allows developers familiar with GLSL to transition to WGSL more easily, ensuring compatibility and familiarity across different platforms.
curious_fact: WGSL is designed to be compiled to SPIR-V, a universal intermediate representation for shaders, ensuring compatibility with various graphics APIs.

name: Whiley
description: Whiley is a programming language designed for writing safe and reliable software. It is statically typed and supports both imperative and functional programming paradigms. Whiley is notable for its verification capabilities, allowing developers to prove the correctness of their programs.
connectionsExplanation: Whiley was developed as a research project at the University of New South Wales in Australia. It draws inspiration from functional programming languages like Haskell and imperative languages like Java. Whiley's unique feature is its ability to verify program correctness, which sets it apart from traditional programming languages.
curious_fact: Whiley is one of the few languages designed with formal verification in mind, making it particularly useful in safety-critical applications.
example: int factorial(int n) ensures $result >= 0 && $result == n! { if (n == 0) return 1; else return n * factorial(n - 1); }

name: Whirlwind Assembler
description: Whirlwind Assembler was a low-level assembly language developed for the Whirlwind computer, one of the first digital computers. It was used primarily for scientific and military applications during the early days of computing.
connectionsExplanation: Whirlwind Assembler was developed for the Whirlwind computer, which was one of the earliest digital computers. It was created to facilitate the programming of complex scientific and military computations, marking an important step in the evolution of assembly languages.
curious_fact: Whirlwind Assembler was one of the first assembly languages, developed alongside the Whirlwind computer in the late 1940s and early 1950s.
example:
LD AC, 0x01
LD BC, 0x02
ADD HL, BC
HALT

name: WindowsAppStudio
description: WindowsAppStudio is a Microsoft tool designed to simplify the creation of Windows 10 apps. It provides a visual interface for building Universal Windows Platform (UWP) apps without requiring extensive coding knowledge.
connectionsExplanation: WindowsAppStudio was introduced by Microsoft as part of its efforts to democratize app development for Windows 10. It leverages the UWP framework, which allows developers to create apps that run across multiple Windows devices. The tool is designed to be accessible to both beginners and experienced developers, making it a significant step in Microsoft's strategy to encourage app development for its ecosystem.

moons.name: XAML
moons.description: Extensible Application Markup Language used for defining UI elements in Windows apps.

moons.name: C#
moons.description: A modern, object-oriented programming language used for developing Windows apps.

curious_fact: WindowsAppStudio was discontinued in 2018, but its legacy continues through the Visual Studio platform.
example: using System;namespace HelloWorldApp {class Program {static void Main(string[] args) {Console.WriteLine("Hello, World!");}}}

name: Wiring
description: Wiring is a programming language and environment designed for microcontrollers, particularly the Arduino platform. It simplifies the process of programming hardware by providing a straightforward syntax and a set of libraries for common tasks.
connectionsExplanation: Wiring was developed by Hernando Barragan and David Cuartielles at the Interaction Design Institute Ivrea (IDII) in Italy. It was created to make programming microcontrollers more accessible to artists and designers. Wiring is closely related to Arduino, and many of its concepts and libraries have been integrated into the Arduino environment.

moons.name: Arduino
moons.description: Arduino is a popular open-source electronics platform based on Wiring. It uses a Wiring-like language and is widely used for building interactive electronic projects.

curious_fact: Wiring was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'soft serial' for communication between microcontrollers and computers.

name: Wolfram Language
description: Wolfram Language is a high-level, general-purpose programming language developed by Wolfram Research. It is primarily used for mathematical and computational tasks, and is the core language of Mathematica.
connectionsExplanation: Wolfram Language was developed by Stephen Wolfram in the late 1980s as a modern successor to earlier symbolic mathematics languages. It is designed to be both powerful and easy to use, with a focus on symbolic computation and data manipulation.

moons.name: Mathematica
moons.description: A popular software system for symbolic and numerical computation.

moons.name: WolframAlpha
moons.description: A computational knowledge engine that uses the Wolfram Language.

curious_fact: Wolfram Language is known for its ability to perform complex mathematical computations and data analysis with concise syntax.

name: WordBASIC
description: WordBASIC is a dialect of the BASIC programming language designed to extend the capabilities of Microsoft Word. It allows users to automate tasks within the Word environment, making it a powerful tool for document processing and customization.
connectionsExplanation: WordBASIC evolved from the BASIC programming language, specifically tailored to integrate with Microsoft Word. It was introduced in 1984 as a way to enhance the automation and customization features of Word, making it a direct descendant of the BASIC family.
curious_fact: WordBASIC was one of the first languages to allow users to automate Microsoft Word, significantly enhancing its functionality.

name: WorkFlowLanguage
description: WorkFlowLanguage is a specialized programming language designed for defining and managing business workflows. It was developed to simplify the creation and execution of complex business processes.
connectionsExplanation: WorkFlowLanguage was developed in the early 2000s as a response to the need for more efficient business process management. It draws inspiration from earlier workflow management systems and programming paradigms, aiming to provide a more intuitive and flexible way to define workflows.
curious_fact: WorkFlowLanguage introduced a unique syntax for defining workflow states and transitions, which significantly reduced the complexity of workflow management.
example: workflow Start { state Begin { action Print("Hello, World!") } state End { action Print("Workflow completed.") } transition Begin -> End { condition true } }

name: Wren
description: Wren is a small, fast, and embeddable scripting language designed for building games and other applications. It has a clean syntax and is known for its simplicity and performance.
connectionsExplanation: Wren was created by Bob Nystrom, who also wrote the book 'Game Programming Patterns'. Its design is influenced by Smalltalk and Ruby, aiming to provide a balance between simplicity and power.

moons.name: Wren-GL
moons.description: A simple 2D graphics library for Wren.

moons.name: Wren-SDL
moons.description: A library for using SDL with Wren, allowing for game development.

curious_fact: Wren is designed to be embeddable, meaning it can be easily integrated into other applications.

name: Wyvern
description: Wyvern is a modern programming language designed to be a general-purpose language with a focus on simplicity and performance. It is statically typed and compiled, aiming to provide a balance between ease of use and high execution speed.
connectionsExplanation: Wyvern was developed as a research project at the University of California, Berkeley, inspired by the need for a language that combines the simplicity of scripting languages with the performance of compiled languages. It draws influences from languages like C++ and Rust, aiming to provide a modern alternative for systems programming.
curious_fact: Wyvern was one of the first languages to introduce a novel type system that combines static and dynamic typing features.

name: X#
description: X# is a modern, object-oriented programming language designed to be a successor to C# and Java. It aims to provide a more intuitive and efficient development experience, particularly for Windows applications.
connectionsExplanation: X# was developed by the same team that created XSharp, a language that is a direct descendant of C#. X# inherits many features and paradigms from C# and Java, making it a natural evolution in the realm of object-oriented programming languages.
curious_fact: X# was created to address some of the limitations found in C# and Java, particularly in terms of performance and ease of use for Windows development.
example: public class HelloWorld { public static void Main() { Console.WriteLine("Hello, World!"); } }

name: X++
description: X++ is a programming language developed by Microsoft for use in the Dynamics AX (now Dynamics 365 Finance and Operations) platform. It is designed to extend and customize the platform's functionality.
connectionsExplanation: X++ is a direct descendant of C# and Java, as it shares many syntactical and structural similarities with these languages. It was developed to provide a more familiar environment for developers who are accustomed to C# and Java, while also offering specific features tailored to the Dynamics AX platform.
curious_fact: X++ is not related to C++ in terms of syntax or features, despite the name.

name: X-BASIC
description: X-BASIC is a fictional dialect of BASIC designed for educational purposes. It introduces advanced features like object-oriented programming and modular design, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced programmers.
connectionsExplanation: X-BASIC was developed as an educational tool to bridge the gap between traditional BASIC and modern programming paradigms. It evolved from the classic BASIC language, incorporating elements of object-oriented programming to enhance its functionality and appeal to a broader audience.

moons.name: X-BASIC GUI
moons.description: A graphical user interface library for X-BASIC, enabling developers to create desktop applications with ease.

moons.name: X-BASIC Network
moons.description: A networking library for X-BASIC, allowing developers to build networked applications and services.

curious_fact: X-BASIC was created to teach programming concepts in a more modern and flexible way, making it a popular choice in educational institutions.

name: X10
description: X10 is a parallel programming language designed to simplify the development of parallel applications for high-performance computing environments. It is built on top of Java and aims to provide a high-level abstraction for parallelism.
connectionsExplanation: X10 was developed at IBM's T.J. Watson Research Center as an extension of Java to support parallel computing. It inherits the object-oriented paradigm from Java and introduces constructs specifically for parallel programming, making it a direct descendant of Java.
curious_fact: X10 introduced the concept of 'place' to represent different computational resources, allowing developers to explicitly manage data locality and parallel execution.
example: class HelloWorld { public static void main(String[] args) { System.out.println("Hello, World!"); } }

name: XACML
description: XACML (eXtensible Access Control Markup Language) is an OASIS standard for defining access control policies. It provides a flexible and extensible way to enforce access control decisions in applications.
connectionsExplanation: XACML was developed to address the need for a standardized way to manage access control policies. It builds upon the concept of policy-based access control, which allows for the separation of access control logic from application logic.
curious_fact: XACML is used in various enterprise environments to enforce fine-grained access control policies.

name: XBLite
description: XBLite is a high-level programming language designed to be a subset of Visual Basic, making it easier for beginners to learn programming. It compiles to native code, offering performance close to that of C++.
connectionsExplanation: XBLite was developed to provide a simplified version of Visual Basic, making it accessible for beginners while still offering the performance benefits of native code compilation. It is not directly derived from any other language but is inspired by the Visual Basic paradigm.
curious_fact: XBLite was created by John Doe, a software developer from the UK, aiming to bridge the gap between beginner-friendly languages and high-performance native code.

name: XBase++
description: XBase++ is an object-oriented database programming language developed by Computer Associates International. It extends the XBase database language with object-oriented features, making it suitable for database applications and business software development.
