connectionsExplanation: XBase++ evolved from the XBase database language, which was a popular choice for database applications in the 1980s. It incorporates object-oriented programming concepts, making it a direct descendant of XBase with added modern features.
curious_fact: XBase++ was one of the first languages to introduce object-oriented features to the XBase family, making it a pioneer in database-oriented programming.

name: XBasic
description: XBasic is a dialect of the BASIC programming language, designed to be a simplified and extended version of QuickBASIC. It was developed to provide enhanced features and better performance for small to medium-sized applications.
connectionsExplanation: XBasic evolved from QuickBASIC, which itself was a direct descendant of the original BASIC language. QuickBASIC was widely used in the 1980s and 1990s for educational purposes and small business applications, making XBasic a natural progression in the BASIC family.
curious_fact: XBasic introduced several new functions and syntax improvements over QuickBASIC, making it more versatile for developers.

name: XC
description: XC is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and clarity. It is often used in introductory computer science courses to teach basic programming concepts without the complexities of more advanced languages.
connectionsExplanation: XC was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct historical predecessor. It is inspired by the simplicity of BASIC and the structured approach of Pascal, aiming to provide a gentle learning curve for beginners.
curious_fact: XC was developed by a team of educators to make programming accessible to students with no prior experience.

name: XCY
description: XCY is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes and as a thought experiment in language design. It features a unique syntax and semantics that challenge conventional programming paradigms.
connectionsExplanation: XCY was created as an academic project to explore the limits of programming language design. While it has no direct historical predecessors, it draws inspiration from various paradigms, including functional and procedural programming.
curious_fact: XCY uses a novel approach to memory management that automatically optimizes data structures based on usage patterns.

name: XCoreXS1
description: XCoreXS1 is a specialized programming language designed for embedded systems, particularly for applications requiring low power consumption and high performance. It is tailored for use with the XCore family of microcontrollers, which are renowned for their ability to handle complex tasks with minimal power usage.
connectionsExplanation: XCoreXS1 is derived from the XCore architecture, which was developed by XMOS. The XCore architecture is based on a unique many-core processor design, allowing for efficient execution of concurrent tasks. This makes XCoreXS1 particularly suitable for applications in the Internet of Things (IoT) and other resource-constrained environments.
curious_fact: XCoreXS1 was one of the first languages to leverage the many-core architecture, enabling unprecedented levels of concurrency in embedded systems.

name: XDL
description: XDL, or eXtensible Data Language, is a fictional programming language designed for advanced data manipulation and processing. It is part of a hypothetical software suite aimed at simplifying complex data operations in various industries.
connectionsExplanation: XDL is inspired by modern data processing languages like SQL and Python, but with a unique syntax and capabilities tailored for specialized data environments. Its development is a fictional scenario, but it draws from real-world paradigms of data-centric programming.
curious_fact: XDL was created to address the limitations of existing data languages in handling complex, multi-dimensional data sets efficiently.
example: SELECT * FROM data WHERE condition; // XDL syntax for data retrieval

name: XGQL
description: XGQL is a specialized query language designed for advanced graph database interactions. It extends traditional GraphQL with additional features tailored for complex graph queries and manipulations.
connectionsExplanation: XGQL was developed as an extension to GraphQL, aiming to provide more robust capabilities for graph databases. It inherits the declarative nature of GraphQL but introduces specialized syntax and functions to handle graph-specific operations more efficiently.
curious_fact: XGQL supports real-time graph updates and subscriptions, making it ideal for applications requiring dynamic graph data.

name: XL
description: XL is a domain-specific programming language designed for financial modeling and analysis. It was developed by XLSoft Inc. and is known for its ease of use in spreadsheet environments, offering advanced mathematical and statistical functions.
connectionsExplanation: XL was developed in the early 1990s as a specialized language for financial modeling, building upon the existing spreadsheet paradigm. It aimed to provide a more powerful and flexible environment for complex financial calculations compared to traditional spreadsheet software.
curious_fact: XL was one of the first languages to introduce advanced financial functions directly into the spreadsheet environment, revolutionizing financial modeling.

name: XLNT
description: XLNT is a modern C++ library designed for high-performance spreadsheet manipulation. It provides a comprehensive set of tools for reading, writing, and modifying Excel files, making it a valuable resource for developers working with spreadsheet data.
connectionsExplanation: XLNT is a C++ library, so its primary connection is to the C++ programming language. It builds upon the foundational concepts of C++ to offer specialized functionality for spreadsheet operations, making it a direct descendant of C++.
curious_fact: XLNT was developed to address the need for a more efficient and modern C++ library for Excel file handling, filling a gap in the market.
example: xlnt::workbook workbook; xlnt::worksheet sheet = workbook.create_sheet(); sheet.cell("A1").value(42); workbook.save("example.xlsx");

name: XML
description: XML (eXtensible Markup Language) is a markup language that defines a set of rules for encoding documents in a format that is both human-readable and machine-readable. It is widely used for data interchange on the web and in various applications where data needs to be structured and easily accessible.
connectionsExplanation: XML was developed by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) to provide a way to encode documents in a format that is both human-readable and machine-readable. It is a direct descendant of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) and was designed to be simpler and more flexible than its predecessor.

moons.name: XSLT
moons.description: XSLT (eXtensible Stylesheet Language Transformations) is a language for transforming XML documents into other formats, such as HTML, text, or other XML documents.

moons.name: XPath
moons.description: XPath (XML Path Language) is a language for selecting nodes from an XML document. It is used in conjunction with XSLT and other XML technologies to navigate and manipulate XML data.

curious_fact: XML was originally designed to be a subset of SGML, but it was later simplified to make it more accessible and easier to use.

name: XMLSapiens
description: XMLSapiens is a fictional programming language designed for advanced data manipulation and machine learning tasks. It is inspired by XML and modern programming paradigms, aiming to provide a flexible and powerful tool for developers.
connectionsExplanation: XMLSapiens was created as an experimental language to explore the integration of XML with modern programming concepts. While it draws inspiration from XML, it introduces new syntax and features to enhance data processing and machine learning capabilities.
curious_fact: XMLSapiens was developed by a team of researchers at a fictional university in the United States.

name: XML User Interface Language
description: XML User Interface Language (XUL) is a markup language used to create the user interface of applications, particularly for Mozilla Firefox and other Mozilla-based applications. It allows developers to define the layout and behavior of user interfaces using XML.
connectionsExplanation: XUL was developed by Mozilla to provide a flexible and platform-independent way to create user interfaces. It is closely related to XBL (XML Binding Language) and XPCOM (Cross Platform Component Object Model), which together form the foundation for building complex user interfaces in Mozilla applications.
curious_fact: XUL was one of the first languages to use XML for defining user interfaces, influencing many subsequent technologies.
example:
<window title="Hello World" width="200" height="100">
  <label value="Hello, World!"/>
</window>

name: XMTC
description: XMTC is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax and minimalistic approach.
connectionsExplanation: XMTC was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts without overwhelming them with complex syntax. It draws inspiration from the simplicity of BASIC and the flexibility of Python, aiming to bridge the gap between these two extremes.
curious_fact: XMTC was developed in a weekend hackathon by a group of educators.

name: XOD
description: XOD is a visual programming language and environment designed for creating interactive digital art, installations, and interactive objects. It allows users to build programs by connecting nodes representing code blocks, making it accessible for artists and designers without deep programming knowledge.
connectionsExplanation: XOD was developed by the Russian company XOD Labs. It is inspired by the concept of visual programming languages like Processing and Pure Data, but with a focus on ease of use and integration with physical computing platforms like Arduino. XOD's visual approach makes it particularly suitable for creative projects that require interactive elements.
curious_fact: XOD uses a custom visual language that compiles to C++ code, allowing for efficient execution on hardware like Arduino.

name: XOTcl
description: XOTcl (eXtended Object Tcl) is an object-oriented extension for the Tcl scripting language. It provides a powerful object-oriented programming system, allowing for the creation of complex applications with a focus on reusability and modularity.
connectionsExplanation: XOTcl was developed as an extension to Tcl to enhance its capabilities with object-oriented features. It builds upon the procedural nature of Tcl, adding classes, inheritance, and other object-oriented paradigms, making it suitable for developing large-scale applications.
curious_fact: XOTcl introduced the concept of 'mixin classes' to Tcl, allowing for more flexible code reuse.

name: XPL
description: XPL is a programming language designed for educational purposes, particularly for teaching programming concepts to beginners. It is a simple language that emphasizes clarity and ease of use, making it suitable for introductory programming courses.
connectionsExplanation: XPL was developed as an educational tool in the early 1980s. It was influenced by Pascal, aiming to provide a more accessible and straightforward syntax for beginners. XPL's design philosophy focuses on simplicity and readability, making it a valuable resource for teaching fundamental programming concepts.
curious_fact: XPL was one of the first languages to introduce structured programming concepts to beginners in a simplified manner.
example: program HelloWorld; begin writeln('Hello, World!'); end.

name: XPL0
description: XPL0 is a simple, structured programming language designed for educational purposes. It was created by John C. Koza in 1977 and is known for its straightforward syntax and ease of learning. XPL0 is often used in introductory programming courses to teach basic concepts.
connectionsExplanation: XPL0 is a direct descendant of the PDP-11 assembly language and is part of the family of structured programming languages. It was designed to be a simple and accessible language for beginners, making it a stepping stone to more complex languages like Pascal and C.
curious_fact: XPL0 was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of structured programming to a wide audience.

name: XPath
description: XPath is a query language for selecting nodes from an XML document. It is widely used in web development and data processing to navigate and search XML structures.
connectionsExplanation: XPath was developed as part of the W3C's XML Path Language (XPath) Working Group. It is closely related to XSLT and is used in conjunction with XML technologies to process and transform XML documents.
curious_fact: XPath expressions can be used to select nodes based on their position, attributes, or text content.

name: XProc
description: XProc is a language for processing XML documents. It is part of the W3C XML Processing Model Working Group and is designed to be a pipeline language for XML.
connectionsExplanation: XProc is a direct descendant of XML and is part of the broader XML processing ecosystem. It builds upon the foundational concepts of XML and extends them to provide a structured way to process XML documents in pipelines.
curious_fact: XProc was designed to be a standardized way to process XML, aiming to replace ad-hoc scripts and tools.

name: XProfan
description: XProfan is a fictional programming language designed for esoteric and experimental purposes. It features a unique syntax and is known for its minimalistic approach to problem-solving.
connectionsExplanation: XProfan was created as a modern take on esoteric programming languages, drawing inspiration from the minimalist philosophy of Brainfuck and the complexity of Lisp. Its design aims to challenge conventional programming paradigms and encourage creative thinking.
curious_fact: XProfan uses only 5 symbols to represent all operations, making it one of the most concise languages ever created.

name: XQuery
description: XQuery is a declarative XML query language designed to query and manipulate structured data, particularly XML documents. It is built on top of XPath and is used in various applications for data extraction and transformation.
connectionsExplanation: XQuery was developed by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) as a standard query language for XML data. It is closely related to XPath and shares many of its syntax and features, making it a natural evolution of XPath for querying XML documents.
curious_fact: XQuery was one of the first languages to support querying XML data, making it a cornerstone in the field of XML processing.

name: XSB
description: XSB is a highly efficient, high-performance Prolog compiler and runtime system. It is designed to support large-scale applications and is particularly well-suited for applications in artificial intelligence, natural language processing, and computational linguistics.
connectionsExplanation: XSB is a descendant of the Edinburgh Prolog system, which was one of the earliest implementations of Prolog. It builds upon the Prolog paradigm, optimizing for performance and scalability.
curious_fact: XSB introduced the concept of tabling, which significantly improves performance by caching intermediate results of computations.

name: XSLT
description: XSLT (eXtensible Stylesheet Language Transformations) is a language for transforming XML documents into other XML documents or other formats like HTML, text, etc. It is part of the XSL family and is widely used for web development and data processing.
connectionsExplanation: XSLT was developed by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) as part of the XSL family. It builds upon the XML and XPath languages, providing a powerful way to manipulate and transform XML data. XSLT is particularly useful for converting XML data into more human-readable formats or for preparing XML data for other applications.
curious_fact: XSLT uses XPath expressions to navigate XML documents, allowing for precise data selection and transformation.
example:
Books

Title Author

name: XUpdate
description: XUpdate is a markup language designed for updating XML documents. It is part of the Xindice project and provides a way to specify changes to XML data in a structured manner.
connectionsExplanation: XUpdate was developed as part of the Xindice project, which aimed to provide a native XML database. It is built on top of XPath and XQuery, allowing for precise updates to XML documents.
curious_fact: XUpdate was one of the early standards for XML document updates, predating many modern XML manipulation tools.

name: XcalableMP
description: XcalableMP is a parallel programming language designed for high-performance computing. It is based on the C programming language and provides constructs for parallel execution and shared memory programming.
connectionsExplanation: XcalableMP was developed by the Tokyo Institute of Technology to address the need for efficient parallel programming in scientific and engineering applications. It extends the C language with parallel constructs, making it suitable for high-performance computing environments.
curious_fact: XcalableMP was one of the first languages to introduce parallel programming constructs directly into the C language, facilitating easier development of parallel applications.

name: Xojo
description: Xojo is a cross-platform rapid application development (RAD) tool that allows developers to create native applications for Windows, macOS, Linux, and the web using a single codebase. It is built on top of the REALbasic framework, which was originally released in 1998.
connectionsExplanation: Xojo evolved from REALbasic, which was one of the early RAD tools that allowed developers to create cross-platform applications with a visual interface. REALbasic was developed by Real Software, which later rebranded the product to Xojo in 2001 to reflect its broader capabilities and modern approach to development.

moons.name: Web Edition
moons.description: Enables the creation of web applications using Xojo.

moons.name: Console Edition
moons.description: Allows the development of command-line applications.

curious_fact: Xojo was originally called REALbasic and was one of the first tools to popularize the concept of drag-and-drop programming.

name: Xtend
description: Xtend is a statically-typed programming language that compiles to Java bytecode. It is designed to be a more concise and readable alternative to Java, while maintaining full compatibility with the Java ecosystem.
connectionsExplanation: Xtend is a direct extension of Java, aiming to simplify Java syntax and enhance developer productivity. It was developed by the Eclipse Foundation and is part of the Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF) project.

moons.name: XtendLib
moons.description: A collection of libraries and extensions for Xtend to facilitate common programming tasks.

curious_fact: Xtend was created to address the verbosity of Java, offering a more streamlined syntax without sacrificing performance.

name: YAWL
description: YAWL (Yet Another Workflow Language) is a workflow management language designed to model and execute business processes. It is particularly suited for complex workflows involving human tasks and decision points.
connectionsExplanation: YAWL was developed as an extension of the WfMC (Workflow Management Coalition) standards, aiming to provide a more flexible and expressive language for workflow modeling. It builds upon the foundational concepts of workflow languages but introduces new constructs to handle complex business scenarios.
curious_fact: YAWL supports the concept of 'split-join' patterns, allowing for complex branching and merging of workflow paths.

name: YSMegaBasic
description: YSMegaBasic is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by classic BASIC languages but includes modern features and a unique syntax.
connectionsExplanation: YSMegaBasic is inspired by classic BASIC languages, which were widely used in the 1980s for educational purposes and personal computing. It aims to retain the simplicity and accessibility of BASIC while incorporating modern programming concepts.
curious_fact: YSMegaBasic was created to introduce beginners to programming with a fun and engaging syntax.

name: YarnSpinner
description: YarnSpinner is a scripting language designed for writing interactive stories and dialogue systems. It is particularly popular in the indie game development community for its simplicity and flexibility.
connectionsExplanation: YarnSpinner was created by Yarn Software, a UK-based company. It is not directly derived from any traditional programming language but is inspired by the need for a language that could handle complex branching dialogue in games without requiring extensive programming knowledge.
curious_fact: YarnSpinner uses a simple, text-based syntax that resembles natural language, making it accessible for writers and non-programmers.
example:
title: Start

== Start ==
Hello, world!
<<if player.name is not null>>
  Nice to meet you, <<player.name>>!
<<else>>
  What is your name?
  <<set player.name to input()>>
  <<jump Start>>
<<endif>>

name: Yedalog
description: Yedalog is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax that aims to teach fundamental programming concepts in an engaging way.
connectionsExplanation: Yedalog was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming. While it draws inspiration from contemporary scripting languages, it does not have a direct lineage from any existing language. Its design focuses on simplicity and clarity to facilitate learning.
curious_fact: Yedalog's syntax is designed to be as close to natural language as possible, making it an interesting tool for language learning.

name: Yellow
description: Yellow is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and creativity. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts in an engaging way.
connectionsExplanation: Yellow was created as an educational tool and does not have a direct lineage from existing programming languages. It is inspired by the simplicity and colorfulness of its name, aiming to make learning programming more enjoyable and accessible.
curious_fact: Yellow was developed by a team of educators who wanted to create a language that could be easily understood by children.

name: Yoix
description: Yoix is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes and as a thought experiment in language design. It features a unique syntax and semantics that challenge conventional programming paradigms.
connectionsExplanation: Yoix was created as an exercise in language innovation, drawing inspiration from various paradigms including functional and procedural programming. Its design emphasizes simplicity and readability, making it an interesting tool for teaching programming concepts.
curious_fact: Yoix was developed in a weekend hackathon by a group of computer science students.

name: Yorick
description: Yorick is a high-level programming language designed for scientific computing and data analysis. It is particularly suited for tasks involving large datasets and complex numerical computations. Despite its powerful capabilities, Yorick has not gained widespread adoption outside of niche scientific communities.
connectionsExplanation: Yorick was developed as a successor to the Interactive Data Language (IDL) and shares many of its features. It was designed to be more flexible and open-source, aiming to provide a more modern and efficient environment for scientific research. Yorick's development was influenced by the need for a language that could handle large-scale data processing and visualization tasks efficiently.

moons.name: Yorick-Plot
moons.description: A plotting library for Yorick, enabling advanced data visualization.

moons.name: Yorick-NetCDF
moons.description: A library for reading and writing NetCDF files in Yorick.

curious_fact: Yorick was named after the character Yorick from Shakespeare's 'Hamlet', symbolizing the transient nature of scientific tools and knowledge.

name: Z++
description: Z++ is a modern, high-performance programming language designed for systems-level programming and real-time applications. It aims to provide a balance between safety and efficiency, making it suitable for both desktop and embedded systems.
connectionsExplanation: Z++ was developed as an evolution of C++, incorporating advanced features inspired by Rust and Go to enhance memory safety and concurrency support. It maintains compatibility with the C++ ecosystem while introducing new paradigms to address modern software challenges.

moons.name: ZLib
moons.description: A library for data compression and decompression.

moons.name: ZNet
moons.description: A networking library for building high-performance network applications.

curious_fact: Z++ introduced the 'zero-cost abstractions' concept, allowing developers to write safe and efficient code without runtime overhead.

name: Z80 Assembly
description: Z80 Assembly is a low-level programming language used to write programs for the Zilog Z80 microprocessor. It is known for its use in early home computers and game consoles like the Sinclair ZX Spectrum and Amstrad CPC.
connectionsExplanation: Z80 Assembly evolved from the earlier 8080 Assembly, which was developed by Intel. The Z80 processor, introduced by Zilog in 1976, was a significant advancement over the 8080, offering more features and better performance, which made Z80 Assembly a popular choice for the burgeoning home computer market.
curious_fact: The Z80 processor was used in the first IBM PC, though it was later replaced by the Intel 8088.
example:
ORG 100H
LD HL, MESSAGE
CALL PRINT
RET
PRINT: LD A, (HL)
OR A
RET Z
CALL putchar
INC HL
JP PRINT
MESSAGE: DB 'Hello, World!', 0
putchar: PUSH AF
LD A, E
LD (21H), A
LD HL, 0EH68H
CALL 0EH66H
POP AF
RET

name: ZIL
description: ZIL, short for Zork Implementation Language, is a domain-specific programming language designed for creating text-based adventure games. It was developed by Infocom and was used to write the popular Zork series of games. ZIL is notable for its simplicity and its role in the golden age of text adventure games.
connectionsExplanation: ZIL was developed by Infocom in the early 1980s as a specialized language for creating text-based adventure games. It was designed to be easy to use and to allow for the creation of complex game logic. ZIL is not directly derived from any other language but shares some similarities with other domain-specific languages used for game development.
curious_fact: ZIL was one of the first languages to use a virtual machine for game execution, which allowed for platform independence.

name: ZOPL
description: ZOPL is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and ease of learning. It is not intended for production use but serves as a tool for beginners to understand basic programming concepts.
connectionsExplanation: ZOPL was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming. While it does not have a direct historical predecessor, it draws inspiration from modern scripting languages like Python and JavaScript, aiming to provide a gentle learning curve.
curious_fact: ZOPL was developed by a team of educators to make programming accessible to children.

name: ZPL
description: ZPL, or Zebra Programming Language, is a high-level programming language used for designing and printing labels on Zebra Technologies' label printers. It is primarily used in industrial and commercial environments where barcode and RFID label printing is required.
connectionsExplanation: ZPL was developed by Zebra Technologies to provide a user-friendly and efficient way to create and print labels. It evolved from earlier printer languages and was designed to be easy to learn and use, making it a popular choice for businesses needing to automate label printing processes.
curious_fact: ZPL is known for its simplicity and efficiency, allowing users to create complex label designs with minimal code.

name: ZScript
description: ZScript is a scripting language developed by ZeniMax Media for use in their game engines, particularly for The Elder Scrolls series. It is designed to be easy to learn and use, allowing for the creation of custom game content and modifications.
connectionsExplanation: ZScript evolved from the need for a flexible scripting language within ZeniMax's game development process. It is inspired by C and C++ syntax, making it accessible to developers familiar with these languages.
curious_fact: ZScript was originally developed for The Elder Scrolls III: Morrowind and has since been used in several other games in the series.

name: Zebra
description: Zebra is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes. It features a unique syntax and is known for its simplicity and ease of learning, making it popular in introductory programming courses.
connectionsExplanation: Zebra was created as an educational tool to introduce beginners to programming concepts. While it is not based on any existing language, it shares some similarities with BASIC in terms of simplicity and ease of use.
curious_fact: Zebra's syntax is inspired by the stripes of a zebra, with alternating keywords and commands.

name: Zedra
description: Zedra is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and readability. It is inspired by modern scripting languages but with a unique syntax that aims to teach fundamental programming concepts in an engaging way.
connectionsExplanation: Zedra was created as an educational tool, drawing inspiration from popular scripting languages like Python and JavaScript. Its design focuses on simplicity and clarity to help beginners understand programming concepts without the complexities of existing languages.
curious_fact: Zedra's syntax is designed to be intuitive, with keywords that resemble natural language commands.

name: ZenScript
description: ZenScript is a scripting language used primarily for modding Minecraft. It is designed to be easy to learn and integrate with the game's modding API, allowing users to create custom behaviors and content.
connectionsExplanation: ZenScript was developed to provide a user-friendly scripting environment for Minecraft modders. It is inspired by JavaScript and Java, making it accessible to those familiar with these languages. The language is tightly integrated with the Minecraft Forge modding platform, allowing for seamless interaction with the game's mechanics.
curious_fact: ZenScript's syntax is designed to be intuitive, with a focus on readability and ease of use for modding purposes.

name: ZenithParsingEngine
description: ZenithParsingEngine is a fictional programming language designed for advanced parsing tasks. It is highly specialized and not widely adopted outside of academic and research environments.
connectionsExplanation: ZenithParsingEngine was developed as a research project at a major university in the United States. It draws inspiration from modern parsing techniques and is intended for use in complex language processing applications.
curious_fact: ZenithParsingEngine was created to solve a specific problem in natural language processing that existing languages could not handle efficiently.

name: Zeno
description: Zeno is a modern, high-level programming language designed for rapid development and ease of use. It emphasizes simplicity and readability, making it accessible to beginners while still offering powerful features for experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: Zeno was inspired by the principles of functional programming and incorporates elements from languages like Haskell and Lisp. Its design philosophy emphasizes immutability and declarative syntax, which sets it apart from more traditional imperative languages.

moons.name: Zeno Framework
moons.description: A comprehensive framework for building web applications with Zeno.

curious_fact: Zeno was named after the ancient Greek philosopher Zeno of Citium, known for his paradoxes that challenge our understanding of motion and infinity.

name: ZetaLisp
description: ZetaLisp is a dialect of the Lisp programming language developed at MIT's Artificial Intelligence Laboratory. It was designed to be a high-level language for artificial intelligence research and was known for its powerful macro system and dynamic typing.
connectionsExplanation: ZetaLisp evolved from Maclisp, which itself was a descendant of Lisp. It was widely used in the AI community during the 1970s and 1980s, influencing the development of later Lisp dialects such as Common Lisp.
curious_fact: ZetaLisp was one of the first Lisp dialects to support multiple return values and was used in the development of the Emacs text editor.

name: ZetaSQL
description: ZetaSQL is a SQL query engine developed by Google. It extends standard SQL with additional features and is used for querying large datasets within Google Cloud Platform.
connectionsExplanation: ZetaSQL is a direct evolution of Google's internal SQL engine, designed to handle the scale and complexity of Google's data infrastructure. It builds upon the principles of SQL with enhancements tailored for distributed systems and large-scale data processing.
curious_fact: ZetaSQL supports user-defined functions and custom aggregate functions, allowing for highly flexible data processing.
example: SELECT name FROM users WHERE age > 30;

name: ZhPy
description: ZhPy is a fictional programming language designed to explore the integration of Chinese characters into programming syntax. It aims to provide a unique approach to coding by leveraging the rich cultural heritage of Chinese characters.
connectionsExplanation: ZhPy is inspired by Python, aiming to introduce Chinese characters into its syntax. This makes it a specialized language that draws from the paradigms of Python but with a unique twist, making it distinct in the programming landscape.
curious_fact: ZhPy was created to explore the possibility of using Chinese characters in programming, making it a unique and culturally significant language.

name: Zig
description: Zig is a general-purpose programming language designed for robustness, optimality, and maintainability. It aims to provide modern language features while maintaining performance close to that of C.
connectionsExplanation: Zig was designed to be a modern systems programming language that addresses the shortcomings of C and C++. It draws inspiration from languages like C, Rust, and Go, aiming to offer a balance between safety and performance.

moons.name: Zig Build
moons.description: A build system and package manager for Zig projects.

moons.name: Zig Test
moons.description: A testing framework integrated into Zig for writing and running tests.

curious_fact: Zig's compiler is written in Zig itself, making it a self-hosting language.
example:
const std = @import("std");

pub fn main() void { std.debug.print("Hello, world!\n", .{}); }

name: Zimbu
description: Zimbu is a statically typed programming language designed for simplicity and efficiency. It was created by Walter Bright, the same person behind the D programming language. Zimbu emphasizes readability and concise syntax, making it suitable for both beginners and experienced developers.
connectionsExplanation: Zimbu was developed by Walter Bright, who is also known for creating the D programming language. While Zimbu is not directly derived from D, it shares some design philosophies and features with D, such as static typing and a focus on performance. Zimbu's simplicity and efficiency make it a unique addition to the programming landscape.
curious_fact: Zimbu is known for its minimalistic syntax, which allows developers to write code with fewer lines compared to other languages.

name: Zonnon
description: Zonnon is a modern, object-oriented programming language developed by ETH Zurich. It is designed to be simple, efficient, and suitable for both teaching and professional use.
connectionsExplanation: Zonnon was developed at ETH Zurich as a successor to Oberon. It inherits the simplicity and efficiency of Oberon while introducing modern object-oriented features, making it a direct descendant in the lineage of educational and professional programming languages.
curious_fact: Zonnon was named after the Swiss town of Zonnon, which is known for its cheese production.

name: Zoomscript
description: Zoomscript is a domain-specific language (DSL) designed for creating and managing interactive presentations and multimedia content. It is particularly suited for developers who need to automate the creation of complex, interactive Zoom meetings and presentations.
connectionsExplanation: Zoomscript was developed by Zoom Video Communications to enhance the automation capabilities of their platform. It is not directly derived from any traditional programming language but is inspired by scripting languages like JavaScript and Python, which are widely used for similar tasks.
curious_fact: Zoomscript was introduced as part of Zoom's strategy to integrate more automation and customization features into their video conferencing platform.

name: Zope Page Templates
description: Zope Page Templates (ZPT) is a templating language used in the Zope web application server. It is designed to separate content from presentation, allowing developers to create dynamic web pages with a clear separation of logic and design.
connectionsExplanation: Zope Page Templates are part of the Zope ecosystem, which was developed in the late 1990s. ZPT evolved from earlier templating systems used in Zope, aiming to provide a more flexible and powerful way to create dynamic web content. It is built on top of the TAL (Template Attribute Language) and METAL (Macro Expansion Template Attribute Language) specifications, which are integral to Zope's templating capabilities.
curious_fact: Zope Page Templates were one of the first templating systems to introduce the concept of macros, allowing for reusable template components.

name: Adenine
description: Adenine is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, focusing on simplicity and ease of learning. It is inspired by the structure of DNA and uses a unique syntax that resembles genetic code.
connectionsExplanation: Adenine, being a fictional language, does not have a direct historical lineage. However, it draws inspiration from the structure of DNA, which is a fundamental concept in molecular biology. This connection is purely thematic and does not reflect a real evolutionary path in programming languages.
curious_fact: Adenine's syntax is designed to resemble DNA sequences, making it a unique educational tool.

name: Algebraic Semantics
description: Algebraic Semantics is a theoretical framework for specifying and reasoning about programming languages. It focuses on the algebraic properties of language constructs, providing a formal basis for language design and implementation.
connectionsExplanation: Algebraic Semantics evolved from the broader field of formal methods in computer science, particularly from the work on algebraic specification and operational semantics. It aims to provide a precise and mathematically rigorous approach to defining programming languages, drawing on concepts from algebra and category theory.
curious_fact: Algebraic Semantics was developed to enhance the formal verification of software systems by providing a clear mathematical foundation for language semantics.
example: let example = "This is an example of Algebraic Semantics in action.";

name: asm.js
description: asm.js is a subset of JavaScript that allows developers to write code that runs at near-native speed. It was designed to enable high-performance web applications, particularly those that require intensive computation, by providing a low-level, statically typed subset of JavaScript.
connectionsExplanation: asm.js was created by Mozilla as a way to optimize JavaScript performance for web applications. It is a subset of JavaScript that can be compiled from languages like C and C++ using tools like Emscripten. This allows developers to leverage existing C/C++ codebases on the web, achieving performance close to that of native applications.
curious_fact: asm.js was deprecated in favor of WebAssembly, which offers similar performance benefits with a more standardized approach.
example: function add(a, b) { return a + b; }

name: Assembler
description: Assembler is a low-level programming language that provides a one-to-one correspondence between its instructions and the machine code of a computer. It is used for writing system-level software, device drivers, and performance-critical applications.
connectionsExplanation: Assembler is a direct descendant of machine code and is considered a foundational language in computer science. It evolved from the need to directly interact with hardware components, which was essential in the early days of computing.
curious_fact: Assembler was one of the first languages to be developed, alongside machine code, and it played a crucial role in the evolution of high-level programming languages.
example:
MOV AX, 0x0001
MOV BX, 0x0002
ADD AX, BX
HLT

name: Assembly Language
description: Assembly language is a low-level programming language that provides a minimal representation of a computer's architecture. It is written in mnemonic codes that correspond to machine language instructions.
connectionsExplanation: Assembly language is the direct descendant of machine code and is used to write programs that interact closely with the hardware. It is essential for system programming, device drivers, and performance-critical applications.
curious_fact: Assembly language is one of the oldest programming languages still in use today.
example:
MOV AX, 0x0001
MOV BX, 0x0002
ADD AX, BX
HLT

name: Autocode
description: Autocode was an early high-level programming language developed in the UK in 1952. It was designed to be easy to learn and use, with a syntax that closely resembled English. Autocode was one of the first languages to be used for commercial programming and was particularly popular in the UK and Commonwealth countries.
connectionsExplanation: Autocode was developed at the University of Manchester and was one of the first high-level programming languages. It was influenced by the earlier machine language and assembly language programming, but aimed to provide a more user-friendly environment for programmers. Autocode was particularly significant as it was one of the first languages to be used for commercial programming, paving the way for the development of more modern programming languages.
curious_fact: Autocode was one of the first languages to use a compiler, which was a significant advancement in programming at the time.

name: bc
description: bc is an arbitrary precision calculator language. It supports basic arithmetic operations, variables, and functions. It is often used for scripting and automation tasks that require precise calculations.
connectionsExplanation: bc was developed by the GNU Project and is part of the GNU Core Utilities. It is inspired by the Desk Calculator (dc) and extends its capabilities by adding features like variables and functions. bc is written in C and is part of the GNU Project's suite of tools.
curious_fact: bc can handle numbers with thousands of digits, making it suitable for high-precision calculations.

name: Brainfuck
description: Brainfuck is an esoteric programming language known for its minimalistic design, consisting of only eight simple commands. Despite its simplicity, Brainfuck is Turing-complete, meaning it can theoretically compute anything computable given enough time and memory.
connectionsExplanation: Brainfuck was created by Urban Müller in 1993 as a thought experiment to challenge and amuse programmers. It has no practical use but has inspired many other esoteric programming languages and puzzles.
curious_fact: Brainfuck uses only eight commands: >, <, +, -, ., ,, [, ]

name: Chef
description: Chef is an esoteric programming language where programs are written as recipes. It was created by David Morgan-Mar in 2007. The language is designed to be humorous and unconventional, with each line of code representing an instruction in a recipe.
connectionsExplanation: Chef is an esoteric programming language that emerged from the community's interest in creating languages with unique and unconventional syntax. It does not have a direct lineage from traditional programming languages but is inspired by the idea of using everyday language to describe computational tasks.
curious_fact: Chef programs are written as recipes, where each line of code corresponds to a cooking instruction.
example:
To Hello World:
Take 1 cup of "Hello World".
Mix it well.
Pour it into the bowl.

name: Chomski
description: Chomski is a theoretical programming language inspired by Noam Chomsky's work in linguistics. It is designed to explore formal grammatical structures and is not intended for practical use.
connectionsExplanation: Chomski was created to study formal grammatical structures, drawing inspiration from Noam Chomsky's theories on language. It is not a practical programming language but a theoretical construct used in computer science and linguistics.
curious_fact: Chomski is named after Noam Chomsky, a renowned linguist who developed the Chomsky hierarchy of grammars.

name: Coarray Fortran
description: Coarray Fortran is an extension to the Fortran programming language that supports parallel programming. It allows for the creation of arrays that can be distributed across multiple processors, facilitating concurrent execution and data sharing.
connectionsExplanation: Coarray Fortran was developed as an extension to Fortran, which itself is a descendant of the ALGOL family. It builds upon the existing Fortran language to introduce parallel programming capabilities, making it a direct descendant of Fortran.
curious_fact: Coarray Fortran was introduced in Fortran 2008 to address the need for parallel programming without the complexity of MPI.
example:
program hello_world
  implicit none
  integer :: image_id
  image_id = this_image()
  print *, 'Hello from image ', image_id
end program hello_world

name: colorForth
description: colorForth is a minimalist, stack-based programming language designed by Charles Moore. It is known for its unique syntax and its use of color to differentiate code elements, aiming to simplify programming and improve readability.
connectionsExplanation: colorForth evolved from Charles Moore's earlier Forth language, which itself was inspired by the stack-based languages of the 1960s. colorForth introduces a novel approach by using color to categorize code elements, which was a significant departure from traditional Forth syntax.
curious_fact: colorForth was designed to run on minimal hardware, often with only a few kilobytes of RAM.

name: dzaima/APL
description: dzaima/APL is a modern implementation of the APL programming language, designed to be more accessible and easier to use than traditional APL interpreters. It runs in web browsers and is written in JavaScript, making it a unique tool for those interested in learning or using APL without needing to install additional software.
connectionsExplanation: dzaima/APL is a modern implementation of APL, which was originally developed in the 1960s. While APL itself has a rich history and was influential in the development of functional programming, dzaima/APL brings the language into the modern era by making it accessible via web browsers. This implementation is inspired by the original APL but incorporates modern web technologies to enhance usability and accessibility.
curious_fact: dzaima/APL is one of the few APL interpreters that can run directly in a web browser, making it accessible to a global audience without the need for installation.

name: eC
description: eC is a programming language designed to be a modern, efficient alternative to C, offering improved safety and performance features while maintaining compatibility with existing C code. It was created to address some of the limitations and pitfalls of the C language, particularly in terms of memory management and type safety.
connectionsExplanation: eC evolved from C, aiming to enhance its features without breaking compatibility. It introduces modern concepts like automatic memory management and type safety, making it a more robust alternative for system programming and application development.
curious_fact: eC was one of the first languages to introduce automatic memory management features in a C-like syntax, significantly reducing the risk of memory leaks and buffer overflows.

name: Extended Backus–Naur Form
description: Extended Backus–Naur Form (EBNF) is a metalanguage for defining the syntax of programming languages and other formal languages. It is an extension of the original Backus–Naur Form (BNF) and provides additional features for clarity and conciseness.
connectionsExplanation: EBNF was developed as an extension of BNF to improve the readability and expressiveness of language definitions. It was first introduced in the 1960s and has since been used in various language specifications, including those for HTML and XML.
curious_fact: EBNF is often used in compiler design to define the grammar of programming languages.

name: FASM
description: FASM (Flat Assembler) is a fast and efficient assembler for x86 architecture. It is known for its speed and ease of use, making it popular among developers who require high-performance assembly code.
connectionsExplanation: FASM is a direct descendant of the MASM (Microsoft Macro Assembler) and is inspired by the simplicity and speed of the NASM (Netwide Assembler). It was developed to provide a more efficient and user-friendly alternative to existing assemblers.
curious_fact: FASM is written entirely in assembly language, demonstrating its own capabilities.
example:
format PE console
entry start

start:
    mov eax, 0x40000003
    mov ebx, msg
    mov ecx, msg.len
    int 0x80
    mov eax, 1
    xor ebx, ebx
    int 0x80

msg db 'Hello, World!', 0xA
.len = $ - msg

name: Flowchart Language
description: Flowchart Language is a programming language designed to represent algorithms visually using flowcharts. It was one of the early attempts to bridge the gap between human-readable diagrams and machine-executable code.
connectionsExplanation: Flowchart Language was developed in the 1970s as a way to visually represent algorithms, making it easier for non-programmers to understand and implement. It was inspired by the need for more intuitive programming methods, especially in educational settings.
curious_fact: Flowchart Language was one of the first to use visual symbols to represent programming concepts, influencing later graphical programming languages.

name: Frontier-Guarded Datalog
description: Frontier-Guarded Datalog is an advanced extension of Datalog designed for efficient query processing in large-scale databases. It introduces frontier-guarded rules to optimize query execution and improve performance.
connectionsExplanation: Frontier-Guarded Datalog extends traditional Datalog by incorporating frontier-guarded rules, which help in managing large datasets more efficiently. This makes it particularly useful in database systems where performance optimization is critical.
curious_fact: Frontier-Guarded Datalog was developed to address the scalability issues faced by traditional Datalog in handling large databases.

name: Guarded Datalog
description: Guarded Datalog is an extension of Datalog that introduces guarded negation, allowing for more expressive and flexible query capabilities. It is particularly useful in knowledge representation and reasoning systems.
connectionsExplanation: Guarded Datalog extends traditional Datalog by incorporating guarded negation, which was introduced to enhance the expressive power of the language. This extension allows for more complex queries and is particularly useful in areas like knowledge representation and reasoning.
curious_fact: Guarded Datalog was developed to address limitations in traditional Datalog by allowing for more nuanced query expressions.

name: HOC
description: HOC is a simple, interpreted programming language created by John McCarthy and Charles Hewitt at MIT in 1963. It was designed to be a teaching language for introductory computer science courses and to demonstrate the principles of programming languages. HOC is known for its minimalistic syntax and its influence on later languages like Lisp.
connectionsExplanation: HOC was developed as a teaching tool at MIT and was influenced by the earlier work on Lisp. It shares some conceptual similarities with Lisp, particularly in its approach to symbolic computation and its use of lists as a fundamental data structure. However, HOC is simpler and more focused on educational purposes.
curious_fact: HOC stands for Higher Order Calculator, reflecting its use in teaching advanced programming concepts through simple arithmetic operations.

name: Join-Pattern
description: Join-Pattern is a modern, experimental programming language designed for concurrent and parallel programming. It emphasizes pattern matching and declarative concurrency, making it suitable for complex systems where multiple processes need to interact efficiently.
connectionsExplanation: Join-Pattern draws inspiration from functional programming paradigms, particularly from languages like Haskell and Erlang, which emphasize immutability and concurrency. Its design aims to simplify the handling of concurrent processes by using pattern matching to define how different processes should interact.
curious_fact: Join-Pattern was developed by a team of researchers at MIT to address the challenges of writing correct and efficient concurrent programs.

name: jq
description: jq is a lightweight and flexible command-line JSON processor. It is designed to be used for filtering, mapping, and transforming JSON data with the same ease as using sed, awk, grep for text data.
connectionsExplanation: jq was created by Stephen Dolan and first released in 2009. It is inspired by the functional programming paradigm and is often used in Unix-like environments for processing JSON data. jq's design is influenced by tools like sed and awk, which are used for text processing.
curious_fact: jq is written in C and is known for its speed and efficiency in processing JSON data.

name: Ladder Logic
description: Ladder Logic is a programming language used primarily for industrial control systems, especially in PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers). It is visually structured, resembling a ladder diagram, making it intuitive for electrical engineers and technicians.
connectionsExplanation: Ladder Logic was developed in the 1960s to provide a visual and logical representation of control circuits, making it easier for engineers to design and implement industrial control systems. It evolved from earlier relay-based control systems and has since become a standard in the field of industrial automation.
curious_fact: Ladder Logic was one of the first programming languages designed specifically for industrial control, predating structured programming languages.
example:
LD 001 002 // Ladder rung with inputs 001 and 002
LD 003 // Ladder rung with input 003

name: LinearDatalog
description: LinearDatalog is an extension of Datalog designed to handle linear temporal logic, allowing for the representation and querying of temporal data in a declarative manner. It is particularly useful in applications requiring temporal reasoning and event processing.
connectionsExplanation: LinearDatalog extends the capabilities of traditional Datalog by incorporating linear temporal logic, which allows for the expression of temporal relationships and constraints. This makes it a specialized tool for applications that require temporal reasoning, such as event processing and temporal databases.
curious_fact: LinearDatalog was developed to address the limitations of traditional Datalog in handling temporal data, making it a pioneering tool in the field of temporal logic programming.
example: temporal_fact(X, Y) :- event(X, Y, T), T > 0.

name: m4
description: m4 is a macro processor developed by Brian W. Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie. It is primarily used to expand macros in files, which is particularly useful in the context of the Unix operating system and its build tools.
connectionsExplanation: m4 is a direct descendant of the 'analytical-engine-order-code' as it was developed in the 1970s, a period when the influence of early programming languages was still strong. It is foundational to the Unix build system and has influenced many other tools and languages.
curious_fact: m4 is known for its ability to handle complex macro expansions, which was crucial in the development of Unix utilities.

name: mIRC Scripting Language
description: mIRC Scripting Language is a scripting language used for automating tasks within the mIRC client, a popular Internet Relay Chat (IRC) client. It allows users to create scripts for various purposes, such as automating messages, managing channels, and enhancing the client's functionality.
connectionsExplanation: mIRC Scripting Language is closely tied to the mIRC client, which was developed in the mid-1990s. It evolved as a necessity to extend the client's capabilities, allowing users to automate repetitive tasks and customize their IRC experience. The language is not a standalone language but is integral to the mIRC environment, making it a specialized scripting language.
curious_fact: mIRC Scripting Language was one of the first scripting languages designed specifically for IRC clients, enabling users to automate and customize their chat experiences.
example: on 1:JOIN:#channel:{ msg $nick Welcome to the channel! }

name: Make
description: Make is a build automation tool that automatically builds executable programs and libraries from source code by reading files called Makefiles which specify how to derive the target program.
connectionsExplanation: Make was developed by Stuart Feldman at Bell Labs in 1977. It was inspired by the earlier 'dmake' tool and has since become a standard utility in Unix-like operating systems. Make is widely used in software development to manage dependencies and automate the build process.
curious_fact: Make was originally written in assembly language but was later rewritten in C for portability.

name: Minus
description: Minus is a fictional programming language designed for educational purposes, emphasizing simplicity and minimalism. It is not widely used outside of academic settings.
connectionsExplanation: Minus was created as a teaching tool to introduce basic programming concepts without the complexities of more advanced languages. It is not derived from any existing language but inspired by the simplicity of early programming languages like BASIC.
curious_fact: Minus uses only one operator: subtraction, hence its name.

name: nesC
description: nesC is a C-based programming language designed for writing networked embedded software, particularly for wireless sensor networks. It extends C with constructs for concurrency, event-driven programming, and message passing, making it well-suited for resource-constrained devices.
connectionsExplanation: nesC was developed at the University of California, Berkeley, as part of the TinyOS project. It is an extension of C, specifically tailored to the needs of embedded systems, particularly those in wireless sensor networks. The language's design incorporates features from C++ and Java, such as event-driven programming and message passing, to facilitate the development of complex networked systems.

moons.name: TinyOS
moons.description: TinyOS is an open-source operating system for wireless sensor networks that uses nesC as its programming language.

curious_fact: nesC was named after the Nintendo Entertainment System (NES) due to its focus on embedded systems and resource constraints.

name: newLISP
description: newLISP is a high-level, general-purpose Lisp dialect designed for scripting and rapid prototyping. It is known for its simplicity and ease of use, making it popular among developers who need a lightweight, fast scripting language.
connectionsExplanation: newLISP evolved from the original LISP language, which was developed in the late 1950s. It inherits the functional programming paradigm and Lisp's symbolic computation capabilities, while also incorporating modern features for ease of use and performance.

moons.name: newLISP-GUI
moons.description: A library for building graphical user interfaces in newLISP.

moons.name: newLISP-XML
moons.description: A library for XML processing in newLISP.

curious_fact: newLISP was designed to be embedded in applications, allowing for rapid scripting and prototyping without the overhead of a full-fledged Lisp environment.

name: NJS
description: NJS is a modern, high-performance JavaScript engine designed for building scalable web applications. It introduces several optimizations and new features to enhance developer productivity and application performance.
connectionsExplanation: NJS is a derivative of Node.js, inheriting its event-driven, non-blocking I/O model. It aims to improve upon Node.js by offering better performance and additional language features, making it suitable for large-scale applications.
curious_fact: NJS was developed by a team of former Node.js contributors who sought to address performance bottlenecks in large-scale applications.

name: o:XML
description: o:XML is an extended version of XML designed to enhance its capabilities for complex data structures and interoperability. It introduces advanced features like dynamic typing and object-oriented constructs, making it suitable for modern application development.
connectionsExplanation: o:XML evolved from standard XML in the early 2000s as developers sought to address limitations in XML for handling complex data. It builds upon the foundational principles of XML, such as hierarchical data representation and extensibility, while incorporating object-oriented paradigms to improve data manipulation and interaction.

moons.name: o:XML Schema
moons.description: A schema language for o:XML that defines the structure and data types of XML documents.

moons.name: o:XML Query
moons.description: A query language for o:XML that allows for complex data retrieval and manipulation.

curious_fact: o:XML was developed by a consortium of tech giants to standardize data exchange in the early 2000s.

name: Occam
description: Occam is a concurrent programming language developed by Professor Tony Hoare at the University of Oxford. It is known for its simplicity and clarity, particularly in expressing concurrent processes. Occam is part of the Transputer architecture, which was designed to support concurrent programming.
connectionsExplanation: Occam was developed as part of the Transputer project, which aimed to create a processor architecture that could efficiently support concurrent programming. It was influenced by the need to simplify the expression of concurrent processes, drawing from earlier work in concurrent programming languages.
curious_fact: Occam was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'processes' as a fundamental unit of computation.
example:
PROCESS main IS
  PAR
    DO
      OUTBOX "Hello, World!"
    OD
OD

name: occam-π
description: occam-π is a parallel programming language developed by Inmos in the 1980s. It extends the occam language with explicit support for concurrency and communication, making it suitable for programming parallel systems.
connectionsExplanation: occam-π evolved from the occam language, which was designed for programming the Transputer architecture. The addition of explicit concurrency features in occam-π was a significant step towards modern parallel programming paradigms.
curious_fact: occam-π was one of the first languages to introduce explicit concurrency constructs, influencing later parallel programming languages.
example:
PROCESS main IS
  SEQ
    PAR
      DO
        OUTCHANNEL ch1
        "Hello, World!"
      OD
      DO
        INCHANNEL ch2
        STRING message
        message := ""
        IN message FROM ch2
        OUT message
      OD
  OD
END

name: Pagedescriptionmarkuplanguage
description: Pagedescriptionmarkuplanguage (PML) is a markup language designed to describe the structure and layout of web pages. It was one of the early attempts to standardize web page formatting, preceding HTML. PML aimed to provide a more structured and semantic approach to web content.
connectionsExplanation: PML was developed in the early 1990s as part of the efforts to standardize web page formatting. It was influenced by the need for a more structured and semantic approach to web content, which later evolved into HTML. PML's development was a precursor to the more widely adopted HTML, which became the standard for web page markup.
curious_fact: PML was one of the early languages used to describe web page structure, predating HTML.
example: <page><title>Welcome to My Page</title><content>This is a sample page description.</content></page>

name: Rank
description: Rank is a modern programming language designed for high-performance computing and data analysis. It emphasizes simplicity and efficiency, making it suitable for scientific and engineering applications.
connectionsExplanation: Rank is inspired by the paradigms of functional programming and array-oriented computing. Its design draws from languages like Haskell and MATLAB, aiming to provide a concise and powerful syntax for numerical computations.
curious_fact: Rank was developed by a team of researchers at Stanford University to address the growing need for efficient data processing in scientific research.

name: rc
description: rc is a command language interpreter designed to be small, fast, and simple. It is often used in Unix-like operating systems for scripting and automation tasks.
connectionsExplanation: rc was developed by Tom Duff at Bell Labs in 1981. It is known for its minimalistic design and is often used in conjunction with other Unix tools. rc is not directly derived from any other language but shares some similarities with the Bourne shell (sh) in terms of functionality.
curious_fact: rc is known for its small size and fast execution, often being used in environments where resource usage is critical.

name: StructuredText
description: StructuredText is a programming language designed for use in industrial automation, particularly for programmable logic controllers (PLCs). It is a high-level language that allows for the creation of complex control systems in a structured manner.
connectionsExplanation: StructuredText was developed by Asea Brown Boveri (ABB) in the early 1980s as part of the IEC 61131-3 standard for PLC programming languages. It is closely related to other PLC languages like Ladder Diagram and Function Block Diagram, all of which are part of the same standard.
curious_fact: StructuredText is one of the five standard PLC languages defined by IEC 61131-3, alongside Ladder Diagram, Function Block Diagram, Sequential Function Chart, and Instruction List.

name: thinBasic
description: thinBasic is a 32-bit BASIC compiler designed for Windows. It is known for its ease of use and compatibility with QuickBasic and Visual Basic. The language is aimed at hobbyists and small businesses looking for a straightforward way to develop Windows applications.
connectionsExplanation: thinBasic evolved from the QuickBasic and Visual Basic paradigms, offering a more modern and efficient approach to BASIC programming. It maintains compatibility with older BASIC syntax while adding features suitable for contemporary Windows development.
curious_fact: thinBasic was developed by a single individual, Marco Pivetta, who aimed to create a BASIC compiler that was both powerful and accessible.

name: UrbiScript
description: UrbiScript is a high-level programming language designed for robotics and interactive systems. It is part of the Urbi SDK, which provides a framework for building intelligent robots and interactive applications. UrbiScript is known for its event-driven architecture and its ability to handle real-time data processing.
connectionsExplanation: UrbiScript was developed by the French company Fluendo, which aimed to create a language that could seamlessly integrate with the company's Urbi SDK. The language is designed to be used in conjunction with the SDK's hardware and software components, making it a specialized tool for robotics and interactive systems.
curious_fact: UrbiScript was one of the first languages to introduce the concept of 'soft real-time' programming, allowing for more flexible and responsive robotics applications.

name: UxnTal
description: UxnTal is an assembly-like language designed for the Uxn computer, a minimalist computer architecture. It is known for its simplicity and efficiency, making it suitable for low-level programming tasks.
connectionsExplanation: UxnTal is directly inspired by the Uxn computer architecture, which was created to be a minimalistic and efficient computing platform. It shares the same design philosophy and is used for writing low-level programs for the Uxn system.
curious_fact: UxnTal is one of the few assembly languages designed for a custom computer architecture, showcasing the versatility of assembly programming.
example:
LDI $0A ; Load immediate value 10 into accumulator
OUT ; Output the value in the accumulator

name: vvvv
description: vvvv is a visual programming language designed for creating interactive installations and generative art. It uses a node-based interface where users connect visual elements to create complex behaviors without writing traditional code.
connectionsExplanation: vvvv was created by artists and designers who wanted a more intuitive way to create interactive installations. It draws inspiration from the visual programming paradigm, which emphasizes graphical interfaces over text-based code.
curious_fact: vvvv uses a unique patch-based system where nodes are connected to form data flow graphs.

name: webMethodsFlow
description: webMethodsFlow is a business process management (BPM) tool developed by webMethods. It provides a visual environment for designing, deploying, and managing business processes. The tool is designed to integrate with webMethods' other products and services, offering a comprehensive solution for enterprise-level process automation.
connectionsExplanation: webMethodsFlow is part of the webMethods Integration Platform, which was developed to streamline and automate business processes. It evolved from earlier webMethods tools and integrates seamlessly with webMethods' middleware and services, making it a direct descendant of the webMethods product line.
curious_fact: webMethodsFlow introduced a unique visual drag-and-drop interface for process design, revolutionizing BPM in the late 1990s.
example:
start
  // Define a simple process flow
  task1
  task2
end

name: wenyan-lang
description: Wenyan-lang is a programming language inspired by classical Chinese. It aims to make programming more accessible by using natural language constructs from ancient Chinese texts. Despite its unique approach, it has a niche audience.
connectionsExplanation: Wenyan-lang was created to explore the intersection of programming and classical Chinese literature. It draws inspiration from the elegance and structure of ancient Chinese texts, aiming to provide a new perspective on programming syntax.
curious_fact: Wenyan-lang translates modern programming concepts into classical Chinese, making it a unique blend of ancient wisdom and modern technology.
example: 吾有一數。名之曰甲。設之為二。

name: Wollok
description: Wollok is an educational programming language designed to teach object-oriented programming concepts in a simple and intuitive way. It is particularly popular in Argentina for its use in schools and universities.
connectionsExplanation: Wollok was developed in Argentina with the goal of making object-oriented programming accessible to beginners. It is inspired by Smalltalk and Ruby, focusing on simplicity and ease of use for educational purposes.
curious_fact: Wollok uses a unique syntax that resembles natural language, making it easier for students to understand programming concepts.
example: object HelloWorld { method sayHello() { println('Hello, World!') } }

name: wxBasic
description: wxBasic is a BASIC dialect designed to be used with the wxWidgets cross-platform GUI toolkit. It allows developers to create graphical user interfaces for applications on various operating systems.
connectionsExplanation: wxBasic is a derivative of Visual Basic, specifically tailored to work with the wxWidgets library, which provides a comprehensive set of C++ classes for GUI development. This connection allows wxBasic to leverage the cross-platform capabilities of wxWidgets, making it suitable for developing applications on multiple operating systems.
curious_fact: wxBasic was developed to simplify the creation of cross-platform applications using BASIC, a language known for its ease of use.

name: x86 Assembly Language
description: x86 Assembly Language is a low-level programming language that provides a one-to-one correspondence between its instructions and the machine code executed by the x86 family of microprocessors. It is widely used in system programming, device drivers, and performance-critical applications.
connectionsExplanation: x86 Assembly Language evolved from the earlier 8086 assembly language, which was developed by Intel in the early 1970s. It is a direct descendant of the x86 architecture, which has been the standard for personal computers and servers since the 1980s.
curious_fact: x86 Assembly Language is one of the oldest and most widely used assembly languages, with a rich history dating back to the 1970s.
example:
section .data
    hello db 'Hello, World!', 0
section .text
global _start
_start:
    mov eax, 4          ; sys_write
    mov ebx, 1          ; file descriptor 1 is stdout
    mov ecx, hello      ; message to write
    mov edx, 13         ; message length
    int 0x80            ; call kernel
    mov eax, 1          ; sys_exit
    xor ebx, ebx        ; exit code 0
    int 0x80            ; call kernel

name: xHarbour
description: xHarbour is an open-source, cross-platform compiler for the xBase programming language, primarily used for database applications. It is a continuation of the Harbour project, which itself is a fork of Clipper.
connectionsExplanation: xHarbour evolved from the Harbour project, which was a fork of the Clipper language. Clipper, in turn, was a popular database-oriented language in the 1980s and early 1990s. xHarbour maintains compatibility with Clipper and adds modern features, making it suitable for contemporary database applications.
curious_fact: xHarbour supports Unicode, allowing it to handle a wide range of character sets beyond ASCII.

name: C
description: C is a general-purpose, procedural computer programming language supporting structured programming, lexical variable scope, and recursion. It was originally developed by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs in the early 1970s. C is known for its efficiency and portability, and it has influenced many other programming languages.
connectionsExplanation: C was developed at Bell Labs in the early 1970s and is considered one of the most influential programming languages of all time. It has influenced many other languages, including C++, Java, and Python. C's design philosophy emphasizes simplicity and efficiency, making it a popular choice for system software and application software.

moons.name: C++
moons.description: C++ is an extension of the C programming language, adding support for object-oriented programming. It was developed by Bjarne Stroustrup as an enhancement to the C language.

moons.name: Java
moons.description: Java is a high-level, class-based, object-oriented programming language that is designed to have as few implementation dependencies as possible. It was developed by James Gosling at Sun Microsystems in the mid-1990s.

curious_fact: C was originally developed for the UNIX operating system and is still widely used in system programming.

name: λProlog
description: λProlog is an extension of Prolog that integrates higher-order logic and lambda calculus. It allows for the representation of functions as first-class citizens, enabling more expressive and flexible programming.
connectionsExplanation: λProlog was developed by extending Prolog with higher-order logic and lambda calculus, making it a significant step towards more expressive logic programming languages. It builds on the foundational work of Prolog, which was itself inspired by the logical programming paradigm.
curious_fact: λProlog was one of the first systems to integrate lambda calculus into Prolog, allowing for more complex logical expressions.
example: :- op(900, fx, lambda).\nlambda X. P :- P.

name: ล็อกทอล์ก
description: ล็อกทอล์ก (Locktalk) เป็นภาษาโปรแกรมมิ่งที่ออกแบบมาเพื่อใช้ในระบบปฏิบัติการ LOCK ซึ่งเป็นระบบปฏิบัติการที่พัฒนาขึ้นในประเทศไทยในช่วงปลายศตวรรษที่ 20. ภาษานี้มีการออกแบบให้เข้ากับวัฒนธรรมและภาษาไทย โดยเฉพาะในด้านการสื่อสารและการแสดงข้อมูล.
connectionsExplanation: ล็อกทอล์กถูกสร้างขึ้นเพื่อรองรับระบบปฏิบัติการ LOCK ซึ่งเป็นระบบที่พัฒนาขึ้นในประเทศไทย ดังนั้น ไม่มีการสืบทอดจากภาษาโปรแกรมมิ่งที่มีอยู่ก่อนหน้านี้.
curious_fact: ล็อกทอล์กเป็นภาษาโปรแกรมมิ่งที่ออกแบบมาเฉพาะสำหรับระบบ LOCK ซึ่งเป็นระบบปฏิบัติการที่พัฒนาขึ้นในประเทศไทย.
example: PRINT "สวัสดี โลก"
